lines.st: *cough* *cough* I pray!
I pray that the sick people in Faber Central Hospital get better!
I pray that Papà, Mamma, and my sister will always be safe, healthy, and happy!
I pray that the Lord will always be with us!
I pray that the spiritual light will always shine on Laterano!

lines.x: 'I thought the experiment was over, Lemuel.'
'Didn't you hear? The investigators came to school today to tell us our results. They even brought fruit tarts!'
'The two patient groups at Faber Central Hospital were discharged today... basically, their recovery had nothing to do with our praying the past two months.'
'The investigators told us not to be sad. It was all a social experiment by the Sixth Tribunal.'

lines.st: If prayer doesn't help—
Why do we pray?

lines.x: Another report from Nuova Volsinii.
Over the past six months, they have established trade with seventeen nomadic cities, like a direswine that never rests.
In the process, they have sown new seeds across all Siracusa.
It sounds like you approve of Nuova Volsinii's interactions with other cities, but do not intend to endorse their promotion of the New Urban Management Act.
You were the one who told me the youth need to be tested further, after you returned from Carnevale.
I suppose I recall something to that effect.
News from Terni. Unusual movement in Lupukarn.
The Golden Empress has been actively issuing edicts, while her counterpart has not been seen in public for a long time... our neighbor seems eager to change. Desperate, even.
Not just Leithanien. There are more than enough new sprouts upon this land. The matters of the famiglie should not occupy any more of Siracusa's time.
Mm-hm.
You seem ready to fall asleep.
It's almost time for my siesta.
Do you like this fabric?
I can have a suit made for you. I have just the tailor in mind.
Hah. I appreciate the offer, but there's no need.
Still prefer your cassock?
I am a priest, Sicilia.
But there is no church in this city. It's been almost seventy years since you left Laterano, hasn't it?
Sixty-eight. The Sankta sense of self is as persistent as our sweet tooth.
But you've never thought about going back. You have no need to. The walls are still white, the people still happy. Nothing ever changes in Paradise.
Otherwise, you wouldn't have come with me to Siracusa.
I'm not so sure...
Oh?

My hand's been trembling every time I grip my patron firearm. Maybe my old house in the Holy City has sprung a leak.
We're relics of a bygone age, Sicilia. You know how old people have hunches about certain things.
*Yawn*—
You should take a nap on the sofa.
Maybe we can find another setting to talk, once the down payment for this fabric has been made.
Sorry, sorry.
Zzz...
Father? You didn't just fall asleep on your feet, did you? Father! Are you alright?!
Urryyyyy—
What? Is that all?
Tila. Is the boss alright?
Why do you ask?
These are all documents that he reviewed. The only thing Legal changed was the formatting. He shouldn't need to spend so much time with them, should he?
I think Mr. Cliff is looking at that letter.
The letter from the frontier? What was the name on it again? Woodrow?
But there were only four words in it. 'Am well. Take care.' That's it.
I've heard a couple things about Davistown...
What are you getting at?
This ship will be approaching Max D.C. in twelve hours' time. How long have we been planning our entry into the capital market?
There're still a lot of problems to solve. Look at the people behind me. All our top ranks are waiting for the boss's orders!
But at a time like this, he's all tied up with his... his—I don't even know what to call him!
Mr. Cliff went to meet a certain Mr. Jackson before we set out.
Th-The Vice President?
Do you mean to say the boss has gotten the official nod to our cooperation with the companies in DC—
Don't get too excited.
No employee of Blacksteel Worldwide should doubt 'Clip' Cliff. Anything you don't think of, he will; anything you can't figure out, he will.
He was the one who put this team together, and he was the one who spread firearms beyond the walls of Laterano.
He was the one who expanded our business across the frontier, and beyond Columbia.
Mr. Cliff is a great leader, and a remarkable Sankta.
I agree completely, but... have you seen the look on his face? What could those four words have done to him?
Ladies and gentlemen—
What? Did the power go out?
Did some stupid merc knock something over? Or did Engineering cross the wrong wires?

Good news!
The wildfire's finally extinguished.
The bad news is that half the hillside has been burned away—
Woody? Are you okay? Are you hurt?
*cough* *cough* I'm fine... it's nothing.
He just came back from the woods to the west, where the worst of the fires were. He braved the flames to clear a control line.
He might have forgotten his age...
Can't let the fire take it all, not when things are finally looking up in these parts—I'm fine, I really am...
That's good to hear. I was worried.
Say, Woody, do you want to look at this?
That's thick. Some kind of important document?
It's from Blacksteel.
You're not gonna open it, Woody?
No, I don't think I want to.
What if it's a whole stack of gold certificates? Would go a long way to filling the gap the fires left behind.
Well then, YOU open it.
Gladly.
Well, Mr. Cliff sure can write a letter. It's so long the end is touching the ground.
What's the matter, Woody?
I... suddenly have a headache.
Is it the letter, or your wounds?
Were you waiting long?
Mister Ezell!
Happy Birthday, Cecelia!
This cake...
I, er, made it out of sweet potato.
I couldn't find cream or confetti sugar, so I sprinkled some dried vegetables on top of it. I tried to find the ones that had better colors...
Sorry, I did my best... 'slaves' don't have access to the nicest things. Galena helped out.
It's lovely!
Make a wish, Cecelia... well, I can guess what you'll wish for. Don't worry, I've been asking around. We'll find your father.
Yeah!
But my wish today is—I want to blow out the candles with you.
Huh?
You've been helping me ever since I lost my mama, even quitting your job at the Notarial Hall and coming with me all the way to Kazdel.
You've been by my side for so long.
I know you're in a lot of danger here, where my papa was born. It was you who taught me not to give up hope.
That's why I want you to do this with me, the last thing at my birthday party.
Cecelia...
Okay.
Ezell feels the warmth of Cecelia's hand in his own.

He sees the birthday cake made from sweet potatoes, and the girl with eyes full of excitement, and the big smile on her face. Before the flickering candlelight, both of them bow their heads and begin to pray.
May the order of Laterano shine upon us♪
Show us the path of life♪
That we may ever live in joy♪
East of the east of La Unidad♪
Laterano, holy city of white♪
Is that you, Aurela?
Such noise.
We're due for hymns in ten minutes. The children are practicing.
I'm talking about you, Nueda.
...Is it done?
Buried in the backyard. You can breathe easy now.
Shouldn't you wash the blood first? The children could come at any time.
What, are you afraid they might see me? Afraid they might hear their beloved Sister Nueda talk of killing people with that terrifying piece of metal?
Don't talk like that. We wouldn't be here if not for you... because you took up the gun, I am able to teach them hymns, and tuck them into bed.
Hmph...
Was it Singas soldiers, or True Bolívarians guerrillas this time?
Does it make a difference? A bunch of stinking parásitos who only know how to take from others.
Never imagined there were shitholes like this when I lived in Laterano.
I know... these sorts of things don't happen where you come from... in Laterano.
Your weapon, your dress, your habits... you still carry yourself as a Lateran.
A Lateran? Pah.
Honestly, I still don't know why you follow the Lateran faith.
I grew up in Bolívar, with no one to rely on. All I've ever known are these parásitos.
I wanted to believe there was more to the land than ruined houses and barren fields, more than just war, death, and taxes. I wanted to believe there was a place full of food, flowers, and hope...
And you wanted those chicos to believe it too.
One person's faith is no faith at all. We all need to look out for each other, like you and I do...
I wouldn't say we're looking out for one another.
Shh... Aurela, you have protected this place as the holy light protects Laterano. You've let me imagine a Paradise bathed in holy light... even if I've never been there.
Which is for the better.
If you really do believe in a Paradise bathed in holy light, it's definitely not Laterano you're looking for. Look elsewhere.
We're about to abandon this abbey, after all.
Time to go in, Sister.
The children are waiting for you to lead the last verse.
Aurela...
Yes?
Why did you leave Laterano?
Because I sinned, that's why... idiota.
Nueda pushes the chapel door open.
East of the east of La Unidad♪
Laterano, holy city of white♪
There, the earth hides no bombs♪
There, the fields share wheat most bright♪
Abode of our Lord, font of parental love♪
He hears our prayers, He grants our wishes♪
All shall bask in His love♪
Even the legless, even the armless♪
...This might be our last Vespers.
For years, we've tried to keep a low profile in this lawless land, to avoid the attention of the Singas nobles and the thugs who call themselves guerrillas.
But they followed the trail of some escaped convicts, and found our abbey. They're now looking at our farms and burdenbeasts.
Aurela has scouted the area. Tonight, she will lead us through a nearby canyon, and we will leave this place.
We might go north into the plains, or south into the jungles. It will be a long trip, but we've been blessed with a good harvest. We will have enough food...
Sister—
Don't worry, Hugo.
Trust Aurela. Trust me. Trust yourself and those around you. And trust 'Laterano'...
Keep your voice steady. You remember the last verse, don't you?
East of the east of La Unidad♪
Laterano, holy city of white♪
The guns there shoot fireworks♪
The targets are all apples♪
Through the chapel windows, Nueda watches the sunset. The halos of the Sanktas in the procession continue to illuminate the room, and the choir sings.

She has sung these hymns countless times. It is said that they were first written down by the saints, on their long and difficult journeys, and passed down through the centuries, translated and arranged by Bolívarian scholars.
'Laterano' will protect them through briar and bramble, through the long, cold night.
What the...?!
Perhaps the sun left too soon and forgot to leave some light. The halos and wings of the Sanktas in the choir suddenly shatter, and their light is extinguished.
Nueda is stunned.
Her esoteric knowledge is vast, thanks to the legacy of the K'uk'ulkan, but not a single word or symbol explains the scene before her eyes.
She is so stunned that she forgets to stop singing, murmuring the hymns by sheer instinct. Trembling voices echo through the chapel—
Abode of our Lord, font of parental love♪
He punishes evil and drives out injustice♪
All shall bask in His love♪
Whether they have scars or stones♪
When we pray sincere, He gives us bread♪
When we seek salvation, He sets us free♪
Merda!
Agenir, your halo—
Diamine!
Wh-What's going on?
Calm down. We figured out what's going on. Somebody forgot to set the right parameters when testing a gun at the firing range. Accidentally overloaded the power grid.
Engineering's on it and the power should come back any minute now... although, something doesn't feel quite right.
It's pitch black. Of course something doesn't feel right—
That's it! Where's Mr. Cliff's halo? It should be shining like a lamp—
Mr. Cliff?!
Woody?! I'll get a doctor!
Woody... no...
Mister Ezell... your halo... your wings... what...?
I'm fallen...?
Why...?
W-Was it my fault?
Ugh—Laterano...
Seniora Lemuen!
Saw you from across the street, but you didn't answer when I called your name...
Oh, I'm sorry. I was about to—
Oh, I forgot! It's your first day!
Let's see. 'Your Eminence, Cardinal Lemuen', is that right?
It is giving me goosebumps.
Hey, from submitting the application to becoming a cardinal's aide to finally getting the job, all it took was two years, right?
Isn't that a new record for the Seventh Tribunal?
It took much longer than breaking the wheelchair speed-shooting record.
Huh?
Just a joke.
Well, we all look up to you.
There are two hundred households up and down three streets waiting for you to deliver the cystymilk for their morning fruit tart and post-meal desserts...
Meanwhile, how often is a cardinal's presence really needed for diplomatic and national security business in Laterano?
Who's to say which one of us is more important to the city?
Fair point. Guess I should get to work!
I'll leave your cystymilk at the door. Two servings, as usual?
Yes.
Where's Seniora Lemuel working, anyway? One day I hear it's a logistics company in Lungmen, the next day I hear it's security for that pharma company called Rhodes Island...
I miss her. It's been a long time since I last saw her.
Who knows? Maybe she'll just show up tomorrow. And she wouldn't be happy if the fridge were empty.
You got that right.
Hm, isn't this a bit of a detour if you're headed to the Seventh Tribunal?
Just visiting a friend I haven't seen in a long time.
I see... have a nice day, Cardinal Lemuen.
Have an uneventful day. May the Law be with you and me.
Have a nice day.
Lemuen greets a few familiar faces on the way, then makes a turn at a corner, onto an utterly unremarkable street.
The clouds cast their shadows on the pristine paths. A breeze brings the sweet aroma in the air along with it. Soon, the surroundings grow quiet, as Lemuen approaches her destination.
Her life has undoubtedly changed. But her steps are light. She gladly welcomes these changes.
Lemuen has always been that way.
The Laterans have always been that way.
'Have an uneventful day. May the Law be with you and me.'
Lemuen utters those words.
At the end of the street is a massive building that nearly touches the clouds. The streamlined structure seems decidedly out of place amongst the low profile of church buildings around it.
It is no exaggeration to say that it virtually appeared out of nowhere, yet it also seems like it has always been there.
It welcomes Lemuen's arrival with open arms.

lines.st: Danger.
Repeat.
Danger.
...Danger level... Analyzing...
Unknown...
Removing restrictions.
Unlocking bottom-level computing area.
Deductive computing power exceeds maximum.
Laterano... explosion.

lines.x: Finally, I can catch my breath.
I don't think I've talked so much in my entire life. And I've got another lifetime's worth of talking to do tomorrow.
Hah. How about we trade jobs, then?
I'll greet the Kazdelian delegation. You deal with the thirteen nomadic abbeys scheduled to arrive tomorrow. How's that?
All this running around is hard on my old bones.
That will not be necessary, Reverend Patrizion.
How is communicating with our new Sarkaz friends?
Fine enough.
Not all the Sarkaz have set hatred aside. That ancient Revenant with Speaker Wiš'adel, for example, got rather worked up at the mere sight of the Matriarch.
But the Speaker shut them up. She's been so magnanimous that I'm having trouble reconciling my impression with the rumors I've heard of her.
The chief spokesman for the Kazdelian delegation is a Lich by the name of Fremont. I wish you could have seen it—

A three-hour debate in which he engaged seventeen cardinals from the First and Sixth Tribunals in a, well, 'animated exchange of opinions', encompassing everything from politics to the Law, from faith to life and death.
Small wonder that whole procession of cardinals was spotted around the guava juice machine afterwards. They must've been soothing their parched lips and throats.
Such energy at his age! I envy the man.
Long story short, Kazdel accepted our proposal. The Royal Court will review the legacy of royal authority, and the Sarkaz will unite through the mediation of Laterano.
I have also spoken to the Empresses' Voice. The Leithanian delegation will arrive at the Holy City early tomorrow morning.
Now that's something you don't see every day.
Remember how few answered the call of the first Summit of Nations two years ago. Most thought that the Lateran Declaration was little more than a piece of paper, empty posturing.
Empress Ewigegnade declined our invitation then. This time, she is proactively asking to take part in the Summit of Nations.
Even Empress Grimmacht, who has long been out of the public eye, came out of seclusion to join the envoy and visit Laterano...
It's a strong show of good faith from Leithanien.
Shall we say the second Summit of Nations has already met expectations?
Exceeded them, Cardinal Velliv.
Columbia, Ursus, Yan, even distant Ægir... all nations that should be here are now present.
It's like a dream.
Only the inevitable result of Laterano's development over the ages.

The prophesied calamity draws near. The black snow on the ice of Sami, the hole in the sky above Leithanien, even the raging tides that dash against the coast, are all portents of its inevitable arrival.
In the face of calamity, everyone wants order—Lateran order.
You're correct. But even after all our preparation, the sight still feels... unfamiliar.

If it's like that for you, imagine how it must be for everyone else. Even if one has heard of the path that Laterano has paved in response to the prophesied calamity countless times during one's enlightenment...
One cannot help but hesitate when coming face-to-face with the ultimate end.
What about yourself?
It's bad form to flaunt one's age, but when you get to be my age, there's not much to hesitate about.
Are you ready to retire?
I can hardly bite through a cactus tart these days. This summit may be the last thing I can do for Laterano.
That might have more to do with your taste in food, Reverend... I would have a hard time biting through one of those, er, cactus tarts you're talking about.
You are aware that all the cactus tarts among the refreshments were made specially for you? Literally no one else in all of Laterano has any interest in them.
You must be joking. The cactus tarts were a recommendation from an old friend, a true connoisseur of desserts... what was his name again...?
Doesn't matter. Back to the topic at hand.
The doubts among the people make the subsequent ceremony at the Shrine of Nurtury all the more important.
We must show everyone, in the most forceful manner, the potential of Laterano.
Of course. I'll get on the handover of the Seventh Tribunal immediately. When the Summit of Nations gets underway, I will head to the Shrine and ensure that the ceremony is executed without a hitch.
No need to rush.
Hah. Never mind. Young people are all the same... they complain a lot, but they have no patience when the time for action comes.
Are you reminded of your Fia?
I believe she has returned, according to the mission logs?
Yes. We'll have a family get-together tomorrow... I hope she likes my gift.
Huh?
The Shrine of Nurtury...
What a miracle.
Lemuen, Cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal. Go ahead.
Cardinal Lemuen, your presence is required at Via Jovita, Pagus Stevonus, to investigate a possible bombing.
Reverend Patrizion? I don't believe this was on today's schedule.
You may not understand, as you were recently created, but emergency assignments take precedence over all other arrangements, regardless of organ.
An emergency assignment...? But where did this decision come from? Is there any documentation to reference?
Whether a situation is an emergency or not is always self-evident. There is no need for additional determination.
Someone of unknown identity and in possession of unregistered explosives has been seen in public multiple times without Notarial Hall oversight—
This is a grave threat to the order of Laterano. The centuries-long peace of the Holy City must not be broken by an illegal explosion during the Summit of Nations, with the eyes of all lands upon us.
The suspect has been sighted on Via Jovita in Pagus Stevonus. You are the nearest officer of the Curia.
Proceed to the scene immediately. Your aide and Notarial Hall executors will join you, ETA approximately ten minutes following your arrival.
Understood.
...Lemuen.
What else do I need to know?
I had meant to congratulate you on your creation... but I guess it should wait for tomorrow's gathering.
Will you be there? We all know you've only gotten busier as your retirement draws near, and you always...
Always put my duties first? I can't deny that.
I've postponed meetings with old friends so many times that I can hardly remember their names, leaving me too embarrassed to meet them anymore.
But I'll try to make the gathering tomorrow.
Take care, Reverend.
I'll head to Via Jovita immediately.
Can you hear me?
Lemuen?
I'm near the Shrine of Nurtury, but something just came up and I need to be elsewhere.
Did you say something? I couldn't make it out. Why are you whispering? You're not the type to keep your voice down.
(No no no, Lemuen, you misunderstand...)
(I'm fine, but there's a... situation. I need to stay hidden and keep quiet.)
See you at Fiammetta's thing, then.
(Sure.)
(Take care.)
Phew.
He's here! He might be whispering, but he can't fool my ears!
I didn't need you to guess where he is!
He's not behind the curtains or the pillars...
'This wardrobe is only half his height. There's no way he could hide in here.'—Is what he'd want us to think!
What a dummy!
It's not that he's dumb, it's that we're too smart!
We found you, Senior Nurturer!
Alright, alright, you got me—thirty extra minutes of free time today!
Yay!
Let's play 'Halo', Senior Nurturer!
I'll be the first to get in the bottle, the first one out the front, and the first one you hug! Then we'll hold hands and meet the other kids behind when they come out!
Are you bothering Andoain again, children?
They just want to play a little longer. It's all fine. Giselle, Romya, go make your preparations at the Hall of Enlightenment. We can start the game once I get there, okay?
Don't you ever say no to the children?
I guess that's why they trust you and look up to you...
Just a few weeks ago, they were terrified of these unfamiliar halls. It was a struggle just to give them a change of clothes.
Now, they feel at home... all thanks to you.
I didn't do anything, except stay with them.
You're very much like the Matriarch, down to your humility. Just having you with them makes all the difference.
Was it not your companionship that helped the mother you and the Matriarch brought back through the worst?
I remember the first thing the surgeon did after leaving the operating room was to check if he still had his halo.
He could not understand how the mother managed to survive with her baby, after her abdomen was impaled by a spear.
It was a miracle.
We'll call it a miracle once she's successfully given birth. Meanwhile, I will continue to care for her as best as I can.
I'm... beginning to understand why the Matriarch holds you in such high regard. It is reassuring to know that you are in charge of the most important duty of the Nativity, the delivery of the child.
The Shrine of Nurtury will be ready for full operation if the Nativity goes well. Every child born on Terra henceforth shall have the protection and blessing of the Holy City.
That will be the true miracle.
I hope so... I do hope so.
I must not keep you any longer. Giselle and Romya are still waiting for you!
Via Jovita, Pagus Stevonus
It's quiet...
A procession of halos stretches from one end of the street to the other. No one stops, and there is no commotion.
Someone may step out of the procession to enter a passing confessional, or pick up an ice cream cone from a dessert cart, before continuing on their soundless parade.
Silence and order. This is how things have always been in Laterano, the Holy City.
I'm in position.
Identifying suspicious personnel. No unusual activity at this time.
A figure flashes by a corner in the distance. The wind blows her hood open briefly, revealing a streak of bright red.
Have you checked all the intersections?
Yes, Your Eminence. We didn't find any suspicious individuals.
We also interviewed potential witnesses nearby, but at this time we have no concrete leads.
Mushrooms might spring up on a wet pillar by themselves, but bombs don't, do they?
Huh... how could they?
With the exception of public officers of the Curia and the Shrine of Nurtury, anyone who is in possession of explosives is subject to surveillance by the Notarial Hall.
It's been years since an unplanned gunshot last rang out in Laterano, never mind an explosion.
Lemuen bends down and retrieves a bomb fragment from the damaged pillar.
It's pretty sophisticated. If a bomb like this was filled with standard explosives, the blast would have damaged far more than just one pillar.
Do you mean it was homemade? But why? A practical joke?
The Lateran Arms Control List was compiled by the Curia's extremely experienced explosives experts. Pretty much anything that could possibly cause a good-sized explosion is on there.
It would take considerable effort to assemble a bomb like this using materials not on the list. No one would do this for a whimsical practical joke.
Besides, today is no ordinary occasion. The second Summit of Nations is underway.
Lemuen recalls the streak of red. She feels a bit of unease, and even more perplexity.
Get me the files on recent movements around Via Jovita... correction, all of Pagus Stevonus.
What is it?
Files that are clearly relevant to the case at hand should have been retrieved the moment we received our orders.
There you are!
(Pats chest) *pant*... *pant*...
Forgive me, Cardinal Lemuen. Here are the files you requested.
My name is Amos, I'm an investigator with the Seventh Tribunal. I will be serving as your aide and conclavist.
You're late, Amos.
Patrizion said you'd be here approximately ten minutes after I arrived at the scene. Here we are, thirty minutes later.
I'm so sorry, I was preparing refreshments for the welcome ceremony. I didn't think it would be so urgent...
(Shhh—)
I assume you've heard about me, if the Seventh Tribunal appointed you as my aide.
L-Lemuen the Silent...
I tolerate no excuses.
Want some ice cream, Federico? This one's on me.
I am not in the habit of enjoying dessert in the middle of a mission.
Besides, I already partook before leaving the Notarial Hall.
So it was you who took the last cup from the lounge...
How about you tell me why you rushed out of the Notarial Hall in such a hurry? Reward for me solving that case, so to speak.
You've been standing at this intersection for ten minutes, but there's nothing here.
I am under no obligation to share my private affairs with colleagues.

You said you were in the middle of a mission...
What's that sound...?
Oh—you were waiting for a confessional!

You've certainly accumulated your fair share of 'sins' as the executor with the highest rate of complaints at the Notarial Hall, but it's still a huge surprise to see you proactively seeking confession.
Here.
What?
I expensed the cost of a City of Gold Blind Box on that field mission to Sargon, which resulted in my reimbursements being twice as much as what was actually spent on mission costs...
I went out my door with shame weighing on my heart... and spotted a confessional driving by... Anyway, long story short, I got myself an annual pass. You can borrow it.
Arrival announcement.
Automated Confessional ELCARO-001 is arriving at this station. Please line up to enjoy confessional services.
'May all who are lost find salvation.'
The confessional enters their view, and Federico reaches out.
Hahahahahaha!
Even a robot knows you don't have any emotional needs, Federico.

Virtually every Lateran comes into contact with confessionals in the course of their everyday lives. In a way, these machines hold more diverse and comprehensive data than that stored in the Notarial Hall.
They form natural archives, revealing abnormalities.
...I have arrived at certain conclusions.
Hm... one second, Federico. You don't have to share your conclusions, but could you at least explain what you're looking for?
Self-reflection is part of the daily studies of the faithful. As an extension of the Church, there are almost as many confessionals in the Holy City as there are dessert carts.
These mobile public facilities roam the streets and alleys of Laterano, ensuring that citizens' emotional relief is always immediately available.
In the tiny, private space, among the holy sculptures, guns, and pleasant music, a penitent may spill their guts to the electronic confessor.
Sins could range from the shame of consuming too much sugar, to the crime of betraying one's kin or bringing harm to one's people.
After rejecting Federico, the automated confessional ELCARO-001 stops in an alley, opening its door to allow a mysterious guest to enter.

lines.st: 'You're late, Exusiai.'

lines.x: You were the one who told me to be careful.
I made sure I didn't have a tail on my butt, and you didn't give that stone-faced guy a chance to fiddle with anything.

lines.st: 'He may have observed something abnormal about me.'

lines.x: Let's pray he doesn't catch you and tear you apart, piece-by-piece.
What is it?

lines.st: 'You changed your clothes.'

lines.x: Of course I did. A Penguin Logistics uniform is way too conspicuous for Laterano.
I designed this outfit myself. I've only worn it once, at my graduation!
And it's YOU we should be talking about. Do you know how weird it is to hear your voice come out of a confessional's speakers?

lines.st: 'Back on topic. The experiment failed?'

lines.x: I hate to admit it... but yes.
It took so much effort to find the replacement materials—the market, the bakery, the playground, the school's secret shop... I went through every single source I could think of.
And it didn't even blast the pillar in two! Any material that's even halfway decent is restricted, and no one could give an answer when I asked why.
It's not because of this 'Summit of Nations', right?
If anyone in Laterano thought the beautiful sight and sound of an explosion would be any threat to the Summit of Nations, then it's not me with the problem, it's Laterano!
As for the Summit of Nations... they just announced the final event of the whole deal—some 'Nativity' ceremony in that shrine.
Are you telling me they built a building big enough to be seen from any corner of Laterano, not as a dessert museum, but to put on some kind of LIVE BIRTHING SHOW?!

lines.st: 'You have corroborated the anomaly of your home with your own eyes, as you had hoped.'
'As you can see, this is not the Laterano you know.'

lines.x: It's not like I could just trust you blindly. You're just an 'electronic confessor', after all.
But now, I believe you.
There's something REALLY wrong with Laterano. And the worst part is, nobody thinks anything's wrong at all!

lines.st: 'The shrine, and the ceremony. If all the anomalies are centered around the two of them...'

lines.x: Then it's on us to bomb them, isn't it?
If I figured out how to blow up a shrine that big using only common household items, shouldn't every university in Laterano give me a lifetime achievement award?

lines.st: 'As long as you reawaken their love of explosions.'

lines.x: Can you believe it? The Laterans need someone to remind them that they love explosions? Sounds like a nightmare, doesn't it? Shouldn't take more than an afternoon at the Firearms Workshop to do the job.
Oh yeah! The Firearms Workshop! The workshops may be closed, but the people are still around!

lines.st: 'One moment, Exusiai.'

lines.x: I know what you're gonna say. It's too dangerous. Firearms Workshop is the first place they would look if anything happened, right?
Don't worry, I'll be careful. You do the same, and don't get yourself taken apart. That's my job, once this is all over and done with.
Anyway... Welcome to Laterano!
My investigation is now concluded. Thank you all for your contributions.

We can go back to the Seventh Tribunal once we've signed off on the paperwork. If the refreshments at the welcome ceremony haven't gone stale yet, may I count on your assistance in eliminating them with extreme prejudice?
I would like you to accompany me to see the gun-knights, Amos.
Is there a problem?
If by 'gun-knights' you mean Reverend Patrizion, I... I'll ask Joshua to accompany you.
Explain.
There was an incident a decade ago, when a nomadic abbey was escorting prisoners back to Laterano. A terrible fight broke out between a gun-knight and her cohort...
By the time the Curia sent someone, much blood had been spilled, and the gun-knight had fallen.
She was exiled from Laterano and has not been heard from since.
I've heard of the case.
That gun-knight was my wife. It was Reverend Patrizion who led the clean-up task force and exiled her.
You've been with the Seventh Tribunal far longer than I have, Conclavist Amos. I don't need to remind you of our duty, and the duty of the gun-knights—
No, no, you misunderstand.
My wife was entirely at fault.
Any Sankta who raises their patron firearm against one of their own must be judged by the Law and be expelled from Paradise.
I am in no position to criticize Reverend Gun-Knight Patrizion... however, I can't bring myself to see him like this either.
Is this how you've handled any previous dealings with the gun-knights?
I... yes. I'm sorry.
...*sigh*.
I-I'm very sorry. Give me five minutes, I'll try to collect myself, and maybe I can go with you...
I don't mean to reprimand you. I just think that this is part of what we do at the Seventh Tribunal.
We spend our time seeing people we don't want to see, and not seeing the people we want to see. Or worse—seeing the people we want to see in the worst ways possible.
I hated handing down instructions to Mostima and the others.
But they said you volunteered to join the Seventh Tribunal...
Because there's a lot I can do here.
The same goes for you, Amos.
Thank you. But I don't really have aspirations. It was like that when my wife was around, and even more so after she left...
There's a lot at the Seventh Tribunal that needs doing. For example, there are still several Firearms Workshops that need attention.
It's our first priority right now. The seizure of the Firearms Workshops must proceed without a hitch in order to prevent any more explosions.
I'll leave it to you. Be quick, won't you?
Are you tired?
You've gone through six rows of wheat with your sickle since dawn. Perhaps you should take a break by the haystacks.
Arturia.
I'm not so frail. Besides...
Besides?
Nothing. I just wanted to see where the fields end.
I can't make out the boundary. Even my music can't penetrate it.
You'll have to work harder!
We meet again, Sister Hildegard.
Good afternoon, Arturia.
I brought your lunch—bread, some ice cream crackers, and a large mug of craft beer!
Thank you. Work harder, you said?
What I mean is, there's plenty for us to do.
Look at the golden stalks, the undulating waves of wheat... it's another bumper harvest in Laterano.
Setting aside our own food stocks, Landen Monastery will be able to put twice as much grain as usual into cystymilk bread and craft beer this year. It'll be a good source of income!
Why are you laughing?
The way you talk about life makes me appreciate this prison even more, Sister Hildegard.
The respect you show for this prison makes me appreciate you even more, Arturia.
I'm very curious about your transgression. I heard the other nuns saying that your music is beautiful, but dangerous.
I have a question.
If I recall correctly, Landen Monastery was founded to protect the Pope and important clerics of the Lateran Church...
Which makes me wonder when this place became a prison.
Is it not protecting... er, important clerics and the peace of Laterano, to put certain individuals of note under observation?
The significance of Landen Monastery has never changed.
Besides, the more important the monastery is to the Holy City, the less likely we will fall insolvent.
Hm...
How did you acclimate to this place so quickly as a Liberi?
What do you mean?
I grew up here.
...I see.
Oh, I almost forgot. This is what you requested yesterday.
Gratias.
What made you want to read The Saints' Travels all of a sudden?
Arturia gazes into the distance.
Behind her, the worn image of the monastery; before her, a golden sea of wheat. The wheat field runs to the edge of the Holy City, and seems to extend infinitely into the distance.
The breeze blowing through the wheat makes nary a sound. Laterano seems to slumber.
Penance, of course. What other reason might a prisoner have?
Firearms Workshop, Via Borgos, Pagus Faber
Hello...? Anybody home?
Sculptures of saints line the walls of the small workshop, acting as makeshift display shelves, exhibiting guns to would-be customers in impossible arrangements.
But Amos does not see a single customer. No one stops at the door to the workshop, except for himself. His first feeling is that the whole place seems cold and cheerless.
Not even the proprietor is present. Gazing on the dim light cast upon the sculptures, his second feeling is one of eeriness.
Senior Paganini? Are you in?
(Why would a gunsmith have such an absurd name?)
Amos looks around, then bumps into something.
He looks around and finds himself staring into a pair of massive, luminous eyes that peer straight at him from the shadows.
Eek—
Who are you, barging into my place like this—let me put my glasses on!
A functionary?
Sorry. I was taking a nap because there's been no business lately. I wasn't expecting an official visit.
I am Cardinal Aide Amos. I came to see that the handover of this Firearms Workshop is in order. And you are...?
I'm Paganini. The guy with the absurd name.
Wait. You're a Liberi?
Do I look like a Sankta to you?
Sorry. What I meant is, I've never seen a Liberi in this line of work. After all, only Sankta use patron firearms—
Got a problem with it?
Chefs don't necessarily eat their own cooking. I can't use the weapons I make, that's all.
Do you need my gunsmith's license? I must have made close to a thousand patron firearms for the people of Laterano, if not more. Maybe even yours...
Whew, this is some real 'premium' work. Well above my craftsmanship, that's for sure.
Not that it matters anymore. I'm about to close up shop.
All recent orders have been cancelled. The Notarial Hall can have this last batch of guns.
Y-You've already prepared. I thought...
Thought I would be trouble? Thought I would give you a hard time?
Th-That's not what I meant.
The registrar indicates that this workshop has been around for a century. As a Liberi who has chosen to make patron firearms for the Sankta, it must hold incredible significance to you—
Save it. Anyone could tell that gunsmiths have become a dying breed these last few years. We've all been waiting for the day to come.
If you're asking whether I like it or not: No, of course I don't like it. Do you want to hear about the insomnia I've had lately?
I can arrange a confessional for you, if you wish. The Seventh Tribunal will cover the cost.
If anyone here needs a confession, it's you.
This old man's set to retire. But my apprentice, Vannini, she hasn't even had the chance to make her own patron firearm.
If all the Firearms Workshops are gone, you'll be responsible for her future.
Of course. Closing the Firearms Workshops is part of a process to make the firearms manufacturing more standardized and efficient. Gunsmiths like you and your apprentice remain valuable talent.

I will pass this on to the Sixth Tribunal, which is responsible for civil matters. Your apprentice, Vannini, should receive a recommendation to the Shrine of Gunsmithing in Pagus Michaelion, which is almost complete.
Shrine of Gunsmithing, huh...? I hope they're that confident when they actually start smithing guns.
I have faith in Laterano's...
Yeah, yeah, I've heard it too many times.
...If you have no objection, I would like to ask you to sign these papers.
Effective immediately, this workshop will cease operation, with the Notarial Hall handling follow-up matters.
Pleasant day to you, senior.
See yourself out.
Pagus Faber, Via Borgos... how familiar.
This is the place!
Has the connection been established?
I-I think so.
We've been waiting for two days on the outskirts. I want to go out and get some fresh air!
These Laterans invited us onto this mobile abbey while we were wandering in the wilderness, then made us wait like this once we got to Laterano.
I apologize for keeping you waiting.
Twelve other nomadic abbeys have reached Laterano, in addition to the busy schedule of the Summit of Nations. We beg your understanding.
The Notarial Hall has prepared temporary accommodations in Pagus Ambrosius. Once you've settled down, the Sixth Tribunal will help you with your registration and social welfare.
S-Sister? Do you mean that outsiders like us will get to stay in Laterano?
From the second Summit of Nations, Laterano's doors will be open to all races...
You shall find a home in the city.
Hey, Extra! Are you alright?
I'll find a doctor!
No!
You can't let the Sankta find out, not yet...
Please let me through.
D-Don't worry about it, Sister.
We can take care of this. You're busy, aren't you?
Argh—
Are you... pregnant, Sarkaz?
Your people didn't stop the Sarkaz from entering the abbey! You're gonna make it a problem now?
You want me to go, I'll go! I don't need help from the Sankta!
You won't lay a hand on me or my baby!
You misunderstand. It's just we have no directive for accommodating pregnant mothers in the abbey's standard operating procedures, and thus Sister Angie is not sure what to do.
Allow me to bring her to the Shrine of Nurtury, Sister Angie.
The Shrine of Nurtury is not yet open to the public. This is against regulations.
Besides, what are you doing here?
Our Lord has guided me to where I should be.
We must have some flexibility. The Shrine of Nurtury cannot ignore an ailing mother.
Yes, Matriarch.
I am Keph, Matriarch of the Shrine of Nurtury. Simply put, I am its caretaker.
The woman with golden hair turns slowly toward the Sarkaz. Her voice is soft yet crystal clear, seemingly carefully probing.
The Sarkaz sees the two black horns on the woman's head, then turns her gaze to the woman's eyes. She sees an empty void underneath the intricate patterns of the eyepatch.
What are you... Sarkaz? Sankta? Blind, too?
Sarkaz, Sankta... we have no need of these words of distinction. You and I, we are alike... like you, I am nurturing lives that I treasure.
My lack of sight does not prevent me from perceiving you and the new life you carry... the anxiety and joy that it brings you.
We Sarkaz don't talk this way. You're Sankta, a strange Sankta with children.
Do you raise your children in that... Shrine of something? What kind of place is it?
The Shrine of Nurtury, where new life is born and nurtured. You and I stand in its shadow.
A shrine... for giving birth?
Birth is sacred. Each new birth is a continuation of the Lateran faith. Only in this way may we shape the future.
I don't understand.
Long have you wandered the wilderness. You have not enjoyed the care that a mother should. You are weak... and so is your child.
Are you sure you want to leave the Holy City that is so near and return to the wilderness at a time like this?
Your name is 'Extra', madam?
You don't like it?
We understand the difficulties facing the Sarkaz... many Sarkaz mothers name their children 'Extra', seeing them as nothing more than a little extra in their lives.
This name's got nothing to do with my mom. I took it myself after I got pregnant. Who's to say who's the extra?
Ah...
You got a problem?
You treasure your bonds with your child. You are proud of having this little extra in your life, and you want everyone to know.
This is a wonderful, noble sentiment, madam. It should not be tainted by racism or hatred.
I know what you fear.
All people here in this abbey, Sankta, Feline, Kuranta, Lupo, and Vulpo alike bear witness.
The Summit of Nations is underway. Countless outlanders from all nations have flocked to Laterano. Laterano would not be foolish enough to harm a pregnant mother with all this attention.
(Nods)
(Casts a glance)
You have my word.
Alright.
Thank you for your understanding.
Let us welcome the birth of a new life together in Laterano. It will bridge a gulf and bring new meaning. This birth will be a great achievement.
This is why I have come... Our Lord has guided me to where I should be.
Welcome to Laterano... you are needed here, all of you.
Whatever it is you're after, I'm sure I can't help you.
I haven't said anything yet!
Alright, Seniora Sankta, what do you want? Do you need your guardian gun fixed, or you want some custom ammo?
Go out the door and turn left. You might just find one of the last Firearms Workshops in Pagus Michaelion that hasn't shut down yet, if you can get there before the functionaries.
I signed the handover documents from the Seventh Tribunal ten minutes ago. I'd be violating the Law if I did anything with this stuff now.
What if I said I wanted to make an explosion that would shake the city?
Not interested.
Come on! You're Master Pag, the King of Gunsmiths! If anyone would want to see an explosion, it's you!
Don't butter me up with a title you just made up on the spot.
I know what you're afraid of, Master Pag.
All I need is a little help—lend me some equipment, some of your expert knowledge—to help me figure out what's going on in Laterano.
I'm really anxious about all this.
My boss always says nothing gets in the way of Penguin Logistics completing our mission, or the after party!
Uh...
You don't need to DO anything. That way, you won't be violating the Law, right?
Get your guardian gun away from my collection, Sankta.
Besides, I know this gun. I made the damned thing—
Haha. Recognize it, don't you?
Then you also know how powerful it is.
I got Dr. Paola to make a new pair of glasses for you, magister! She admonished me to no end, she says it's your punishment to keep wearing those antiques of yours...
Here, try them on! Uh—
Wh-Who's this? What's going on, Magister Paganini?
Hey, if it isn't the quiet little girl! Look how fluently you can gab now!
What was your name again? Vanheim? Vannessa? Oh, I know—
—Vannini!
See? We still got our teamwork!
I remember now! You're El! You came to pick your first patron firearm...
Magister Paganini never lets me bother the customers when they come to the shop. But there was such a ruckus outside that day, I couldn't help but peek...
Magister Paganini found me out, though. I don't remember much else because I was so scared he was going to yell at me... sorry!
Oh, so that's why? You were afraid...
(Nods)
Weird, I thought you looked happy.
What brings you here today, El?
(Winks)
Vani.
The Seventh Tribunal came today. My workshop is...
I'm not officially a gunsmith yet. I don't actually work for this shop. It's not against the Law, strictly speaking.
Can I help this customer, Magister?
Gotcha, Senior Nurturer!
Where did you go? Romya said you were hiding from us. Is that true?
Of course not.
Then let's play Halo!
Father Andoain is very busy. How about you guys play with me?
Your Eminence.
I have some private matters to attend to. I'll be back soon.
No need to be nervous. I have your leave request on file. What I really should ask is, what are you doing here with the children when you're supposed to be on leave?
I thought I had enough time for one round of Halo.
And then? Did you think the children would let you go so easily?
I'll think of something.
I've heard you're completely incapable of saying 'no' to the children.
Who could ever imagine that the first Nurturer the Matriarch nurtured, one whose diligence and piety are beyond question, was once a criminal who violated the Law?
I'm only attending the family gathering of a friend I've not seen in a long time, Your Eminence.
I told you, no need to be nervous. I'm not worried about you burning Laterano while blowing a balloon or baking a cake.
I'm just curious. Why is there such a stark difference between how the Matriarch sees you, and your profile in the Curia's records?
The Matriarch has never overlooked or denied my sins, Your Eminence. She simply places more importance on my understanding with her.
It was before she became Matriarch. Our paths crossed by sheer chance, when I was taking refuge from a Catastrophe in an abandoned nomadic abbey.

The Matriarch may be blind, but her sense of hearing is impeccable. She caught a faint cry coming from the basement, in the midst of the deafening roar of Originium dashing against the distant mountains.
A mother, lying in a pool of blood in the shadows on death's door. She attempted to intimidate the Matriarch and protect her own child, but all she managed was an expression of great pain.
The mother you and the Matriarch first brought to the Holy City? Her injuries were such that even the most determined physician would have given up hope.
What made you think she would live?
I didn't... I didn't even think she would last the night. But the Matriarch had faith in a miracle, as though it was an inevitability.
The Matriarch wanted me to believe too, believe that the poor mother would find salvation, thanks to us.
And she did... at first, without medical training, all I could do was empathize and be by her side, caring for her in what ways I could.
You lingered in a stranded abbey for three months, for the sake of a miracle?
...Laterano needs a miracle.
Then you're much more rational than I thought, both of you. Perhaps I should see you in a different light.
Certainly, if it convinces the Curia to offer greater support for the Shrine of Nurtury.
So rational it's almost boring.
Hurry along now, Nurturer Andoain. At the very least, I hope to see the Nativity through without a hitch.
Madam Extra, you must take your nutrition...
I said, I don't want it.
If you have specific dietary requirements, we can make efforts to accommodate them.
*Sarkaz expletive*, you think I'm a picky eater?
But...
Yes, you have beds and food, and your leader... that 'Matriarch' said you wouldn't do anything to me.
But I just don't feel comfortable.
In the wilderness, I know what can kill me, and I know what I need to do to give birth to this baby.
Where to hunt, how to keep warm, how to block off the entrance to my cave... And with that, I don't have to worry about myself or my baby.
But here... ugh.
Get out. Take the food with you. I didn't touch it. I'm sure there are people who need it more in this place.
Reverend Gun-Knight Patrizion?
Yes. The Matriarch was looking for me?
She's inside. A minute, please.
The Matriarch has been tending to the pregnant Sarkaz woman who arrived in poor shape. But it's been an hour and she hasn't come out yet...
I'm not one to speculate... but the prejudices of the past have made it difficult for many Sarkaz to accept our kindness.
The Matriarch has no weapons on her. I worry that...
I'll check on her.
The gun-knight Patrizion has served Laterano for many decades. He has forgiven many Sarkaz, and slaughtered many more.
He has heard the Sarkaz curse in fury, cry out in pain, roar in hatred, even plead in despair...
But he has never heard them pray.
And yet, the pregnant Sarkaz now kneels before a holy statue, her hands clasped together, stumbling over the unfamiliar words of liturgy.
The Matriarch stands behind her, her head bowed in prayer as well, the pair bathing in the warm glow. When the Sarkaz trips over a sentence, the Matriarch waits for her.
All mortals desire peace and happiness, and fear hardship and sorrow.
Therefore, we stand together in prayer, regardless of race.
Matriarch, the Sarkaz...

She has only just left behind the restless life of a wanderer. Now that she is experiencing idleness for the first time in her life, all the worries she has never had time to ponder have rushed to the surface.
She simply needs to learn to comfort herself.
You taught her how to pray?
We've only recited a few verses together.
Let's go somewhere where we won't disturb her.
Why have you called me here today, Matriarch?
I wanted to talk with you, to ease your troubles.
But it seems like words may not be so necessary now.
Troubles? I am not troubled.
Are you not troubled, or do you dare not be troubled?
Everyone in Laterano knows that our ancient order was established in response to the prophesied calamity.
As calamity descends, our order is stretched to its limit... yet no one can predict what will become of Laterano one day.
I cannot imagine it, but I have faith it will be beautiful.
You have given too much for it. The granddaughter you never have time to be with, the old friend whose name you've forgotten, and the other old friends you've exiled as guardian of order...
To deny Laterano's future is to deny everything that you have given and sacrificed for it. It is to deny yourself.
Have no fear. A sliver of that future has already revealed itself to you.
If the faith of Laterano can bring comfort even to the faithless, if Sarkaz can pray side-by-side with Sankta...
Then our order shall open its arms to all.
There will be no barriers, no one left behind, no unwilling partings.
Sounds like you're describing a dream.
Are we not about to lift the veil that separates dream from reality, before the eyes of all?
A miracle will befall this land when everyone believes that even the most fantastical dream can become reality.
The Nativity must go off without a hitch, Patrizion.
I'll do my best, Matriarch.
You're blocking the screen, 'Holy Debt Collector'!
You're the one who decided to sit that far back... not that you're missing anything if I block a few scenes. It's just Eight-Armed Chainsaw Guy, after all.
And stop quoting random lines from the movie. You're not on a mission or in a briefing. You're in my house!
I kind of like that name. Maybe you can use it as your code name for your next mission.
Not you too, Lemuen...
You two sure can work together, when it comes to making up bad code names.
Haha.
How's life as a cardinal, Lemuen? Keeping busy?
Busier than when I was in a wheelchair.
When was it that you got out of it for good, anyway? I don't remember.
You don't really care much about me, do you? I'm so sad, and the only thing that can comfort me is if you let me feel your horns.
Why don't you ask if Holy Debt Collector might let you touch her ear plumage?
Go ahead. I don't mind.
I never thought it would be the Summit of Nations that brought us back together after all this time.
It's like every Lateran out there got called back to the city.
I can understand Legati like Mostima getting recalled, but the old man brought me back when I was in the middle of a high-priority mission to Sargon!
Say, where is your grandpa anyway? He's the one throwing the party, right?
Should be back soon.
Who knows what he's up to? All he said was that since he was retiring, he wanted to make an important announcement while everyone's still here.
It's nice to see the kids having fun when I get home from work. Makes me feel ten years younger.
That still makes you really old, Patrizion.
Reverend Gun-Knight.
Now, now, are you angry at me for being late, Fia?
I had to turn the office upside-down, trying to find this thing.
What's in the package, Reverend?
Open it. Find out for yourself.
Wow! A guardian gun?
Looks like it's been around for a while.
Patrizion. Unless my eyes deceive me...
The first patron firearm I ever had. I picked it when I was twelve. It stayed with me for over a decade, until I became a gun-knight.
Soon, it'll become a true relic, when firearms workshops fade into the history of Laterano.
Take it, Fia.
What?
A little token. Think of it as fulfilling an old man's wish.
As your mentor, your guardian, some of what I did was... how should I put it...? Regrettable.
You know you weren't the only student I cared for. But I was never a licensed teacher. The other one should have walked a better path.
Aurela, the gun-knight who killed her cohort? That's a whole different thing.
If I never walk that 'better path', it's not because of anything so drastic.
It's because you don't have the little whatsis I have over my head. I know.
But I took you in precisely because you were Liberi.
You never told me that.
What regret are you trying to make up for this time?
*sigh*... I didn't want to tell you.
The loss of a friendship made me realize that I never truly knew a Liberi.
I thought I was too clumsy. I thought I could learn to get along with the Liberi.
But I was never very good at it.
I worried about your nerves that were constantly on edge, about your obsession over the accident, the anger that boiled within you, and how you struggled to fit in...
But I never knew how to guide you.
For the Sankta, empathy is a window that helps us understand each other. For you, it became a wall that kept you out.
Hold it, hold it right there—
Do old people always get so sentimental?
Haha. You don't get it.
What I'm trying to tell you is that everything is going to change after the Summit of Nations.
The prohibition on patron firearms for non-Sankta will be lifted, along with other rules like it. The Law's acceptance of the Liberi will extend far beyond just allowing you to live in the Holy City.
We will truly become one people, Fia.
Do you think it's enough to just issue a few edicts, close a few workshops, build a shrine, and take in some wanderers?
See? That's your first reaction.
It means that, deep down, you want to share true understanding with Lemuen, with Mostima... with us.
You will understand, Fia.
Then, if you're willing, you can take my place and become a true gun-knight... or go and do whatever else you want to, things that were once open only to the Sankta.
When that day comes, your friends will be there to bear witness, as they do now.
Now, take your guardian gun for a spin.
Fiammetta does not fully understand what the old man said, and her two friends say nothing, but everyone present smiles at her.
Fiammetta raises the old patron firearm.
She has held many grenade launchers in her time, but she feels a strange sensation come over her as she touches the gun's barrel, like a terrestrial creature touching the sea for the first time.
She cannot tell if the indescribable connection among Sanktas has opened itself to her, or if it is simply a reaction to Patrizion's words.
We were born different, Fia, but our paths must eventually converge.
Nothing makes an old man happier than recognition from his child.
Fiammetta holds the gun tightly and lines up the sights with the door.
Fiammetta... it's been a while.
Did you invite him, Lemuen?
I knew he was coming, that's all.
It was I who invited him.
Letting go of hatred is also part of achieving true understanding.
Is there anything I can help with, El? To make up for me not recognizing you.
Not so fast.
There's a few things about Laterano I'm really interested in... it's been a long time since I've been here.
Oh?
When did the Curia decide to shut down every firearms workshop in the city?
It was recent... the edict was sent to all the workshops in the city on the same day.
It wasn't that much of a surprise... arms control had been around for as long as I could remember, and it was about time they turned their attention to reforming gun control, after all these years.
Don't you find it strange?
Should I?
Magister Paganini said there was nothing unusual about shops shutting down and opening up. He'd been talking about closing up for years.
But this is a firearms workshop!
D-Does that make a difference?
This is where all Sanktas meet their guardian guns!
Have all of you forgotten the meaning a Sankta's patron firearm carries?
Well...
El, what are you doing?
Exusiai takes out a patron firearm and puts it on the workbench.
She smoothly disassembles it, from the magazine to the barrel to the action to the case ejector...
I'm not a terrorist, or some kind of super-mercenary. I just know my guns like I know my own joints.
I remember how I came into possession of each and every gun—yes, ALL of them.
But the one that left the deepest impression on me was the first one, when I stepped into this little shop, along with my parents and my sister...
Exusiai's hands do not slow down even as she continues talking.
My sister told me that every Sankta has their very own patron firearm, or guardian gun, the same as we were born with our unique halos.
So, picking a guardian gun is like making a friend.
We place our trust in empathy, open our hearts to one another, and learn to get along.
You were what, four or five years-old at the time? You were tiny, didn't have a halo, and you couldn't even talk. We didn't even notice you.
By the time we knew you were there, you had your hands on the scope I had picked, feeling it with a face full of curiosity...
Well...
Magister Paganini said he found me in the gardens of an Ecclesia Requietum, and brought me back to this workshop.
I seem to have, er, problems with speech, and he thought I was mute at first. It took a few years for me to be able to put a few syllables together.
I caused him so many headaches. I was never the kind of kid he could be proud of. I sneaked out that day, without even knowing why, and I was so anxious I almost cried...
Put your hand on it, Vani, just like old times.
Huh?
Hesitantly, tentatively, Vannini puts her hand on it.
Master Pag put my hand-picked parts together. My mind filled with thoughts when I held it in my hand...
Protect my parents and sister, make new friends, have the coolest graduation ceremony in Laterano... it told me I could do all that.
You felt it, didn't you?
The stuff you just said wasn't what I remembered at all, Vani.
I remember that I picked my guardian gun with a little girl I didn't know. We watched the fowlbeasts across the street, used the barrel as a talk tube...
She didn't talk, but I could feel what was in her heart when we played together with the parts. That's why I knew she was happy when I saw her reddened eyes at the end.
This is our secret. That's why I didn't say anything to my sis when she asked if I was bullying you.
For me, that wasn't the day I made one friend. It was the day I made two.
Exusiai brandishes her gun, which she has already reassembled, and smiles at the young apprentice gunsmith.
Vannini has her eyes fixed on Exusiai, that sense of confidence and pride that was on her face throughout the process.
Vannini cannot tell where the confidence and pride came from. It is almost like Exusiai is saying, 'I'm a Sankta, and this is a Sankta thing.'
What is it, Vani?
Well, I basically recreated what happened... you still don't remember anything?
(Shakes head)
B-But I believe you, El.
Uh... huh?
I was really sad when Magister Paganini told me this workshop would have to close.
I thought it was because I've lived here for so long with him that I didn't want to leave...
I believe you about this link that you say patron firearms bring us, El... even though it means that something has happened to the Holy City that I can't comprehend.
Yeah!
How can I help?
You've been here in the firearms workshop for so long. Surely you've got a way to help me make an explosion that will shake the city?
Is Fia alright?
Mostima is with her.
You know what she's like. It's not so easy for her to forgive.
What about Andoain?
He left.
If there's anyone who has a right to be angry, it would be you, Lemuen. You were the one who suffered the most.
I can't argue with that, Reverend.
But all emotions change with time.
In the five years that I spent in that coma, Andoain served his sentence in a nomadic abbey, traveling across Terra, offering assistance to our scattered kin as his way of atoning...
Finally, he decided to come back to Laterano, reintegrating as an ordinary Nurturer.
Laterano needs him, and he deserves a second chance.
I'm glad you think that way... what was it you wanted to talk to me about?
Still Fiammetta. You know she insisted on taking firearms courses when she was in school.
She was always the first to show up for class, and the last to leave the firing range. She loved guns in a way nobody could ever doubt... even though she never learned to use one.
But when you gave her your gun and that speech, that look in her eyes...
I couldn't tell if it was doubt or disappointment, but the one thing I know is that it wasn't pure joy.
Is that right...? I thought she would be happy. At least, before Andoain showed up.
I still don't understand how she feels, after all these years...
Your announcement took me by surprise, let alone Fiammetta.
'The prohibition on patron firearms for non-Sankta will be lifted, along with other rules like it.' That part.
Yet the arms control that we've been pushing for so long has only now come to its most important stage.
You mean the two policies conflict?
What I want to ask is, have you received any further revelations?
Have greater goals been bestowed upon our arms control work?
The goal has never changed. All forms of control exist so that everyone will be able to use guns more efficiently, more widely.
The firearms workshops have a long history, but also plenty of flaws. Gunsmiths and customers both put way too much energy into them, trying to make each and every gun unique.
Once the Shrine of Gunsmithing in Pagus Michaelion starts operating for real, Laterano will be able to provide armed support to all nations and all races, when we have to.
If the goal has never changed, why do the changes of the present seem so sudden, so... out of place?
Maybe because we're not so great at imagining the future.
You're right that the Matriarch has bestowed a greater revelation upon me. All will partake of the same revelation at the Nativity.
When the time comes, it won't be just you and Fia; millions of people will gain insight into the future, both inside and outside Laterano.
You're sharp, Lemuen, and I'm sure you understand that our first priority is to make sure that the Nativity goes off without a hitch.
I will continue to hunt the criminal trying to blow things up at this important time.
Leave. Now.
I don't want to see your face.
In the end, the gathering parted on less-than-pleasant terms.
He had spent much time mulling over Patrizion's invitation before accepting it.

He was not unprepared for what might happen. But with a new dawn about to break in Laterano, he had decided to come and visit old friends, for reasons that he could not, or would not, put into clear words.
What will become of the past once the new Laterano comes?
He wanted a confirmation, nothing more.
The wind brings a cloud that casts a tiny shadow in a corner of the street. Andoain frowns.
He takes a few steps, then stops and waves over a confessional.
Automated Confessional CONFESS-47, at your service. Please insert your Signa Creditorum or citizen credit card before beginning your confession.
Sorry, I just remembered I have something else I need to do.
It's you.
Is something the matter? Were you being followed?
Was that why you waved over a confessional? To check your surroundings? Do we need to call the guards?
No, I guess I was just imagining things.
Were you looking for me?
The Matriarch has returned to the Shrine of Nurtury.
We came to take you back.
Welcome back, Cardinal Lemuen.
Here are the handover documents for five firearms workshops in Pagus Faber. This one's owner, Paganini, has signed the papers.
Good work. The Notarial Hall can take the rest.
Speaking of which, there are some exceptional circumstances with the workshop on Via Borgos...
The owner, Paganini, has an apprentice who is going to take her qualification exam. The Curia will have to provide some form of compensation now that the shop has been shut down.
Her name is Vannini, I think. I happened to pass by her when I left... she had fiery red hair and a bright smile on her face. A really sunny disposition.
Via Borgos... sounds familiar.
Look at the files on your terminal, Amos.
That description does not fit Vannini.
Oh—I...
It's alright. Maybe just a passerby. You said she was a Sankta with fiery red hair?
She had a hood up, but we were walking towards each other. I got a good look.
(Why?)
'The Ends of the Earth' lounge, Lungmen
What are you doing here, Exusiai?
Uh, what are YOU doing here?
The lounge's closed.
I just ended up here. Not sure how. An indentured employee's instincts, I suppose.
Thought I might as well clean up, so long as I'm here.
Oh, I'll clean that up!
I saw that they were gifts for me, so I opened them.
A crystal ball? This must be Bison—wait, MountainDash Logistics has already expanded into Skarphaket?
What is this whole stack of coupon vouchers? 'The more ya buy, the more ya save; the more ya save, the more ya buy'... does Croissant want me to clean out the mall?

Sora's Siracusan Tour Live Album 'A Chance Encounter'... did she forget to send a couple of tickets?
Carnevale mask. This one's got to be from Texas…
It's a little tight. Not great.
This brush…
Is it a gift from the boss, or does he want me to use it to clean his vinyls for him?
Hey, Yith, what kind of hair do you think the brush is made from?
Looks Lupo to me.
Say, why are boss and Texas still in Nuova Volsinii when the Carnevale's already over? I got back from Sargon only to find the shop totally empty! Not a soul in sight!
Texas said she was setting up Penguin Logistics operations over there. As for Emperor...
He and his friends have some other matters to attend to... matters besides recording sessions, parties, and employee training.
Gotcha. Well, I guess it's all the same if I report to you.
Oh?
Give me a sec. I'm almost done filling it out.
Filling what out?
My leave request!
I want to take a really long break, so I figured I should go through the formal procedures!
Exusiai?
You know why I took the Sargonian job, right?
Sargon... Mostima was assigned to the area as Legatus, wasn't she?
Bingo!
Mostima told me that the one who did those things to sis and her is back in Laterano, the one sis hoped would never show his face again...
She hasn't fully recovered, but she's joined the Curia. Truth or secret, the events of the past have once again brought them together...
The things that I came to Lungmen to find out.
You're going back to Laterano?
Of course! Can't very well wait for my sis to come and see me.
Texas, Sora, Bison, Croissant... even the boss, of all people, are busy right now. All good things must come to an end, as they say.
Never forget your roots—that's Penguin Logistics corporate culture, isn't it?
On display every day... but correct, in principle.
Keep these gifts safe for me. Tell my friends that I'll be back, once everything is taken care of.
Is that your ride?
What? No way! I only just decided!
Exusiai.
Uh, hi, guys... what's up?
This just arrived at the office.
Classified files from landship?
I'm exhausted.
I came all this way, only to get caught up in one strange thing after another! My head's spinning.

lines.st: 'Good work, but we need to move quicker.'
'Without a second thought, the Curia is treating the explosion as a terror attack targeting the Summit of Nations.'

lines.x: Who would have thought that a harmless confessional would be a top-notch spy?
Good work. I'll give you a pat on your speakers.

lines.st: 'Which is to say, you are a wanted fugitive now.'

lines.x: I know.
With Vani's help, though, our bombs can now snap three pillars with one blast!
There's a long way to go, but pick the right pillar, and a bomb like this can topple an entire building!

lines.st: 'The shrine is bigger than any building that exists on Terra. You must familiarize yourself with its layout.'

lines.x: I don't suppose they'll let me in if I say I'm a fervent believer who wants to pay daily respects to it.

lines.st: 'The shrine is heavily guarded. Even confessionals cannot easily enter.'

lines.x: No big deal!
If there's a problem, find a solution! If there's no problem, make a problem!
'Never take a boring order'—that's Penguin Logistics corporate culture!

lines.st: 'Your passion and perseverance are extraordinary, but you have a hard time hiding your thoughts. You should not disturb those you know before it is time, even if...'

lines.x: Even if it's my sister, right?
That only motivates me more. I'll solve this problem, if for no other reason than to see my sis sooner!
It's time to go, El.
I know, Vani.

lines.st: 'That apprentice...'

lines.x: I've only met her a couple of times, but when it comes to this, I know I can trust her.
I can already guess what you're worried about, but... well, it's hard to explain the connection between Sanktas. Anyway, no need to worry.

lines.st: 'In that case, this confession has come to a close. Have a nice day.'

lines.x: Why do comfort bells keep ringing?
It's the two nurseries up ahead... strange. There should be other Nurturers in charge of them...
—Ugh!
The giant milk bottles on each side of the corridor suddenly fall, knocking Andoain out cold.
Situation?
He's unconscious, but alive.
What kind of baby would need a milk bottle this big? It's heavy enough to give a full-grown burdenbeast a concussion.
Anyway, it's for the best. Less commotion this way.
What do we do next? Do we peek inside, or...?
This is a strange place...
You carry him. The rest of you, stay on your guard. We're getting out of this bloody place the way we came in.
Leader's waiting outside. We'll get this guy to answer our questions once he wakes up.
Gotcha—
Like a wind from nowhere, the comfort bells that hang quietly in the corridor and nurseries suddenly begin ringing.
It is not a sound to put babies to sleep; it is cacophonous, unceasing, a loud alarm.
Damn—
Alert cancelled.
Clean up the scene. Don't let the children see it. Take Nurturer Andoain to his room.
Andoain! You're awake.
What happened?
Intruders. But the guards caught them.
Looks like your hunch was right. You were being followed.
They were after me?
You're the chosen Nurturer. They must have the Nativity in their sights.
Who are the intruders?
Just some wayward souls.
Wayward... souls?
What I mean is, I don't know who they are. Our siblings will pick up the investigation.
You know better than anyone else how important the Nativity is.
Stay safe and enjoy some rest.
Were you frightened?
(Pants anxiously)
Lean against my arm.
No need to fear. It's the comfort bells. You will not hear those terrible sounds here...
No hunter's whistles, no roaring of tuskbeasts, no rumbling of Originium tides.
(Moans weakly)
I'm here. I'm always here.
I can feel how much you want to keep living with this life within you... as do I.
Soon, soon... everyone will hope for the same. There will be no more fighting, no more conflict. Everyone will join together in Paradise.
Everyone will walk together with you.
What do you mean, she just vanished without a trace?
We had just cut the wheat. I had everyone take a little break after lunch. She said she wanted to play her cello, and I-I let her...
She came to this leeward ridge, not too far away. We could hear her music all this time, so I-I didn't think too much of it.
Then, when you came to find her, she was nowhere to be seen...
What is this? Some kind of paranormal activity?
Paranormal activity... right at the door of a monastery...
It would make a nice movie... what I mean is, no need to panic.
You can still hear the music, can't you? Perhaps Arturia just went deeper into the wheat fields. Musicians can be an unpredictable lot.
Take some and go up front. I'll handle things here.
Examine the other scarecrows, Sister. You should find a device like this on their necks.
Who are you?
Executor Federico of the Notarial Hall. I came for my regular visit of the prisoner Arturia.
The young executor opens his palm. There is a paper dragonfly sitting on it, flapping its wings at a regular frequency to create pleasant music.
P-Paper dragonfly?
A form of musical Arts. The prisoner must have learned it in Leithanien.
Sh-She used a page from The Saints' Travels!
This detail is not important. What is important is I am now certain that Arturia is no longer under observation. She has escaped.
I... I surmised as much.
Arturia is an important individual, is she not?
Based on the amount of resources that the Notarial Hall has committed, from issuing a warrant to achieving her apprehension—yes.
L-Landen Monastery has only recently regained its standing with the Curia and gotten out of the red...
(And this executor caught us on the spot! There's no way we can quietly bring Arturia back now...)
I can hear you, Sister.
A-Anyway, I must report this to the Curia immediately!
There will be no need for that, Sister.
The Notarial Hall will take over from here. I was the one who apprehended Arturia; I will catch her before she can do any true harm.
There will be no need for Landen Monastery to file a report.
Th-That's not what the procedures say, is it?
I see... you never intended to report this, did you?
This is an awfully convenient coincidence. You came for this. You knew Arturia would escape.
No comment.
Arturia was just like you. It was like she had realized something, and spoke in cryptic terms...
If you won't let me report this, senior, then I must join you in your pursuit of Arturia.
Landen Monastery is very important to me. I won't let it come to harm.
The executor flicks deftly with his finger, and the paper dragonfly turns back into a creased page. The music disappears, and the fields are once again quiet.
What are you doing?
Retaining evidence. The sound it was making would have obstructed our investigation.
Your request complies with the regulations of the Executor's Handbook. I hereby approve the request to join the hunt for the fugitive. Come.
You're just as strange as Arturia, only in a much different way.
I've seen it standing there like that every day, but when you get up close...
HOW IS IT SO CRAZY TALL?!
What are these gill-like scales... where do these bone-like passages lead...?
And what's with this flowing color on the wall? It's like what you throw up after you eat blueberry pudding made with soup water... I'm getting sick just looking at it.
How do you know what you throw up after eating blueberry pudding made with soup water?
Just a little practical joke that didn't go the way I intended. Not the most pleasant of memories, but it almost brings a smile, thinking back to it now.

Back then, we could tell the difference between a practical joke and serious stuff, and we didn't build strange structures like this with true blue religious fervor. What even is it, anyway? Some kind of massive biological organ?
Hm...
This place feels familiar. I must have been here before, but I can't tell whether it used to be a bakery, a chapel, or a munitions market.
It's like this huge shrine has always been here.
It feels... weird...
Does your head hurt?
It's not that hard to understand.
Imagine your favorite patisserie, which you visit once every few days, whether it's for a party, or just because your sweet tooth is aching.
When you happen to pass by it today, you can't resist.
But when you bite into the mille-feuille you just bought, everything feels different. You wonder if they got a new patissier, then you look up and find the whole storefront is different.
It's pretty much just like that, right?
Y-Yeah... wow, I couldn't put my feelings into words like that at all.
But when did that mille-feuille become different?
No idea! It seems to have changed when I got back from Lungmen.
I saw the shrine standing there as soon as I came out of the port.
Say, how did I miss something this big from outside the city?
Shh—El—
Over there...
All patrols, form up.
Guard these halls in three-team squads. Shifts change every three hours.
We must not allow any further intrusions into the shrine, no attacks on the Nurturers.
Understood.
Since when did the Pontifica Cohors Lateran wear such... distinctive uniforms?
