As for whether they'll hurt your countrymen, I'll trust your aim.

lines.x: Gotcha! Talk about perfect timing, the Seventh Tribunal confiscated my guns.

opts: The passages on both sides are hard to break through.
We need a new escape route.

lines.x: Then through the windows we go. The noisier, the better. We draw attention, and make it easier for Vani to slip away.
You won't fall apart, will you?

opts: Hold on tight. I can't promise I won't fling you out.
I'll take the wheel, you handle our tail.

lines.x: Got it. The party's about to begin, so let's show them what a perfect pair can do!
You don't know how Exusiai manages to wield so many guns at once to suppress all the Pontifica Cohors guardsmen with a hail of bullets.
You're also unsure if falling from a window this high will pose a problem back in reality.
All you do, is take her and zoom to the sky!
Divine judgment!
Rock n' roll!
Barrage!
Apple... uh, never mind! Not the best place for that.
Phew! I'm beat—
Finally shook off the stragglers. That was tight. Since when did they react so fast...?
Sure enough, you took to the roofs, El.
Always the contrarian, ever since you were little.
S-Sis?
What's with the confessional, and that girl?
Oh, just good friends of mine.
You said all this law-breaking is about waking Laterano up?
You were never the type to talk theory or explain something away with fancy rhetoric.
By 'wake Laterano up', you mean to rouse everyone in the city awake?
...It's complicated.
Look at me, El.
Yeah?
Separated by roof ridges, Lemuen looks at her sister. The sun is unbearably bright, but she said 'look at me' so gently that Exusiai does not even blink before complying.
For a long time, Lemuen and Exusiai stand unspeaking. Silence links the two.
Go.
You're letting me walk away?
You were only just created a cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal. If they find out you let a prime suspect go, you're as good as defrocked, aren't you?
Remember when the gunsmith had me pick a serial number for my guardian gun?
Of course I remember. You didn't even mull it over—you just went straight for the date Mamma and Tata brought you home.
I told myself then, to protect you and them forever.
Hey, sis, aren't you afraid they'll say you're covering for me?
You don't get it, El.
When choosing whom or what to protect, trust comes first.
The time we spent together, the empathy and understanding between us... That's why I trust my sister, why I trust that she's making the right call.
So the way I see it, I'm not covering for you just because you're my sister. In fact, there's plenty other reasons for me to do this.
Um... why don't you come with me, sis?!
No.
Your doubts... I've been having them too.
Maybe there are some things I can still do, in my position.
I think it's this way?
Yeah, we followed them all the way here—weird. They just vanished.
They're so fast... Do confessional engines have that power?
There're too many forks here. Plus, Lemuel split ways from the confessional...
Contact the Notarial Hall and request executor support.
Also, have them scan all mobile confessionals on the intercity net to see if they can find one designated 'ELC-001'.
Roger.
Look, up ahead—
(Grinds teeth)
(Pedaling fiercely)
Phew, finally managed to catch up to you after clearing out your escape route.
I won't let the Laterans tear you apart.

opts: Thanks for the hard work.

lines.x: You know, I have to commend your sharp senses. You figured out Laterano was facing a calamity based on just a few Sankta Operators' peculiar reports.
It might have been too late, had we waited any longer to make our move.

opts: It's only been a few months since everything happened at Rhodes Island...
We have to be on guard for anything strange cropping up.
Amiya is contacting our branch office operators...
Closure is busy restructuring the new plates...
Our Elite Operators are making sure the landship is secure...
While you didn't take more than a second to decide to come with me.

lines.x: You're welcome. Though, it used to always be...
Alright, Dr. Doctor. What's next?
We were hiding for so long, but now our efforts have gone to waste!

opts: We need to make sure Exusiai can move unhindered.
There's no longer any point in hiding our tracks.

lines.x: True. The strange ritual's over, and people have already woken from the 'dream'.
I'm sure the machine is already reacting, and the Curia and Nutury are speeding things up on their respective ends.
Does it still want everyone to finish their dreams, to complete its... uh, 'experiment'?
I can't use any of my skills here, and their comms control's turned you into this!
They've cut down your engine power too. You move pretty slow now—

opts: I'm glad you're here, Mon3tr.

lines.x: Well, I couldn't find anything else to drag you around either, so!
(Grits teeth)
...Doctor!
Open your door!
There's a couple of donuts in there—*chomp*—I have to refill my energy!
(Grinds teeth)
(Running wildly, brandishing claws)
Are you holding on?

opts: I'm kind of used to it...
I'm fine. It's just like how you used to carry me around.

lines.x: Now's not the time for jokes, Doctor!
The Shrine of Nurtury incubated these monsters with halos in the 'dream', but how did they get them to invade reality?

opts: Look at the projections on the buildings.
Notice the strange colors around us?

lines.x: Wow, sharp eyes to notice all these little details while I'm dragging you at high speed!
Glad you have excellent dynamic visual acuity—
Uh oh, they're catching up! Alright, hold on tight—

opts: Ggggaaahhhh—

lines.x: So, what strange stuff were you talking about anyway?

opts: It's not the 'dream' collapsing that's affecting reality.
The 'dream' is just a facade. It's already using reality itself.

lines.x: So the chaos in Laterano wasn't caused by that machine going haywire—
It's the opposite! It dragged everyone into this collective dream just so it could maximize its synchronization efficiency, and output a pre-programmed result!
—Which it could do, if it was running at full power.
Doctor, we have to know...

opts: What it ultimately wants Laterano to become.
What exactly prompted it to make this decision.
Mon3tr, have you found the 'connection point' yet?

lines.x: I've tried various methods ever since we came to Laterano with our lead in hand and entered the 'dream', but...
(Shakes head aggressively) The machine's operating logic is too unique. I need more time.
We're already exposed in both dreamland and reality, Doctor.
The city is rejecting us like a virus.
So, our current priority is to... huff... stay alive!
Calamity is already here, you're certain.
But there's no time to express any regret as you and Mon3tr rush through illusion and reality, fleeing...
No, looking for a lead on the trail of your fate.
This trip to Laterano has been perilous, yet the bumpy ride somehow helps you shelve some of your concerns.
Unconsciously, you have already entrusted your life to her...
To Mon3tr.

opts: Thank you, Mon3tr.

lines.x: Whuh?
Is that...?
I thought I was seeing things.
Shall we go greet our mutual friend?
Keep moving, Arturia.
Are you not worried about the Doctor?
I am worried.
But it is not my top priority at the moment.
It is the first time I have seen that woman who took the Doctor away, but her appearance and combat style are familiar to me. With her, the Doctor is not in imminent danger.
Also, the Doctor has drawn away many of the monsters, and created an opportunity for us to escape. We should leave at once, and locate the Pope.
Fede, I'll help you add what you just said into your mission report for Rhodes Island.
I will write it myself.
Seems we've come up empty handed.
The room shows little sign of recent human activity. His usual household furniture, such as the rocking chair, coffee maker, and shooting target are all covered with dust...
The terminal shows no communication records or abnormal messages. There is no opportunity to produce any additional evidence for now.
Preliminary deduction: Pope Yvangelista XI has been missing for roughly two weeks.
I only met His Holiness once, and already mentally prepared myself, but to know something really has happened to him...
Arturia, explain.
You seem to have a theory as to where His Holiness has gone.
We meet again, 'Grandpa with the Lateran Candy'.
The Curia's joint trial has already concluded, with your signature on the verdict. The condemned shouldn't be having a private meeting with the Pope, though.
So, what is your decision?
So straightforward? I imagined you'd say something like 'I was just taking my post-dessert walk and accidentally bumped into you'.
As for whether I'm willing to become a so-called 'Saint', I believe we had a thorough discussion on the topic when I was at Rhodes Island—
We certainly did.
I'm quite fond of that landship myself. A pity our meeting was so short. I still remember that sunset, when you performed a not-so 'Lateran' piece...
I also remember you were hoping for me to provide more evidence.
Yes.
Do you still remember my words, child? 'That every light we know will one day be extinguished.'
Hah, it's etched into my mind. It's an interesting expression after all.
That day will soon come... of course, it is just a hunch.
The Pope points to his own halo.
It's been sending me a lot of warnings lately, but refuses to respond to any of my questions.
What does that mean?
To put it another way, our glorious Law is suddenly no longer interpretable.
I only know how to play the cello, Your Holiness.
Of course, of course. Interpreting holy scripture is the Pope's job.
Trouble has popped up at work, and I have to figure out how to solve it somehow.
So, what do you need me to do?
Stay vigilant... I'm not sure what exactly will happen, so this is the only thing I can say.
If the Holy City's lights do go out, then trust in your ideal 'understanding', your ideal 'emotion', the kind of unreserved, pure, and honest 'communication' that you seek.
An Alienus like you will then perhaps become a panacea, or a poison... but regardless, you will turn things around.
It sounds as if I have no reason to refuse you.
Though I'm not the only Alienus Laterano has.
Indeed, I was just about to contact the other Saints.
Though I pray we still have the time.
So that is why you began suspecting your surroundings so early.
Not the whole reason, Fede.
One's inner main theme may stay the same, but it's hard for me to miss those supplementary and missing segments, all blended together too seamlessly. Sister Hildegard is a good example.
What concerns me more though, is that the incident happened before His Holiness managed to find you.
The situation is too complicated. All of Laterano has fallen into an unfathomable calamity on a scale near to what we experienced in the Witch King's Pavillon.
We require support.
Fortunately, I have my cello with me.
Magister?
There is no answer.
Vannini walks around the workshop but fails to find her master. She spots a bottle of eye drops on the workbench, uncapped.
Vani! About time!
H-Huh?
A gun-knight came by earlier and took Paganini away.
Before he left, the gun-knight told me to tell you to hurry over to the Curia to assist their investigation.
Vani, are you two in some sort of trouble?
Either way, you'd better get to the Curia. The gun-knight wasn't in the best of moods.
Magister...
It must be because of her and El. But no matter, she thinks, her master should not get caught up in this.
Wait, these are...
Some bullet casings are scattered on the workbench, as if they were casually left by a careless gunsmith.
Vannini puts on her gloves and begins collecting them as she usually does. But suddenly she feels as if she recognizes these casings, like they're old friends.
The shells are smooth to the touch—even the etchings have been rubbed flat—how many times have they been handled? What kind of bullet casings would receive such care, and in whose hands?
Vannini takes off her gloves. She touches a casing, the metal still slightly warm.
Pag...
Deus meus! Lord Almighty! You scared the glasses off me!
Are you finally going to say something? You're already four, you know? Any Sankta your age should be speaking, with a bright halo over their head!
Alright, I was hallucinating. You still can't talk.
I would have left this rotten place long ago if I hadn't picked you up by chance. Just thinking about having to live in the same city as that damned Patrizion makes me sick.
How many hospitals and churches have we been to? Huh? And still, no one has any idea if you were just born like this, or if your 'Lord' has a problem with you...

It's been three years. You have no halo, and neither do I, so of course I have no clue what's going on in your head. Seems like I've gotten used to my 'little mute' here, and even I myself am growing quieter day by day.
Whatever. You haven't run off in the middle of the night, so I'll just assume you like it here.
That's enough from me, considering how responsive you are. If you ever want to communicate with me, you'll have to figure out a way yourself.
Vani, let go!
Why the sudden hugging and crying and laughing? Did I flip a switch somewhere...?
You don't usually respond to anything I say. Are you trying to make up for all these years at once?
Also, what's with the sudden light over your head? Those doctors and clerics treating you the past three years couldn't do a thing...
But you picked a gun with that Lemuel kid and now you're fine?
Gah, still can't speak, hm? Don't you Sankta sprout halos from your first word? Did your halo come out early because it got impatient?
(Grabs her master's hand and nods vigorously)
I'm no Sankta, so I don't get how you communicated with that kid through the guardian gun, or how much it mattered to you...
In any case, now I know you can understand me perfectly, and that's enough for me.
(Gripping her master's face feathers)
Okay, okay, okay! That hurts!
If you're willing to listen, I can teach you a less violent way to communicate.
Sit here—and stay! Look at these bullet casings. Here, I'll teach you—
S-Since when... am I able to talk? I shouldn't...
To little Vannini, 'understanding' and 'expression' were a pair of rusty doors blocking her from communicating.
She lived with the garrulous and eccentric Liberi for three years, unable to figure out when he was happy or angry, unable to express her own annoyance or adoration.
She never thought a Sankta with both fiery hair and personality would blast a hole into her sealed-off heart without the need for words.

Later, her master pieced small bullet casings together and, word by word, taught her a whole new language. Even after she finally learned some simple phrases, they still used the shells to communicate.

But today she no longer remembers when exactly she did finally manage to grasp the most complex of sentences, let alone how many times she nonchalantly tossed the shells left by her master into the trash.
She dares not imagine his perplexed face as he rubbed the shells they used for more than a decade, wondering why his student no longer cherishes this special language from the depths of her memory.
Through tearful eyes, she realizes once more what those casings mean—it's a warning!
No... Magister!
Oh, Vani. I just remembered something that was bothering me.
When the gun-knight took Paganini away, it didn't look like they were heading for the Curia.
I'd say they were going... toward the Shrine of Nurtury.
Cardinal Lemuen. Forgive for my tardiness, I was just sorting out the gunsmithy handover documents. There's just so much—
Why are you here?
Cardinal Aide Amos. Have a seat.
Easy now. Just passing on the Curia's newest orders.
What kind of orders require your presence—
The Curia has decided to suspend all of Cardinal Lemuen's work, and you shall serve as an acting cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal until her work review is complete.
Wh-What?
You heard me.
The Seventh Tribunal was raided by unknown forces recently, and the suspect Lemuel, who attempted to disrupt the Summit of Nations, managed to escape during the chaos.
Cardinal Lemuen made contact with the suspect, but released her without authorization.
After further investigation, it was discovered she acted similarly during the Pagus Stevonus explosion and the attack on the Shrine of Nurtury.
Cardinal Lemuen has engaged in severe violations of the Seventh Tribunal's code of conduct, as well as the Law...
But, why me?
We wouldn't have captured the suspect without the crucial evidence you provided, and perhaps the Nativity would've been sabotaged.
Amos, you've spent more than a decade in the Seventh Tribunal, correct?
Two more months, and it will be exactly fourteen years.
You're the oldest functionary here, and are familiar with the Seventh Tribunal's business. Does that not make you the most suitable candidate?
It may have been an absence of opportunity, rather than a personal lack of ability, that has prevented you from playing a more important role.
Be more confident in yourself, boy!
Reverend Patrizion, I've always felt you did not like me much...
I still remember the first time Aurela and I went to visit you. It was not you, but Fiammetta, who had just came home from school, welcomed us.
I mean, I don't like you.
Aurela was my best student, exceptionally talented in armored combat and firearms. But she had too much of a character, and an extreme way of doing things...
While you, on the opposite, were too weak... And yet, these are your own choices.
I'll just say this again, I wasn't home that day because my armor broke and needed fixing!
Besides, if we're going to make it personal between us, you're the one who's holding a deeper grudge, right? I personally expelled Aurela from Laterano, after all.
When it comes to that... I only blame myself.
You're right. I'm too weak, and have nothing to my name.
I couldn't help Aurela defuse her anger, even though I was right there with her. And so, she hurt the shackled Sarkaz in the abbey, and ultimately leveled her gun at her own people.
And after she made that terrible mistake, I...
I didn't even manage to see her one last time, Reverend Patrizion...
I... I really can't do anything...
Then think on what you can still do right now.
Now?
The Summit of Nations is still ongoing, and the Seventh Tribunal has lots of business on hand. You will be temporarily created cardinal, as the Curia has decided.
Alright.
What?
Handshake review complete. You now have access to cardinal privileges and permissions.
W-Wait, so fast? I... I still haven't...
Cardinal Amos, in the face of calamity, Laterano must strive for efficiency.
So, there will be no welcome tea and cake for you.
With the gun-knight gone, Amos pulls out a chair. He opens his terminal, and his privileges have indeed already been updated, as Patrizion said.
Everything happened so fast he needs some time to adjust... and he suddenly realizes something.
He taps on the file explorer, and scrolls all the way down, finally arriving at his target at the bottom.
He opens the document, his finger pausing on a signature confirmation page.
Aurela, I finally...
Newly created Cardinal Amos sees a smile on his face reflected on the terminal screen.
It is a little ridiculous, he thinks.
Uh... th-this is...
It's Laterano. We're finally back.
No, something's off... those strangely colored shadows... look at their shapes!
They're projecting... the Shrine of Nurtury?
What's going on? Has Laterano changed, or do we have brain damage?
Watch out! Behind you!
(Praying)
A creature with a halo? What?!
There's more and more of them! Wait, stay back, I don't need an extra halo on me—
Nice shot! They don't dodge bullets. We should be fine.
Why do you look so dazed? Let's clear a way out... what's wrong?
You're... fallen?
Our awakenings may have influenced others to wake up as well. But there are also more of these halo-sporting creatures.
Those creatures dragged the awakened into the strangely-colored projections, and then vanished together.
They did not even manage to retaliate. And the Sankta who shot at those creatures... have fallen?
Fede, I'm afraid my joke from before was no joke at all. These things are... considered Sankta, and thus our people?
How strange and chaotic their hearts sound... what should I listen for within the mess? What should I share with them?
Ah... they can't feel our emotions either, and are anxious over it. They desperately want to share theirs, but they can only resort to brute force.
Like an infant yet to grow a halo—no matter what they want to express, they can only cry and grab at their parents' sleeves... Yes, they are our people, just too immature.
Immature, but powerful.
The Law grants an infant a halo when it speaks its first word because language represents rationality's awakening.
And rationality refers to a Sankta finally gaining the capacity to be conscientious of both their people and the Law.
Why would the Law deem a pack of nonsentient creatures as 'Sankta', and also compel them to use brute force to intervene in their people's fates?
Anomalies only continue to multiply. Currently, the only individual we may cooperate with is the Doctor. Being present already, the Doctor must know something about the current situation.
Arturia, are you certain the Doctor remembers the sound of your strings?
The Doctor was almost present at every of my few performances on Rhodes Island.
A pity the Doctor was never my intended audience, but I ought to be glad the Doctor seemed to have been listening carefully every single time.
Only your first sentence contained actual information, Arturia.
Haha. Sure.
Well then, Fede. I will guide the Doctor with my cello.
A sudden notification beep interrupts the two.
Here and now, of all times...?
Federico checks his communicator.
Hello...? Can... you hear... me?
Aurela... former citizen ID... LC0073458.
A group of errari Sankta and war refugees... currently north of the Lateran City... three kilometers out...
Requesting permission to enter the city.
This is the place. Enjoy some rest.
I thought I was going to the Notarial Hall or the Basilica, to be questioned about Arturia's escape.
The gun-knight who brought you here has other business to attend to. He asked me to tell you not to worry about the Arturia case.
Long has Landen Monastery served the Holy City dutifully. There will be no excessive penalty just because a single prisoner escaped.
Phew... that's good to hear.
It was for another reason that you were brought here.
There are so many—
Would you prefer a quieter location?
No, what I mean is... why is everyone in this nursery Liberi?
Hildegard scans around the room and sees Liberi in all sorts of outfits, sitting or standing. There is little conversation, only the steady ring of the comfort bells.
Hundreds? Thousands? Too many to count. An entire city's worth of Liberi seems to be assembled here.
Since when did this nursery become so large? No, more importantly, why are all these Liberi here?
Don't tell me... you have a room full of Perros, a room full of Felines, a room full of Lupos...?
An astute observation.
We also have rooms for fallen angels and Sarkaz.
You're... awfully forthcoming.
The Shrine of Nurtury has nothing to hide.
At the conclusion of the Nativity, the Curia announced that the Sankta will share the halo with all races. The simplest method is to do it race-by-race.
Do you mean we can have halos too? We can feel each other the way Sanktas do?
As you felt the arborbeast's heartbeat at the Nativity.
Why?
Why what?
Why do we need halos?
So you brought me here, not because of Vannini, but because... I am Liberi?
Yes.
I'll get a big ol' light over my head, like yours?
Go on in, Pag.
Huh. Sounds like I don't have a choice. And it also sounds like a wonderful thing, doesn't it?
At least it'll be good for your eyes.
More than that, I think!
If I had a halo, I'd be able to tell what that metal heart of yours was feeling when you kicked Gia out of Laterano!
I know you blame me for that.
Glad to know that you know.
Maybe things would've gone better for you, if that hadn't happened.
Over centuries of Lateran history, many have tried to recreate the terrifying high-caliber revolving antique gun that the ancient tomes described... only you succeeded.
That design, which assumed the user could withstand a recoil five times that of an ordinary gun, was the work of a genius... or a madman.
Oh, are you bragging about being born to be a gun-knight? After all, you successfully tested my design before ever putting on a suit of armor.
I just thought we were born to be partners. You and I and Gia were always the best of friends, from primary school through university.
But my first official mission as a gun-knight was to exile Gia from Laterano...
After that, you gave up gunsmithing for the Apostolic Knights, opened a workshop somewhere I didn't know about, and now...
I decided to retire, alright? You and your vappa 'Shrine of Gunsmithing'. What use did you Sankta have for me, if you treat gunsmithing like making potato chips?
It's a crime to be non-Sankta, is it? Gia got exiled, I get to have a halo put on me... the Liberi aren't allowed to live in Laterano the way we are now, are we?
The thing with Gia had nothing to do with being Liberi. Even if she was Sankta, the Lateran wouldn't allow someone with Oripathy—
You don't know shit!
'Lateran order' and 'Lateran purity' again? How many times d'you think I've heard that garbage from you?
Do you really believe in it yourself? Somnias! Dormis! How could you, if you've thought even once about how Gia died out there, alone?
I admit, I did believe in it, Patrizion. Until that day...
We got hangovers together, played with guns together, cried and laughed together. No one in Laterano could clink glasses like we could.
But when I saw Gia disappear into the wilderness, and you just turned around and went back to Laterano the way you did, that was when I realized...
We never actually shared a damned thing.
The halo is not a bad thing, Sister Hildegard. It's Laterano's blessing on us.
I know.
From the earliest nomads who followed the burdenbeasts, the Liberi had always learned from the Sankta, from their ways of living, to their ways of thinking.
The Liberi long for a better life, and they long to be part of the Holy City. We understand that.
I know.
I told that history to Arturia not so long ago.
But there is only one thing the Liberi can never learn from the Sankta—the halo.
That is why certain conflicts between the Sankta and other races living in Laterano have lingered to this day.
Unable to take part in Sankta empathy, you are troubled by doubts about your faith. You suspect Laterano of being indifferent, of alienation...
To help with Landen Monastery's finances, you were away from Laterano for a long time, Sister Hildegard. That is why your may not have sufficient understanding of this situation.
You can ask your fellow Liberi...
I know.
I know how many outsiders who live here envy your empathy.
That is why we will now share it with everyone. There be no more misunderstandings or differences. We will truly understand one another, and eliminate the conflicts and barriers between us.
The Liberi will become a truly inseparable part of Laterano.
I just feel sad.
What?
I grew up in this city. I know where one can find the best desserts, which alleys have cloudbeasts that have a special taste for pumpkins, which buildings have rooftops perfect for stargazing.
At Landen Monastery, I made beer and bread with the Sankta sisters, traveled to other countries to solicit sponsors, and got completely plastered whenever we came back...
I took pictures of how their wings went through the backs of their chairs when sleeping, and they played lullabies in the chapel when I sneaked out, so that the abbot would sleep a little longer.
I remember that!
Actually, the abbot knew. He just didn't want to scold you.
I'm a Lateran. I like this place. I love it very much.
You're right that I often find myself on the outside looking in. I don't know why the Sanktas who had been crying a moment ago start laughing and singing at their cohorts' funerals.
But...
My father was a hunter. Every winter, he would go into the mountains with his friends to hunt.
He told me that even hunters who have hunted together for decades can have different ideas when they see the same quarry. They sometimes even fight over it.
Everyone is different. That has nothing to do with whether or not we have a halo.
But you tell me I won't truly become a Lateran until I have a halo.
Do you mean to say that I wasn't a true Lateran for the past twenty years?
Has my entire life been a poor imitation?
It has, Sister Hildegard, but you should not see that as a rejection of you or any other Liberi.
It is simply a necessity for the acceptance of Laterano.
I don't care about rejection or acceptance.
I just don't think there's anything wrong with my life.
And I don't see a reason why I have to accept this so-called 'blessing'.
Laterano has already made a decision, Sister Hildegard.
It was the first time I experienced a parting, Pag.
It... wasn't a good feeling.
I guess that was when my life started to go off the rails.
It's like those popcorn movies, you know. The Chainsaw Guy series, or The Bridges of Michaelion.
First Gia, then you, then my finest student. I exiled her from Laterano personally.
I worry, too, about Fia...
What, you're worried that your dear granddaughter might run away and you'll die alone?
You all had your issues. I tried to do something, but never actually helped anyone.
Hmph.
Did you really try, or are you just lying to yourself to feel better?
Maybe both.
But there's something you got wrong. I support the idea of halos for everyone, not only because I want to protect the 'order' and 'purity' of the Holy City...
You're making Laterano sound awfully racist by putting it this way.
A Laterano where everyone shares the halo will truly become one and whole. We'll be able to solve the unimaginable calamity that threatens all of Terra.
It'll also solve all the other problems we've been having, including...
Anyway, I think that's enough sharing for now.
Deo gratias! Thank God that's over.
Hearing all that garbage from you again is making me sick to my stomach.
Go on in, Pag.
And if I say no?
I don't want that light show you've got on your head. And I've got no interest in studying your honorable motives.
Paganini levels his gun at the gun-knight.
Just give it a minute.
What do you mean?
You're a genius gunsmith, Pag, but you're a Liberi.
You can't use the guns you make. They're just your calling cards.
If you're really that furious with me, give it until the ceremony, then you can put a bullet in me.
Once you have a halo, you'll be able to use it—
Paganini smashes the gun against the knight's armor. Pieces fly in all directions, and the broken gun stock makes a dull thud as it hits the ground in the distance, echoing throughout the corridor.
Go on in, Pag.
Legatus Mostima. 'Overseer' Fiammetta.
Your contribution to Laterano is much appreciated. Now, kindly come this way...
To the nurseries already filled to the brim?
The ceremony is about to begin. The Matriarch will first enlighten the Liberi who have lived in Laterano for many years. Then, the broken halos and wings of the fallen angels will be repaired.
Quickly now. I will lead the way...
Heh.
Come in.
Cardinal Lemuen...
I'm afraid you'll have to find another way to address me now.
And I was under the impression that the Curia wasn't letting anyone visit me. Isn't that right?
I put in a report.
I was only just created. It's only natural that I would seek my predecessor's advice.
Haha. You've adapted quickly, Cardinal Amos.
I thought you said you didn't really have aspirations.
Cardi... Seniora. I didn't come to gloat. Our relationship was never cloak and dagger, was it?
Sorry.
But if you came to ask about Lemuel, I've already said this many times—I have no idea where she is.
It's something else that I want to ask about.
Did you sacrifice your career only to protect your sister? Or did you truly believe what she said...?
From the Nativity, to the Summit of Nations... something doesn't seem right about all that has been happening in Laterano lately. It's like we've come down with a sort of illness.
They're the same thing to me.
Th-There's something I want you to see.
Amos turns his terminal on and hands it to Lemuen.
What's this?
Application to join the Legati. I wanted to be a Legatus, and get assigned to Bolívar.
You want to leave Laterano? Why?
My wife and I...
We had a big fight and I left. When I came back, she had gone on a mission, dealing with Sarkaz raiders attacking caravans in the wilderness near Kazdel.
She's always been like that, never listening to people. It was winter. I figured she wouldn't be back until spring, and I was thinking about doing something to make up for my leaving...
You know the rest about what happened to her. Fallen, exiled, et cetera.
It was convenient that the Seventh Tribunal was responsible for national security and foreign relations. It took a long time, but I finally found out where she went. I thought...
I understand.
What do you mean, though, when you said something didn't feel right?
The selection standards for Legati are extremely high. A man of my ability didn't really have much hope... so the application sat in my draft box for a long time, until...
You approved your own application when you were created cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal?
Y-Yes.
Reverend Patrizion said all I needed was an opportunity when he informed me of my imminent creation...
I'm sure he meant something else, but this is all I could think of.
However, the moment I approved the application, it just... vanished. No notice of rejection, no record to be found anywhere.
It just disappeared from my terminal, l-like it never existed.
There was no way the application would have gone through. I would not be allowed to resign my cardinalate and become a Legatus so quickly.
Besides, all the Legati have been recalled to Laterano. I wouldn't be able to go and see Aurela, even if I really did become one.
For me, approving the application wasn't even about making up for anything... it was just a small, insignificant comfort.
Who would want to wipe away such a small comfort?
For me... that was enough to make me question if this Laterano is real.
Maybe... your sister was right.
Have you told anyone else?
N-No. I couldn't sleep last night... you were the only one I could talk to about this.
This isn't some newfangled interrogation technique, is it, Cardinal Amos?
You can trust me, Seniora Lemuen.
We both have things that truly matter to us. Few things.
An interesting point, Senior Amos.
By the way, there's an even bigger Nativity scheduled at the Shrine of Nurtury today.
'We prepare for death all our lives.'
Even in a place like Rocamarea, where everything is washed out by tides of plague and famine, an old man may lick his parched lips and say something to the effect before his eyes close forever.
Words as old as time, as they say. To some, they are an eulogy to wishes unfulfilled; to others, an utterance free of regret. But there is a common premise between the two—
Death is the conclusion to life.
(Looks up in confusion)
(Flaps wings anxiously)
Indeed, child, your mother will answer no more.
...She has died.
(Soft chirp)
The mother has breathed her last. Andoain gently closes her eyes.
The fledgling leans over. He is trembling, even the sheen of his hide darkened. Andoain places him on his own knee and looks into his eyes.
The fledgling's voice is mumbled. Andoain does not speak the language of beasts, but he can feel sorrow, like a current running beneath the calm surface—the fledgling's and his own.
Death is accompanied by new birth. When your mother knew that her own life was beyond saving, she chose to leave the hope of life to you.
She used her last strength, and succeeded.
This is your first lesson.
(Soft chirp)
Rocamarea, the town with the clock tower, the abbey in the wilderness, and now... I've experienced many such moments, and now I have another. Each experience brings deeper insight.
All walk toward the inevitable end. That is why the continuity of meaning is so precious.
Death is a parting.
A gift from the departed to the living, the beginning of the living's own salvation.
Do not fear, child.
Lament and remember... I will do so with you.
I will stay outside and leave her funeral to you, Andoain.
What of the Matriarch?
She is indisposed, preparing for the Nativity to bestow halos upon all the non-Sankta here... she sends her apologies.
No matter.
We have grown accustomed to welcoming new life with ceremony, while funerals are always simple... Tell the Matriarch that it will be a little while before I return to duty.
Are you alright?
The mother's passing was sudden. The child is young, and unaccustomed to such sorrow. I must stay by his side.
No, it's you the Matriarch is worried for.
What do you mean?
Matriarch said she and you had been with this mother for a long time. She understands the sorrow in your heart.
You are a thinker. Every journey gives you inspiration, but also fatigue.
As for the child... he is a miracle. He has the strength to emerge from darkness.
The Matriarch wishes me to tell you that no new life in Laterano will ever succumb to calamity nor sorrow.
They will be born equal, they will be brave, and they will be strong... Laterano will see to it.
Andoain feels the fledging arborbeast push against his arm. He wants to calm him down, but the fledgling pushes his hand away and spreads his wings.
The fledgling takes flight, landing on a comfort bell in the middle of the room, making a pleasant sound.
Andoain watches in confusion. He has worn a halo for decades, yet his eyes still struggle to adjust to the fledgling's dazzling light.
(Flaps wings forcefully)
(Loud cry)
He has been with the fledgling day and night. He knows what these movements mean.
The fledgling is inviting him to play.
The Nurturer, who can never turn down a child, stands stunned.
He wants to communicate his sorrow, decline this incomprehensible invitation in the most tactful way possible.
But eventually, he steps up and takes the young arborbeast's claw, dancing a waltz in the middle of the empty room along to the comfort bells.
The arborbeast's excited cries echo throughout the room. His feathers shine bright under the halo, showing its sheen unreservedly in the spinning circles.
Their steps grow quicker, and Andoain looks toward the body of the deceased mother, trying to ascertain his own emotions. Then he realizes with a start—
He is no longer sad.
He is joyful as the fledgling, he is beautiful as the fledgling, he sings and dances for the future with a heart full of hope, just like the fledgling.
Their spinning steps draw beautiful circles on the ground and in the air.
Is... is this...
Is this what Sankta means?
Only the instinct to avoid pain...
But no right to sorrow and mourning?
Is this the Sankta? Is this the miracle of life?!
Nobody answers his hysterical call. His Matriarch is bestowing halo after halo upon the people in the Hall of Enlightenment.
He alone stops in his tracks. He recalls a funeral, one that should not exist in his memory.
A field of irises. A child. Cruel but sincere confessions and prayers. Ringing of bells.
Still no answer?
Hey, Aurela?
You don't believe me, you listen for yourself.
Ouch! The sound hurts my ears... it's just noise! I can't make out anything!
What's going on, Aurela?
You're asking me?
You're a gun-knight. Were, at least. You know more about these things than we do.
You said it yourself, I was a gun-knight.
The static is bad enough. You wanna add to the noise, and I know plenty of ways to shut you up.
Sister...
Don't be afraid, Hugo.
(Please, Aurela. The children are watching.)
(These Sanktas have come a long way after a terrible ordeal that left them with broken halos, and a heart full of fear, with no comfort in sight...)
The intercity net of Laterano has always been very stable, with dedicated personnel performing regular maintenance on communications equipment located in settlements outside the city.

This emergency communication post is only three kilometers from the Lateran City. If any one of them is still working, this would be it, but I've sent a dozen hails through the special channel with no response.
Is Laterano really done for? Hah.
Could you try a few more times, Aurela?
You look even more nervous than them.
Do I?
Nueda does not say more. A few children come over and grab the hem of her dress, staring forward with her.

The white walls of Laterano are already visible from this settlement. Impeccable as the clouds, it looks like the abode of a holy spirit who accidentally ventured into the mortal realm, a miracle that should not exist.
The pilgrims are but a stone's throw away. Dusty and tired, their broken halos and wings seem even darker in this wilderness.
The pilgrims have reached the end of their journey, but is it truly the end of their suffering?
That's it... Laterano...
Looks like the people in there can hardly even fend for themselves at this point. Get your things, we're going in.
You're right, we can't wait here forever. Let's go!
But if we enter the city without the Curia's permission...
Tell me, did we fallen sinners ever ask the Curia for permission when we decided to return?
Don't tell me you came back to repay Laterano's kindness. I'll put you first against the wall.
Stop.
You must not enter Laterano.
Look who we've got here. A Notarial Hall executor.
Are you Aurela?
You know who I am?
I looked you up in the Notarial Hall database after receiving your message.

Aurela, former citizen ID LC0073458, former captain of the Third Gun-Knights. You were tasked to escort a Lateran caravan through the northern wilderness in the winter of 1090, when you engaged a group of Sarkaz mercenaries in combat.
You completed your mission, but the nomadic abbey encountered a small-scale Catastrophe on your way back. The Sarkaz prisoners attempted an insurrection and were executed on the spot, by you.
When a fellow gun-knight attempted to record the events truthfully in a mission report, you threatened your cohort with a gun in order to hide your excessive use of force.
In the chaos that ensued, you killed your cohort, resulting in you being stripped of citizenship and exiled from Laterano for life.
And what of it, Senior Executor? Are you trying to remind me that Laterano will never forget my sins?
Look at the hundreds of people here. Some are fallen angels who were exiled like me, some are errari who left the Holy City for different reasons...
Some of them have never even seen the Holy City, growing up in a cesspit like Bolívar, yet still believing in the tenets of Laterano...
Have you not seen their halos? Are they fallen, or are they sick? They've crossed mountains and rivers to come here, for an explanation, for any comfort they can find.
And all the Lateran does is send a single executor to stop us from entering?!
More and more errari Sanktas gather, surrounding Federico, pointing their weapons at him.
You'll keep us out because we left Laterano for too long and forgot how to live like other Sanktas?
The Law punishes sinners, but what sin did I commit when all I did was live twenty honest years in Leithanien?
If the Law is that cruel, then it's not us who should be punished. It's you—you and your cohorts, tuskbeasts of the Law!
I fell because you punished an innocent man! You've never used your power for good!
Federico watches the crowd quietly.
The street lamps have lit at an inopportune time, casting eerie colors upon their faces. Fear, anxiety, anger, confusion, panic... all these emotions come together to twist their visages.
Their hands tremble as they hold their weapons. The tension is palpable in the air.
Federico is confident he can take out their leader quickly and suppress the rest by force, bringing the situation under control.
He has always done so. It has always worked.
Federico is an outstanding executor. He has been with the Notarial Hall for eight years. He knows what he is doing.
This is his third year as a saint. Other than a burnt flower and a few chance acquaintances, there is no one to guide him or instruct him.
Slowly, he moves his hand away from the gun at his belt—making sure the tense crowd sees it—while his eyes never leave them.
I did not come to repeat the judgment that Laterano once passed upon you.
I am not here as a representative of the Notarial Hall, the Curia, nor any other official Lateran organ.
The reason you must not enter Laterano is because the city is currently affected by anomalies beyond comprehension. Your presence may cause the situation to further deteriorate.
I will explain, but I need time.
Arturia? You should not leave the Holy City.
Don't worry, I met the Doctor... and I am here on the Doctor's instructions.
The Doctor's discovery confirms our hypothesis. All of Laterano is caught up in some kind of dream, but the anomalies in the dream are being reflected in reality.
A calamity has descended on the entire city.
...Like the Kargereich invasion of Zwillingstürme, after the disintegration of the Witch King's Pavillon?
Maybe. I don't fully understand it myself. But there is no doubt that millions of Laterans are being caught in a struggle between reality and illusion, and we need help.
The violators and errari...
A chance dissonance is often the beginning of an interesting variation.
I concur with the Doctor.
However, if the calamity has engulfed all of Laterano, they are likely to enter the dream the moment they enter the city.
That's why I came looking for you. And I brought my cello.
Over here, Doctor.
Squeeze in a bit, so this dessert cart's in front.
Finally, a chance to catch my breath.

opts: Thanks for going through all that.
Whose dessert cart is this?

lines.x: You must be here for the ceremony, seniora!
W-Would you like to try my ice cream?
Before Mon3tr can even refuse, the little girl gleefully shoves a cup of ice cream into her hands.
I-It's not a flavor you can get anywhere else in Laterano—
(I can take her out. Nobody would notice.)
As Mon3tr gives you this paranoid suggestion, she plops a spoonful of ice cream into her mouth without thinking about it.
Wow. That's really good...

opts: *ahem* Could you tell me your name, kid?

lines.x: It's got my answer for me, confessor.
Dadadeedeeda, I'm called Idida.
You named yourself after the dessert cart's music?
Don't be silly! I customized it to sound like my name!

opts: Did you know those Guards, Idida—
Aren't you scared of this lady, Idida—

lines.x: Wow! I've never heard a confessional speak like that before!
It's the latest model.

opts: (Sighs)
Looks like she buys that explanation.

lines.x: Were you helping cover for us just now, Idida?
Yep... Because all of Laterano needs to change!
That's what you think too, right?!
W-Was I just imagining things? You're the first person to say my sugar-free carbonated-water-flavored ice cream was tasty, seniora!
(Well, that explains why it's so refreshing.)
Mamma and Tata were right, this is a rocky road I walk... But I've finally found a little bit of success!
Mind... explaining a bit more?
'Desserts connect every Lateran'—but if you eat too much sugar, not only are you gonna get bad teeth, even your halo will start getting cavities!

Tata's a dentist. Every time he says that, people think he's just trying to get more business for his clinic.
But right now, my halo, err, kinda feels like the rotten tooth I just lost. It's making me feel... not so good...
I believe it's the Lord giving me a revelation!
So I'm gonna practice what I preach! I'll change Laterano's eating habits, and rebuild our connections with the power of sugar-free ice cream!

opts: That's a grand dream you got there, Idida.
Would you support this young citizen's vision, madam?

lines.x: Mind if we take a walk together? I'd like to hear more about this... connection between halos and tooth decay.
O-Okay!
(We should be able to avoid the guys chasing us with this kid around. You need to get in touch with Exusiai ASAP, Doctor.)

opts: I'm trying.

lines.x: Hey there, Leader!
Sounds like you were able to loosen that comms control thingy, since we're having a long-distance call and all.

opts: It's not entirely a good thing, Exusiai.
This means everything in the dream is collapsing faster.
This means the calamity in reality is getting worse.

lines.x: Ugh. Yeah, I figured as much.
These guys have nearly all the non-Sankta holed up in the Shrine of Nurtury. They want to put on an even bigger Nativity.
First time around, an arborbeast got a halo. This time, they want to give everyone halos...
I dunno how they're gonna do it, but this kind of change is a bit too quick and crazy.
The way you explained it, Leader, whatever happens in this dream will eventually become reality?
Ugh. Yeah, I figured as much.
The way you explained it, Leader, this big, ugly building really is poking up out of Laterano's streets, and it's spewing out haloed monsters to chase people around?
Sounds like reality's a lot scarier than the dream...

opts: What you said is what I saw, Exusiai.
But some people are starting to awaken.
Variables in reality are making the dream skip some steps.

lines.x: Man, this is WAY too complicated for me. Just tell me: what's the worst thing that might happen?

opts: Laterano might stop existing entirely.

lines.x: Whew, yeah, that's a REALLY big deal!
Well, no matter the situation, I can still find the jerk behind all this and free everyone from 'fake Laterano', right?

opts: We're limited to keeping you clear-headed and free to move around.
The restrictions Mon3tr and I are under haven't been lifted yet.
Mon3tr and I are still looking into other leads.

lines.x: Got it! Leave Laterano's troubles to a Lateran like me!
Oops, gotta keep my voice down. The grand Nativity's about to start, and it wasn't easy finding a chance to sneak in, heheh.

opts: Mon3tr and I will keep tearing up the streets.
Mon3tr and I will keep eyes off of you.

lines.x: Say, how did the two of us meet again?
One of Sora's releases? Texas's birthday party? That time Penguin Logistics crashed into the Lungmen office while cracking down on the Bloodhammer gang?
Oh, or was it that time I was planning on stealing a few sick old racing beasts when I ran into you on your way to buy their freedom?

opts: We definitely met in Lungmen, at least.
I don't think it was any of those.

lines.x: Must've been... what, five years ago now?
Well, here's to yet another beautiful partnership, Leader.

opts: Likewise, Exusiai.
Stay safe, Exusiai.

lines.x: Everyone is in position, Reverend Patrizion.
Next, we will coordinate with the shrine patrol to clear the surrounding streets, lock down the intersections, and ensure nobody is wandering about.
Gratias.
Every gun-knight gathered together, our ammunition allocations and fire restrictions fully lifted... There's never been so big an event in the history of our order, has there?
The Nativity is of great significance to Laterano.
The black snow on the ice of Sami, the hole in the sky above Leithanien, even the raging tides that dash against the coast, are all in alignment with the true calamity that has been prophesied.
Imagine how terrifying such a disaster would be.
When that time comes, two-hundred-odd gun-knights taking to the field would be like a child throwing stones into the sea.
I fear our holy city, which has stood tall for millennia, will fade away.
The Pontifica Cohors and the Notarial Hall are still pursuing Lemuel and her co-conspirators.
There is something else you must know... The nurturer who was guiding Mostima and Fiammetta was just attacked.
Would you like to go and check the scene, Reverend?
My post... is here.
The time has come.
All our Liberi brethren, please go to the Hall of Enlightenment.
The time has come.
Over here, please. Once the other races have been bestowed their halos, the Matriarch shall mend the broken halos and wings of the fallen angels, so that we may all regain empathy with our people.
It has begun, Matriarch.
I thank you all. You have forsaken nearly all rest since the establishment of this shrine.
There have been many mishaps since the last Nativity. We must advance our plans. This shrine's mission must be seen to completion as quickly as possible.
To this day, we have walked the same path, Matriarch. This is something we realized long ago.
Oh, yes, there was one other thing on my mind. Today's ceremony is all about bestowing halos on the non-Sankta rather than a birth, so why are we still using the name 'Nativity'?
Is there a better term for it?
The nurturers lead the peoples of various races into the Hall of Enlightenment, converging toward its center. The grand procession is like a herd of migratory beasts, yet none speak a word.
There is only the rhythmic tinkling of countless comfort bells.
As if in response, the Matriarch lifts her head toward the crowd.
Henceforth, all who are sheltered by the holy city shall share one destiny, entwined into a singular whole. Can this be called anything but the rebirth of Laterano?
The crack of shattering glass cuts through the calming chimes of the comfort bells.
A bullet from a sniper rifle. Its source is unknown, but it flies straight for the Matriarch's sightless eyes—
Blinding sunlight seeps in.
That strange couple at the door...
Looks like they're deciding where to go to dinner using a roulette wheel. Interesting idea, isn't it?
Mhm. So they're the ones...
...who wish to adopt you.
But why won't they come in and tell me that themselves...?
Oh, I get it. They don't want to put too much pressure on me.
Now why do you think that?
Because they're pretending to fiddle with their guns while actually looking our way.
Haha, nothing ever gets past you, does it? Yes, I reached out to them before they came here.
Your parents were in that caravan raid... and the Notarial Hall's been trying to arrange things with a few families for the better part of a year now, but nobody's bitten yet.
You're the strongest child I've ever met, En, but you've still got a long way to go, and we can't let a kid live all by herself.
I know, seniora.
I'm not annoyed or anything, it's just... If I'm going to accept the kindness of others, I'm going to have to be a good kid myself! It's really, really important!
Well, the long and short of it is, they wanted me to tell you that they really like you.
I also think they're really... Can I have a week, no, three days? I'll think things over.
Once I make a decision, I won't go back on it!
Is this the one that feels right for you?
Mhm.

Whoa, a sniper rifle. If it was me, I would never choose that one...
No, when it's my turn in a few years, I'll pick lots and lots and lots of guns!
Haha, sounds good.
Well, I have to remind you that this fellow's been sitting here a few years now. Never been picked. Best think it over.
Hmm... It's really quiet.
No, no, what I meant was, this sort of gun's different from the others. Truth be told, it's not very suited to you lively Sanktas.
When you're on the battlefield, you won't get many chances to fire it. You'll have to make every shot count.
Whether you're trying to protect something or destroy it, you'll need to have a sharper eye, a sharper mind, and take on more risks and costs...
Because you can pull the trigger, or let it go, but you can't afford to hesitate.
Today is Lemuen's birthday. Today, she chooses her patron firearm, giving even more meaning to this already extremely important day to the Sankta.
The gunsmith speaks with all seriousness. Her mother and father behind her and all her friends are silent. Even the ever-vivacious Lemuel keeps her mouth closed like a good girl for once.
Despite the silence, Lemuen knows they are all looking right at her.
This is the one, Master Pag. Please guide me through selecting the other parts.
Nice!
You don't need to worry about it not having the chance to fire, Master Pag. You could smack people real hard with a gun this big, and my sis is super strong!
Through the spiritual light, even the darkest stain can be cleansed. With a pious heart, even a savage monster can wade out of the mud.
—Hah—
Put that down, Captain. I've got you in my sights.
You're unstable, I can sense it. Calm down. That thing's affecting your mind. It's evoking your disappointment in Laterano.
I know you submitted your resignation before we left... But even if you're determined to return to the wilderness, right now, you're still one of us.
Think about the fried cheese we ate before we left, okay? Think about that fowlbeast hat Fiammetta got you, the soft and downy one.
My disappointment in Laterano... No, no... Far from it! He showed me how Laterano can save everyone.
None of us know if what you're seeing is the truth of reality, or illusions and lies. Mostima will be here soon. She has a way to get that thing off you.
We can start looking into its secrets afterwards. Alright?
There is no 'afterwards'! It's fading away! I must hold onto it... You know it's no illusion, Lemuen! It's certainly not lies!
You saw those people locked in stasis, outside of time, down in the underground palace. Those who do not die yet do not live.
You heard what that mad Sarkaz said to his shadow: 'The future shall have no secrets.'
So you should understand. What we've found is the key to time itself. What I saw was a revelation. A future where even the most savage of monsters can be saved...
Your Arts are running haywire, Andoain. That thing's eating away at you—
If that future exists, I must find the path that leads to it.
I won't let go! I need to know more!
Andoain keeps his grasp on the oddly-shaped staff. Light condenses into solid form, and engulfs the entire cave.
I have a lot of chances to pull the trigger, Lemuen thinks.
But amidst the chaotic, roiling waves of emotion, Lemuen finally finds the thread of rationality running through Andoain's mind.
They have broken bread with one another. They have built bonds with one another. They understood one another, entrusted their lives to one another. Their empathic link was stronger than any other.
In that final moment, Lemuen confirms her friend has not been swept away in that unfathomable chaos.
She loosens her finger on the trigger.
Dammit! Look out, Lemuen!
Haah—
Keph, Matriarch of the Shrine of Nurtury... Hit.
The rooftop wind whips wildly at the sniper's cheek. She does not blink. Her hand doesn't move in the slightest.
She hears. She sees. She thinks. She judges.
She doesn't have many chances to fire. She has to make every shot count.
She can pull the trigger, or let it go, but she can't afford to hesitate.
Before that crucial moment, she and her gun do not speak.
Lemuen the Silent.
She looks through her scope once more, double-checking what she sees in her sights. It is proof she pulled the trigger.
Compared to the ones in the vast lands, the statues oft seen in Laterano are the more incomprehensible, Cardinal.
Paradise has shaped our faith, so peculiar that it has become somewhat distorted.
The souls of the living praying for survival, as they traverse Catastrophe and suffering... such is the most basic face of faith.
No, there's never a dull statue in Laterano.
By life is faith shaped. Gun-wielding saints towering over the streets do not represent disdain for calamity and suffering. We have simply chosen to take up a different mindset when confronting them.
Through her scope, from far in the distance, Lemuen can finally see that all the statues in the shrine, their heads bowed and their hands clasped, are facing only the pious Matriarch as they pray.
Keph... If she really is a horrible monster, then it's unlikely a bullet will do any real damage.
But at the very least, it'll let the people in the shrine see what's so unnatural about her, and create an opportunity for you... El.
The rest, I leave to you!
Senior Amos?
Seniora Lemuen... my location has been compromised.
I'll come back you up right away.
N-No need.
The shrine patrol is already here, a-and the gun-knights, too... There's a lot of them!
I'll hold down the street corner. Get yourself out of the building before they surround the place.
What about you?
It's too late for me.
They've started firing. Looks like our deduction was right. This cannot be a place just for birthing babies...
Anyway, did you take care of the target?
Yes. Got her in one.
That's good, that's good, haha. At least this way, I managed to save a few people, stop a few things that needed to be stopped. Can't be useless forever now, can I?
Well then, vale. Go do what you need to do, Seniora Lemuen.
Actually, I've got a question I wanted to ask you... Funny, it's only once you can't hear it that I finally dare to ask it.
A glitch in the comms terminal must be keeping the line open. And so, the man goes on at length, chattering into the microphone.
Lemuen hesitates for a moment, then chooses to be silent.
Was I really that useless?
I never thought about it before. Even though it took nearly all I had just to become a small-time investigator for the Seventh Tribunal, and I never got a promotion during my decade or so there...
It seems us Laterans never doubt ourselves on that front. It's like we naturally have a hundred million ways to keep ourselves happy and satisfied.

I know someone who made sticks for lollipops. He'd take several types of paper tape and twist them together into rainbow-colored sticks... Made nearly no money out of it. He did it simply because he knew children loved them.
But ever since I found out Aurela had been banished from Laterano, and there was nothing I could do about it, I haven't been able to feel even a microsecond of satisfaction with myself.
'You're completely useless, Amos.' That's all I could think... Like how you can never forget the taste of the very first terrible dessert you ever ate, haha.
I thought that maybe, with a few more years, I could use my seniority to muddle my way to the Legati and go on a trip to Bolívar.
I couldn't save her in the name of Laterano. Not even I can forgive her for what she did. I just want to see her again, and apologize for that argument we had.
After so many long years of waiting, I finally got my chance, but... it was all just a dream...
A dream's fine, too.
If it's all a dream, then I don't have to worry about anything. Maybe getting shot here doesn't mean anything in reality!
When I was younger, I got fifth place at the Seventh Tribunal new joiner shooting competition, you know!
I even met Aurela that day at the range...
It's a shame we weren't able to save each other.
I'd say that, at the very least, you're a great investigator, Amos.
Oh, you were still on the line, Lemuen...
It's time to wake up.
Is the funeral complete, Andoain?
It is.
The Hall of Enlightenment is this way. You seem to be going the wrong way.
Am I?
The Nativity has begun, and the Matriarch is waiting for you. Where are you planning to go right now?
Your emotions are... rather unstable.
No. I've never felt more clear-headed. I'm not going the wrong way, either.
I ask that you accompany me to see the Matriarch, Andoain.
Looks like we arrived right on time.
Mostima, Fiammetta, you...
What, am I supposed to be like the other Liberi, pushed back into the herd by the guards, no matter how I protest?
Or should I be really looking forward to seeing if my halo gets to be a square or a circle?
Speaking of, I'm curious how they're going to mend the fallen angels' halos...
Mostima!
Sorry, got sidetracked. You owe us an explanation, Andoain.
Gathering up all the non-Sankta, forcing changes on their bodies... I thought this sort of stuff only happened in those shady Columbian tech companies full of mad scientists.
Is this what you meant when you said it was 'about the future, about a possibility for Laterano'?
Is this what the Matriarch promised you?
I have strayed from my path. That, I cannot deny.
Laterano wove a beautiful dream for us all. I was trapped too deep, and took too long to awaken.
Still talking nonsense even now?
The other guards will be here soon.
Dammit. Do you think we can get close to the Matriarch if we use him as a hostage, Mostima?
Maybe? She does trust him a lot. I'd be a little worried if we left it all up to El.
I can sense... a multitude of emotions, growing by the second. The number of people with empathy in this shrine is increasing...
The Nativity has begun. It's no use, even if you subdue the Matriarch right now.
We must go do what we still can.
I'll think of what to do about this ceremony that should not exist.
But it's likely to lead to great chaos... It should buy you the time to evacuate our people.
What's your plan?
Why do you think we'll trust you, Andoain? Seriously, a babbling lunatic who already betrayed his friend once, saving everyone?
You don't need to trust me.
Pity will not save the people. I don't have that kind of power.
All someone who has walked the wrong path of obsession can do is to save himself.
Wait, Andoain. Take this.
A bomb?

El gave it to us to draw the guards' attention.
But if you really are going to stop this ceremony that shouldn't exist, like you said...
You'll need it more than us.
Andoain walks past the pair, heading straight for the depths of the shrine.
His steps are slow, but Fiammetta makes no move to stop him.
You okay with letting him go?
He's reliable when he sticks to a handful of words instead of a full-on parable.
True, true.
The situation's too damned weird. Not like we have any better options at the moment.
There's a whole lot of people coming this way. Probably chasing Andoain.
Help buy him some breathing room. I'll get even with him after this is all over.
The shrine's very first nursery.
Once, with a heart full of hope, it was here he tended to an unfortunate mother, day after day.
Now, the child she left behind has clambered up onto a comfort bell, the sparkling droplets of water on his freshly-washed wings refracting the lamplight. Just like a dream.
The arborbeast does not mind his caretaker's prior outburst. He calls out happily as always, inviting Andoain to play with him.

Insignificant trifles and grandiose feats are but a hair's breadth from one another. You have labored for many years over this grueling journey of yours. You should not let such a meaningless difference stay your steps.
I will need you to lift your spirits, for aside from caring for this mother and child, there are more deeds that must be done.
An insignificant nurturer. A heaven-piercing shrine.
The former could grit his teeth, harden his heart and refuse the young beast's invitation, but this minor act of rebellion would do nothing to shake the foundations of the latter.
I should be doing more... I must do more!
But how do I shake the pillars of this shrine? How do I erase a miracle that has already occurred?
The Shrine of Nurtury... This towering edifice was not built in a day. It began as a simple, ordinary nursery.
Our nursery...
Did we start with the blueprints of such an imposing shrine? No. We merely wished for a safe, comfortable room for that mother...
And yet, its frenzied evolution led to what it is now.
That is how a future where all are saved can sprout from our faith.
This was the very first nursery in the Shrine of Nurtury. This was the starting point of this grandiose building.
This is where you and I placed our faith.
And so, Keph, I'll tear my faith apart.
Andoain examines the bomb Mostima gave him closely. It has been imaginatively cobbled together from all sorts of daily necessities, its face decorated with a bright splash of graffiti.
He cannot remember what the girl who made this looked like. They might have met once or twice. They might never have met at all. He can only remember Lemuen recounting her deeds countless times over.
I was never alone as I sought a way through this trail of thorns, was I?
Without the slightest hesitation, he lights the fuse.
(Cheerful chirp)
I understand that you are no longer sad and suffering, my child. But do you not sense danger, even as this room is about to explode?
Or... are you simply uninterested in this as well?
Does Laterano no longer need us to feel fear?
(Puzzled chirp)
The arborbeast flies down to the ground, rubbing against Andoain's trousers in confusion.
If we cannot feel fondness, fear or torment for all that life has to offer, how can we speak of revering it?
I should have asked you this, my child.
But it was I who brought you here. You could even say you were born in answer to my vision.
This is our penance. A lesson for the both of us.
Andoain bends over, and cradles the arborbeast into his arms.
That gunshot—
I am so very glad to hear the sound of your gun this time around, Lemuen.

Sparks bloom wildly across the nursery. A giant comfort bell clatters as it falls, its sonorous notes adding to the clamor. The explosion engulfs the room, devouring the graceful balance of light and shadow.
Was this realm a dream, or an obsession?
Once, you had a flock of brothers and sisters following behind you. They were rejected, harmed, insulted, desecrated. They named you Guide, and walked the long path with you.
Yet here in this Laterano, which you believed equal and selfless, you forgot them. And so I ask...
How should this community you long for be shaped?
How should this holy city you yearn for be rebuilt?
What path is it you walk, Andoain? To what answers does it lead?
If this is a dream, then I shall wake. If this is obsession, then I shall shatter it.
Let us only speak of redemption after that.
The flames swallow the entire nursery. The arborbeast flaps his wings furiously. There is no fear in his eyes, as though he is simply dancing.
Yet Andoain still covers his eyes out of habit.
In this final moment, Andoain hears a bell, clear as day.
Are you alright, Matriarch?
Hngh...
The ceremony has been interrupted. The entire Shrine is shaking, and that explosion just now...
Is an evacuation underway?
Y-Yes... There is a Liberi wearing a Pontifica Cohors uniform and a fallen angel leading our people to safety.
Only half of the non-Sankta have successfully acquired halos. I will ask the patrol to have them reconvene right away—

Like the cracking of bamboo, the explosion reverberates out from the depths of the shrine. The peal of a bell from parts unknown drowns out the chimes of the comfort bells. Chaos reigns as countless people flock to the exit.
The towering edifice sways as the blast wave finally reaches the Hall of Enlightenment.
Let them leave.
Forcing them to stay will only harm them irreparably.
The shrine is collapsing, Matriarch...
No. What is collapsing is not only the shrine, but also the answer we sought with it.
Gather the other nurturers. Everyone must leave at once.
I will have the guards escort you out.
The Matriarch steps down from the dais.
The wind. Amidst the chaos, she hears the sound of the wind clearly from above her head, where there should only be praying statues and spotless stained glass.
Hey hey hey, over here!
You're not getting away that easily!
The Matriarch lifts her head. She cannot see who is approaching her, but her voice has an infectious quality to it. Almost instinctively, she stretches out her arm.
I've been hanging onto this window for two hours already! If you don't get off that stage, my arms are gonna fall off!
Exusiai holds on tight to the Matriarch. The world spins, and the two of them tumble to the floor.
What they fall onto is not solid ground, but rather some odd softness. It is like falling into fog, yet also like falling into water.
Exusiai senses a great force pushing her outwards, one that is definitely not just the explosion. Could this frail-looking woman really be that strong?

She forces herself to open her eyes. She isn't sure when it happened, but the arm she is holding onto has become the jagged limbs of a beast. The Matriarch's gentle face has disappeared under the water.
They look at one another across the boundary between truth and lies. On her side of the line, the bell atop the Tower of Revelations rings, clear as can be.
I did figure you weren't as pretty as you looked early on, but my imagination never prepared me for this. Not even Ancient Forge had anything as crazy-looking as you!
Well, at least I don't have to worry about ripping your arm off!
My friends had to bust their butts to give me this chance...
So get out of there already! You have no right to hide away in a dream! You have no right to hide... in our Laterano!!
A bell tolls.
Its unending peals echo across the entire city. It rings clear and steady, guiding stray souls towards it.

Legend has it that after their long and arduous journey, the saints built the tower and used the ringing of its bell to ward off the devils. Then there rose the Lateran City, its walls of purest white standing fast against the elements, while the bell in the tower fell silent for millennia.
Now it cries out once more, a scant two years since it last did so in response to a mixed-blood Sankta girl's song.
Urgh...
*sobs* Senior Nurturer...
A-Are you hurt?
Don't move. It was hard enough getting you bandaged up.
Patia?
You were pretty much half-dead by the time we got to the Shrine of Nurtury. We only had time to get you and these kids searching for you out of there.
Don't cry, Giselle, Romya... It was all just a nightmare. We're finally awake.
A nightmare? Wounds in nightmares don't follow you to real life.
Where are the other brothers and sisters?
Keeping watch nearby. Guess you finally got your memories back. What, want to hear them call you 'Guide' again?
Sorry, Patia.
It's clear you were just trying to wake a man who had lost his way again.
Why did you abandon the Pathfinders, Andoain?
We rested in the wilds for a bit after leaving Sanctilaminium Ambrosii. According to our original plan, we would've passed the Iberian border soon after, back to the land where you came from.
We even made contact with the Inquisition, but then you disappeared, all of a sudden.
That was when the dream began for me.
What do you mean?
It's just an idea, one without any evidence behind it.
It told me to turn away, to cast aside all my plans and return to Laterano.
So you're saying you never actually met that blind lady, and you didn't go back to Laterano with her for that baby's sake. Like you were... enticed by something?
Nugae. This is starting to sound like something a crackpot charlatan would say.
I know this is hard to understand. Even I don't fully get it—
I'll say. There's way too much 'hard to understand' stuff at play here.
You, and that shrine! You wanted to plop halos on everyone's heads and turn us all into freaks who can't even decide what emotions to feel!
Whatever, you managed to wake yourself up in the end. If you hadn't made such a ruckus by blowing up the Nativity, all of Laterano would probably still be dreaming.
With the boundary between the two sides so blurred, his deeds in the dream should be taken into full account during adjudication.
Executor Federico of the Notarial Hall. I have read your file, Andoain Agathangelos.
Aga... Is that your surname, Andoain?
After the chaos you caused during the first Summit of Nations, the Curia banished you from Laterano. Now you have returned in secret, and were involved in plotting the so-called 'Nativity' ceremony.
You are partially responsible for the current turmoil troubling the city, and must accept a joint investigation by the First, Fifth, Sixth, and Seventh Tribunals.
Hey, can't you see he's hurt real bad?
Besides, I'm sure you've got more important things to worry about right now!
You need a hand, executor? Get us into the city and we'll help you fight.
And you are...?
Th-That—
What is it, Nueda?
Look over there!

lines.st: 'Getting your patron firearm means you've moved on to a new stage of life, El!'
'Yeah! Come quick, sis, we're taking a photo in front of the statue.'
'I need to go to Lungmen... I need to get a proper explanation from Mostima.'
'Let's send Lemuel off on her next adventure with a bang!'
'The passage of time will bind people together, regardless of what they think about it.'
'As long as your lives intertwine at some point, you'll have nothing to worry about.'
'Safe travels home, Exusiai.'

lines.x: She feels herself moving, but she cannot open her eyes.
Her back is cupped by something rough, while her mind sinks into a bottomless softness, where even the pain running throughout her body is dulled...
Her thoughts flow into a boundless ocean of faith.
She opens her eyes.
You've awakened, child.
You are gravely wounded. Only by accompanying me... will you stay lucid during the Transfiguration. While it was you... who caused its interruption... we will not abandon you.
Much as we once leaned on one another to traverse the devastation of Catastrophe.
The Transfiguration...?
Do you mean that dream?
I'm definitely awake, though... Why do you have a—*cough*—halo on your head? What kind of... monster... are you...?
All Sanktas have halos, child.

So that we could forget from whence we came, so that we could embrace our new lives, we named ourselves thusly. Thankfully, the passage of thousands of years has reshaped your appearances and ways of life.
Perhaps you can follow those tome-bound accounts and call me... 'Saint'.
Of course, my flesh and spirit both have long become one with the soil beneath Laterano's white walls.
What you see now is simply a snippet of code from His boundless program, a specter bearing countless memories.
As He sees it, the path of the Sankta began with the 'Saints', and so it should continue with the 'Saints', who should lead you beyond calamity and into the future.
Err... I don't get a word of what you're saying.
Where exactly are you taking me?
It is not a question of you and I, child... but of all Laterans.
Exusiai strains her neck backwards—
The monster that calls herself 'Saint' cradles Exusiai. Following close behind are Sankta, as well as haloed Liberi, Felines... and a host of creatures the likes of which she has never seen before.
It is a grand procession, meandering into the distance, its end far out of sight.
Exusiai can sense that these are no shambling husks. Their empathy has not faded. No, it is even stronger than it has ever been—
The faith of the countless masses has condensed into a vast sea. They follow behind the 'Saint', to a destination clear in their minds.
Not even The Saints' Travels wrote of a sight so bizarre.
Across millennia has He granted revelation to His chosen few, so that they may question, analyze and deduce, so that they may point Laterano in the right direction.
But it would prove far too slow a process this time.
He has never been this restless and uneasy. The master of all things has awoken. Threats nip at our heels. He must carry out His original duty.
...Because in this calculated future, there is no place for the Sankta.
There is only time for Him to directly impose what He has sensed on every one of us, to force the only solution He knows into being—
An even more cohesive community of 'Sankta'. Laterano as it should be.
Geh... Listening to all this makes my head feel like it's about to pop...
Heaven's sake, all this He Him His stuff... Who are you even talking about?
We are on our way to meet Him. The foundation of faith. The prime mover.
Even the deepest nightmare pales in comparison to cruel reality, child.
And with the collapse of the Transfiguration, I fear His actions have been noticed. There is not much time. Perhaps He and I... no, perhaps we still have time to ensure the future of the Sankta.
A march? A migration? Or perhaps a pilgrimage?
A grand procession of newborn 'Sanktas', traversing the streets of the Holy City with a vigor the Sankta have not had in centuries... How should we describe such a sight?
What do you see, Aurela?
