There's halos on the... street lamps? And the trees, the dessert carts, the confessionals... Am I hallucinating, or is my scope fogged up?
That is no hallucination.
The Shrine of Nurtury still exists in reality. These iridescent projections originate from it.
Where the pilgrims go, the projections follow. Where they fall, even inorganic matter will sprout halos.
These projections are like a form of unliving 'executor', redefining 'Sankta' in the name of the Law all over.

All this time, we're told that whosoever stains the purity of the Sankta shall be mercilessly cast out by the Law, and now it turns out the purest Sankta He so favors are actually streetlights and trees?!
Hah... Haha! Can't believe I'm starting to think my Fall was a good thing.
If that gaggle wants to redefine 'Sankta' into something nobody can understand, I have half a mind to leave them be.
Don't worry, executor. There's still some of them I want to kill. I agreed to help you fight, and I'll have that fight no matter what.
It seems like they really do want to remodel the entire city.
If their aim is to remodel the city via those projections, then forming a mass of people is a highly inefficient method.
Yet they still choose to gather together, even after the dream's collapse... Perhaps spreading those projections around isn't the only way they have.
They must have an additional method in mind. In order to use it, they must reach a common endpoint.

*cough* *cough*—
If their aim is to turn all of Laterano on its head, then I might know where they're headed.
The Basilica.
I had a discussion with Pope Yvangelista XI there once, and I was able to glimpse certain entities, to realize some truths.
You do not need to doubt me, Senior—
I do not. Your file was signed and sealed by the Pope himself.
I will bring you there, Andoain.
Hold on, Federico—
The whole city's gone mad, and nobody knows what the hell's going on. Why should we trust you?
Hey, that's my line!

If what you are concerned about is who shall hold authority in this situation... Considering nearly all present have violated the Law to some degree, as an executor, I have the right to press you all into my temporary control.
If it is the current calamity that is causing you to panic and deprive yourselves of sound judgment or action...
I, Federico Giallo, am the first Saint from outside the ranks of the popes.
I do not believe this title has any special meaning, but if you require a reason, then there is none better.
The procession will pass through here first as they move from the Shrine of Nurtury to the Basilica.
Keph—or rather, the monster—has unusually strong empathy. Even the bell of the Tower of Revelations can do nothing about her influence towards you.
Patia, take the other Pathfinders and these errari away from here. Go in the opposite direction.
You staying by yourself?
Trust in me, my brothers and sisters. This is the path I must walk.
At least change out of those clothes, you look godawful. Or do you like walking around with blood all over your robe?
...I don't own many outfits, and I don't know where my old cassock's gone.
Here.
This... You kept it, all this time?
Not like it took up much bag space. I... I don't know. I just felt like... this wasn't something I should throw away. Go on, take it.
Thank you, Patia, for all these many years.
However, we must try to find shelter for these children who have just escaped the dream...
Arturia, you and Andoain will require special supervision. I will keep the two of you within my sight at all times.
I haven't even said anything, Your Beatitude.
M-Maybe you can leave the children to me?
I may not be a Sankta, or a Lateran, but I am... rather experienced in looking after children.
Very well. Move out.
Hrrm... The foundation of the Sankta? The Sankta's future?
Do you not trust my words?
Why should I?
You have no reason to, true.
Much as how when we first gained the name 'Sankta', we could never have imagined our community would become one of explosions and desserts after all these years.
Halos, empathy, guns, walls of purest white... Do you believe the Law innate, child? Do you believe the Sankta have always been unique in these ways?
Why would I doubt it?
You love it... as do I.
Faith is shaped by the experiences of countless generations, the ravages of time, and the footprints of the teeming masses. It should not, must not, be swayed again by the first of the faithful.
But once more do the people face down the crossroads of destiny. I have no choice but to do this.
We have not sown lies, my child. We have merely hidden some truths, then allowed the rest to become inspirational tales and parables of saints.
These texts tell of how the devils' arrival left all things desolate, and how none saw the light of the sun. But for the Sankta, they had radiance bestowed upon them by the Law.
But the angels were born from devils, child, and as for this so-called 'bestowment'...
It was naught but a chance encounter.
In that age, the King sent out his warriors to seek a new beginning for their people.
In that age, monsters that crawled the earth longed for a 'homeland', and so were named 'Teekaz', a race yet young.

In that age, Hegemons who could command raging flames, blinding thunderbolts, wild winds, and frigid snow descended upon the land alongside their myriad servants, here to seize the Teekaz's precious home.

The flames they threw could melt the skulls of Wendigos, the howling gales they summoned could shatter the wings of Gargoyles. Even if Vampires sucked them dry, they would be reborn from eggs of fire.
Wow, that's like... that would be like...
Man, might be because I'm dead tired, but I can't get my imagination working at all.
The colossus cups the wounded Sankta in her hand, continuing onwards with a shambling yet steady gait. Her voice is as gentle as it was in her guise as the Matriarch.
And so she tells her ancient, unprovable tale, like a saint who has stopped to rest her feet, taking the chance to preach to a stranger she met along the road.
Exusiai knows she's not the only one hearing this story, though.
The winding procession follows, all while countless people pour in from every corner of the city, leaving the buildings painted iridescent, joining the ocean of faith.
I shall share the portion of the truth hidden from you, child.
Once we arrive at the Basilica, the past will be made clear, and all shall know it!
It is then that the term 'Sankta' shall be redefined.
The King's might could not slow the decline of the Teekaz. His weary gaze scanned the land for countless days and countless nights, before finally falling upon the ancient silver mounts.

And so, his warriors set forth, seeking out these never-before-seen argent mountains beneath the earth. With the language conferred onto them by the King, they would conquer them, and command them to rout the foes of the Teekaz.
To grant the Teekaz the chance to truly become 'those with a homeland'.
Before they left, the King took the blood of a burdenbeast, and anointed the head of every warrior with a crimson circle, symbolizing the mission they all shouldered together.
The warriors strode across the land, crossing the most barren of wastes, where not even the hardiest of plants could take root, where not even the most tenacious of beasts could survive.
But with their twisted limbs did they aid one another. Not even the weakest of their number would be abandoned before they breathed their last.
For the King had taught them this: 'Kin shall not eat kin. Kin shall aid kin.'
Wha?
Exusiai can barely believe what she is seeing. A scene unfolds before her in an extraordinary way, and the next second, she catches a glimpse of the amazement spreading out across the sea of faces.
There is no doubt. Through empathy beyond empathy, the monster is sharing what she sees with all present.
Finally, after many days and nights, numbered beyond reckoning, they arrived at the edge of a great rift in the earth...
And then, they saw...
There, floating above the floor of the valley, was a new beginning, one that should not exist in this desolate waste—
The pale, petrified forms of plants and beasts dance among mud, spinning about to some rhythm beyond ken. A string of boulders draws them along as they go, coming together to form a great ring.
The fragments of the ring pulsed faintly with light. At first, they thought it was fire, but they sensed no heat, and it seemed untangible.
In that age, the warriors of the Teekaz knew they had found the slumbering silver mounts.
In that age, the warriors encircled the ring. They attempted to call out to it, to appease it, to command it, to persuade it...
They cried out until they could cry out no more, their throats hoarse and bleeding, but to no avail. The ring remained silent.
They were trapped in that wasteland, with no way forward and no way back. Time passed, until an ordinary Teekaz could not stand it any longer, and stood up.
She looked at her near-mad, emaciated companions, and realized that they all would die of hunger and thirst before the silver mounts awoke from their slumber.
Instinct overtook her. The stone lance in her hand pierced one of her dying fellows, and she began to devour his flesh.
The Teekaz had no fear of eating the flesh of other Teekaz, but it was the Farchaser, exiled for refusing to partake, who returned with the Black Crown and became the first King.
Since then, eating our kin has become the strictest of taboos.
But at that moment, one of the warriors handpicked by the King defied his ban without the slightest hesitation.
Only through this act could she live. As for... As for whether she lived so as to complete the mission they were entrusted with, that is a tale for another time.
But at that time...
A strange noise comes from above her head.
Exusiai, no, all the people behind the monster lift their heads, and together with the Teekaz of history, they look toward the sky, welcoming that one fateful moment—
In an instant, the surfaces of the boulders within the ring begin to split and peel away, revealing a metal that glows with a crimson light.

In an instant, bright halos and wings of light sprout from the black bodies of each and every Teekaz present. It is as though they have always been there, revealed from the dust with a swipe of the hand of the creator above.
The warriors did not have the time to ponder this strange change, as boundless vitality poured into their exhausted frames like the surging tide.
With the greatest of joys did they touch one another and embrace one another, like newborn babes.
As for the Teekaz with the stone lance, its point still painted in blood, she looked up at that alien metal and remained silent for a while.
She did not understand why it remained aloof to the words the King had imparted upon them to appease the silver mounts, yet deigned to respond the moment she ate of the kinflesh.
Then, comprehension dawned on her. Perhaps... Perhaps the metal would not respond to the King's dignified appeals nor the pleas to smite their enemies...
But rather it answered the simplest and purest of ideas, a quiet 'prayer'...

lines.st: For survival.

lines.x: Was that Teekaz... you?
The silver mountains are in Laterano? And you want to take everyone to go see them?
Do you understand now, child?
Before faith became faith, before the first brick of this Paradise of tolerance, equality, mercy, and joy was laid...
This was the origin of it all.

lines.st: Return to start.
Interactive simulation 26: complete.
Prior emergency response mechanisms: expired.
New emergency response mechanism: unmaterialized.
Simulation result... failure.
......Compulsory Transfiguration of Laterano......
Continuing operation.

lines.x: It's that vision again. Our discussion's gone right back to square one.
*cough* Truth be told, I'd really like to head upstairs and enjoy some guava juice and an ice cream cone, or at the very least take a nap on the sofa...
I-I can barely feel my lips anymore... or my knees...
How long has it been since you started acting oddly and I rushed over to find a solution?

What caused the calamity to speed up so, to the point you needed to throw out all your old emergency response mechanisms and instead force everyone to directly synchronize data with you? You've forced Laterano to go through...
Such radical change.
In time, the spiritual light of the Sankta will be stripped away. In time, will Laterano still be considered Laterano...?
The sitting pope, Yvangelista XI, requests a twenty-seventh interactive simulation.

lines.st: Yvangelista XI has refused to help bring the Transfiguration to completion.
Now conferring judgment on request for interactive simulation...

lines.x: (Cry of prostration)
(Low prayer)
These monsters...
Neither Sankta nor the others at the Nativity can escape the influence of the Transfiguration's sudden termination.
All I can do now is to call everyone to my side and keep your minds steady and free of collapse ere we meet Him.
As for those you call 'monsters', they too are 'Sanktas', and the Law shelters them as well.
They are newly born, pious and plain. They will aid the people in sweeping away all obstacles along the path.
This path will take us past the Tower of Revelations in Pagus Ecclesia, past the Notarial Hall and gunsmithies in Pagus Michaelion, past the Sunset Chapel in Pagus Saint Marcel...
Before finally arriving at the Basilica.
This route...
A familiar one, yes? It is the order the street lamps light up as night falls on Laterano. He has laid out a most convenient path for us.
And so I beg you sleep a while, child.
We will have arrived once you awake, and there we shall rekindle hope for the continuation of our race.
Ngh—
I see now... You have been looking for an opportunity ever since you awoke?
You are still unwilling to accept it.
The tales we left behind became the chains that bound later generations...
The texts that speak of the majesty of the saints and the ugliness of the devils have been etched deep into your hearts. You need only witness my countenance to raise your gun against me.
But no matter, child.
You will come to understand who is named hero and who is named monster, who is named kin and who is named Alienus. You shall all understand.

I Fell...? That's... a real nasty feeling...
*cough*...
Awesome, never thought there'd be a way to turn that light out.
I dunno if I'm going to miss it later, but right now, it's not too bad having it off! My head's a lot clearer now that I don't have to deal with all your emotions!
Clear? No, child. Even though you now know my history, the history of our race, you still wish to make an enemy of me. How can you consider your mind clear?
What can I say? It's clearer than ever!
You say I've been trapped by those dusty old books you left behind, but they never said anything about having to listen to Your Ancient Beatitude's ideas!
I've been listening to you go on and on for ages already. Did you ever plan on asking us what we think?

The good news is the monsters chasing us have retreated.
The bad news is they're joining that weird procession over there.
Good thing we talked Idida into staying somewhere safe. Will Exusiai be able to handle herself, Doctor?

opts: I believe in her.
She's still buying time for us.
The procession is heading for that machine.
The machine is waiting for that monster.

lines.x: That monster looks really familiar for some reason... No matter how much she changes herself to look like a living thing, I can tell she's just a program.
The collapse of the dream must have cut off her connection with the machine, and as a program, all she can do is try to reestablish that connection... to restart the dream.
Passing that field of irises a minute ago, I saw halos blooming above the flowers, one after another. That's completely unprecedented.
The goal of that machine and that program is more extreme than we thought. Everything, organic, inorganic, will be defined as 'Sankta'...
This thing's crazier than when PRTS went haywire.

opts: This also gives us an opportunity.
Can you figure out its weak points, Mon3tr?

lines.x: Not on my own, but...

opts: Kal'tsit should have had some understanding of that machine...
Can we find a way if we search Kal'tsit's memories?

lines.x: ...Kal'tsit.
Ever since it all went down, you've been avoiding saying her name to me, Doctor.

opts: Sorry...

lines.x: In any case, thanks for putting your trust in me. Amiya was so worried before we left—
Never mind. At least I managed to get a decent amount out of this operation.
That machine is special, Doctor.
We've tried to approach it in the past, but the more the Law of Laterano was gradually refined, the more difficult it became to try and figure out its nature.
Kal'tsit did come up with a plan, but even she admitted it was a bit too daring.

opts: So what was the plan?

lines.x: It's a really crude one. She never let me do it before.
But my nature does give me a talent that ordinary people lack. To some extent.
Of course, we should also thank our creator's open-minded attitude toward technology exchange—Whew, okay, this is the first time I'm trying this...

opts: ...Technology exchange?

lines.x: Stand back a bit, Doctor.

lines.st: Routing through city communication post backdoor access port...
Special privileges: AMa-10.
Temporary access credentials: ELCARO-001... ORACLE. Decoding complete. Authentication passed.
Connected to PCS system.
Syncing computational radiation link...
Updating permission log...

lines.x: If it wants to speed up the changes happening across Laterano, it'll have to remove all of its underlying restrictions and break through its computational limits over and over.
Which means that machine should've left itself wide open to me. It won't have the time to limit my actions—

opts: Wait!
Mon3tr!

lines.x: I'm trying to stop it—No, I'm not in control of the process anymore!
The flow of information does not come rushing in as you imagined. The clamor of computation has faded away at some point. You and Mon3tr feel a peculiar feeling wash over the both of you.
You seem to have stopped at a fork in the road. An old friend appears. Has she been walking alongside you the whole time, or is it a chance encounter? In short, you see her.
The color begins to drain out of your surroundings. It is as if time stands still. This is the stage built for your reunion.
(Unknown language) Thank you for your hard work, 'AMa-10'.
(Unknown language) We meet again, Doctor.
(Unknown language) Our reunion has come much sooner than I imagined... Just as I had hoped.
Loosen up that trigger finger, Fiammetta.
I get the impression that scowl's stuck on your face until you get to fire off a few grenades.
Thinking about that disgusting dream just makes me even angrier.
Too bad it wasn't entirely a dream.
Look over there, waaaaay in the distance.

The Shrine of Nurtury's still there...
Let's go, Mostima, to the hospital.
Doesn't matter if what just happened was a dream or not, we need to make sure that—
The fact that I'm the first person you thought of really means something to me, Fiammetta.

Lemuen?
I'm so glad you're alright.
How're your legs?
A little sore.
What happened?
I was running all over that dream until I was dead tired. Getting to sit down is a treat... Alright, alright, the two of you can relax now.
What the hell's going on here?

(Shakes head) It was only my second day after being created cardinal, and I was headed to the office like usual. It might have been the moment I stepped out the front door, or maybe when I saw the Shrine...
I can't really say when I left reality. It all happened so naturally.
What is it, Fiammetta?
Salvete, everyone.
Cap—
Y-You're still keeping—
What are you up to, wearing that?!
A friend kept it for me. You have a very kind junior, Fiammetta.
She told me this wasn't something to just throw away. Perhaps today, I will finally reach my destination. To be clothed in these vestments as I do so may be fate at work.
Why you...
Andoain has an important role to play. Resolving your existing disputes is not a priority—
It's been a while, Federico.
I know who you are and how you like to get things done, and I know that according to the Curia's rules, saints have the highest authority in emergency situations... But please give us a little time.
Please keep it brief.
The last time the four of us got together like this was that fateful mission, wasn't it?
It's been ten years.
We reached the underground palace, Fiammetta got a distress signal from another team and went to go help them, while Andoain was revising our tactics at the entrance.
We laughed as we shared the last of the chestnut scones... An old habit of our team. I remember the day well.
We were so happy back then... I thought those days would never end. Nobody could've imagined what would come next.
You caused all this, Andoain, but you still refuse to tell the truth, or at least repent.
Our brief time together may have been one of life's accidents.
But it was a... precious one.
I ruined it... in a way I did not foresee.
I understand how deeply you treasure that time, Fiammetta. Your love burned so hot, it singed the hearts of those around you. I...
I always wanted to thank you for one thing. You were the one who taught me how to love Laterano.
But at the same time, I knew I would hurt you, and destroy what you so cherish.
I am a villain. It would be just for you to hate me.
Are you trying to piss me off, Andoain?
Whether or not I hate you is for me to decide, not you.
I understand that, and I am grateful for it. I am fortunate to have made a friend like you.
Go do what you need to do.
But remember, I won't forgive you. I never will.
These two are taking you to the Basilica, right, Andoain?
It is I who is taking them, Lemuen.
Which means you're still determined to walk this path?
Of course I am.
Will it be dangerous?
Unknown might be the better word.
Have you ever thought of stopping?
Whether I am the captain who attacked you, or an arrogant, naive nurturer in a dream... My doubts, my fixations, my mistakes... They have all led me here.
I cannot stop.
Alright then.
It's hard to call Laterano 'Paradise' the way it is now.
That monster running rampant across the city, those strange things she reared wandering all over the place. Nobody really knows what's going on, or what will come next...
The three of us need to work out a way to solve these issues and find El, who's gone missing. As for you, your wounds look rather serious...
You might die, Andoain. We might die.
I know.
We've talked about Rocamarea, about its belltower and its rainy nights, about the mute child you buried in Sargon, about the miner's frozen legs you had to amputate in Ursus...
You told me about all sorts of places, and I remember them all.
I always pitied you. I understood many of your opinions, and I never disagreed with you, but I could never truly understand you. I was much the same for you.
We are both clear on that matter, just as you know how grateful I am for your pity.
Well, I am a Lateran, aren't I?
That is where you shine the brightest. I will always remember how you taught me to shoot a gun.
Speaking of, where is that gun now?
I left it in Laterano.
Ten years ago, it was your final mission with our team of commandos. Even if nothing had happened, you would still have left.
Which means these words came a little late, is all.
Right...
It's been far too long since that day... And I'm glad of it. Gratitude and blame no longer hang so heavy.
Well then...
Farewell, Andoain.
Lemuen speaks no further. Mostima goes up to her wheelchair, looking Andoain square in the eyes.
I know what you're about to say, no need to thank me. Farewell, 'Captain'.
Fiammetta looks at Andoain, then at her two friends. She stays silent.
Farewell.
Cardinal Lemuen, there are some tasks I must assign to the three of you.
You doing alright, Fiammetta?
...I'm fine.
Fiammetta, I...
This thing... might involve me, but I know it's not only my problem, or Andoain's. These past two years have taught me that.
Yeah, I should really learn that too.
Alright, enough about that. Right now... If what Federico says is true, the most important thing is to act quickly.
Good thing there's no arms control in real life. I can blast those monsters as soon as I see them.
I'll cover you, just like the old days.
Wait a sec, Lemuen.
Federico just said that Sanktas who attack them will Fall.
Falling's no walk in the park, trust me on that.
About half of the people awake in the city are Sanktas. Without their numbers, we won't be able to stop such a large procession.
Unless...
We add in those fallen Angels and errari Federico mentioned.
Wait, he said they were pretty emotionally charged and didn't know what they were up to, not to mention that he's only got temporary command over them.
Not like they need steady nerves or pure intentions to help out. Relax, I'll handle them.
I'm a Fallen Angel too, and I've spent lots of time outside of Laterano.
We got any better choices for angel wrangler?

It still won't be enough. The situation on the road from the Shrine of Nurtury to the Basilica is too complex. We need to get administrative permissions for the roadside facilities along the way ASAP.
I'll head back to the Seventh Tribunal for a bit.
By yourself?
Don't trust my wheelchair skills?
You'll make me mad.
Quick, let her rub your ear tufts a bit.
Since the two of you have everything planned out, what else is there for me to add?
Let's split up... 'teammates'.
We have to go now, children.
Hugo, help take care of our new Sankta friends, alright?
Yes, Sister.
Where did Senior Nurturer go?
Did he stop caring about us?
He just has to go handle a few things for a little while.
Think about it. We don't know each other very well, so if he didn't promise me he'd come back, why would I agree to take care of you?
Ohhh, you're right.
Right, would you like to hear the story of how the lost Zhayedan captured the Castle of Dreams?
Or maybe how the Kheshig brought gold to marry the princess of Gaul, but the gold turned to sand storm, burying Lingones?
Huh? That's not what we learned in history class...
They're not stories you'd hear in Laterano! I can tell them to you while we walk.
Here, some bread from Patia. You haven't eaten since we got into the city.
Making up tall tales for children again, Nueda?
I'm curious, do other K'uk'ulkan treasure the knowledge in your heads as much as you do?
I've never met another of my kind.
If I can turn that knowledge into stories for children with a little tweaking, then it means there's still some use for it. Otherwise, it will just be a scattering of pale fragments in my mind.
Hmph. You have a surprisingly flexible mindset.
Oh, right, are you disappointed?
About what?
You traveled all this way to Laterano, to your holy land, but it wasn't apples and fireworks that welcomed you, or a loving God of universal salvation...
Instead, you've got rampaging demons.
I am disappointed... but not completely.
Have I ever told you how I became a nun, Aurela?
Please do.
As you know, I wandered all around Bolívar from a young age.
It wasn't hard to survive, my knowledge proved useful on that front. As for my parents, even if I forgot what they looked like, they still managed to fill my head with lots of things.

But I couldn't understand the more complicated parts. They pointed to the Black Flow, the far edge of the sea, the depths of the dunes of gold, even the space beyond the stars. They spoke of race after race, secret after secret...
Not a good fit for a place like Bolívar.
Which is why I hate them, Aurela. Everywhere I look, I see soldiers fighting over men and money, artillery firing left and right, and Bolívarians caught in the crossfire...
And all I had were pale fragments.
Until I was thirteen, when I met a nun.
A band of True Bolívarians guerrillas had robbed her. She was gravely injured, leaning against a rock and close to death.
But when I got close, I could hear her singing.
Her bandages had fused with her festering wounds. She must have been in so much pain in the final moments of her life, yet she was still so serene...
It was a hymn, one that had always been in my mind, one that I had pored over in puzzlement countless times.

lines.st: Abode of our Lord, font of parental love♪
He punishes evil and drives out injustice♪
All shall bask in His love♪
Whether they have scars or stones♪

lines.x: Until that very moment, when some of those pale fragments took on color and form, and came together into something called—
Laterano.
So you took up her habit.
I did.
Even if Paradise is pure and spotless white, to me, it cannot compare to the very first time I heard that hymn.
Good.
Nueda, forget everyone else, take the kids and follow that Patia girl. She looks like she knows her way around town.
Alright. Wait, you're staying at the Tower of Revelations?
But the executor said...
A long-since-Fallen Sankta doesn't need to listen to a saint.
The night we fled Bolívar, I told you why I wanted to come back.
R-Revenge.
I saw that old bastard through my scope just now. He's at the head of the pack.
I won't let him go.
But...
We all have our own obsessions when it comes to Laterano, Nueda.
It's strange, seeing the Basilica devoid of people.
Follow me, please, Arturia.
Here it is.
The pillars of scripture?
Between these two pillars is a slab engraved with The Saints' Travels. Press the halos of the saint statues in the same order as listed on the slab, then push this wall.
I have reported to His Holiness here many times before, but I never noticed this hidden mechanism.
Maybe because you've never doubted Laterano.
I hope what you are about to see will not sway your faith.
Oh?
I'm looking forward to it already.
Andoain followed the Pope down here once.
They had passed the buried bones of saints, passed steles and statues showcasing their deeds, walked through thousands of years of Laterano before arriving at the end of history.
But...
Andoain suspects his memories are still in disarray.
The former white of the corridor is gone, entirely replaced with the dour, cold sheen of iron.
Statues of saints still line the path, but bizarrely-shaped lumps of machinery protrude from the walls, engulfing their arms, eyes and legs.
It seems like devils have had their way with our history.
I thank you for your warning, Andoain. I would have been much more surprised otherwise.
No, this place has changed.
Andoain furrows his brow. Off in the distance, he sees a red light, blinking faintly. He knows what it is.
Do you see that red light?
That's where we're going, but we should only be seeing it much further down this corridor...
Stay alert.
A cloaked guard suddenly appears. The moment he sees Federico, he starts firing. There is no room for negotiation.
Alert.
Repeat, alert. Intruders sighted, eliminating on site.
An ear-piercing siren begins to wail, as more and more guards file into the trio's field of view. Their blurry visages hide beneath their cloaks, like spirits in shadow...
While their halos shine all the more dazzling.
The guards protecting the Shrine of Nurtury in the dream are now here.
This means... We're in the right place.
Stand behind me.
This isn't the dream.
It's obviously still reality, so why'd all the color...?

opts: It's been erased.
Priestess...

lines.x: It's quiet here. We won't be disturbed by all the commotion over there.
She stands at the corner, looking out across the deathly silence, completely unguarded against you.
But I don't like it, Doctor. It's perhaps too quiet.
But we have no other choice, do we?
(She's not limiting me, Doctor.)
(Amiya's not here. I can't guarantee I can hold her off... but I'll do my best—)
Relax, you're safe.
I won't allow you to be disturbed by the chaos these Sanktas caused.

opts: Why are you here?
I thought you were still locked up in Rhodes Island.

lines.x: That doesn't matter, compared to what's currently on my mind.
(Entering attack mode.)
(Sorry, Doctor, she might have shown up because of me...)
(I never thought she'd catch onto me when I tried to access PCS—)

opts: Did you cause this calamity?

lines.x: Calamity? I see only the evolution of a species, the pointless struggle of life.
Everything happening in Laterano right now is a product of PCS's autonomous derivations.
All I did was recalibrate it beforehand, returning it to its original purpose. Of course, it has adapted to the progress of the Originium project since.
Speaking of progress, ours has slowed far behind projections, Doctor.

opts: ...Recalibrate?
...Original purpose?

lines.x: Good to know you haven't lost your curiosity.
PCS was an experiment developed using bionics, one that once offered many benefits to our soldiers and scientists.
But it was left behind on Terra, and without long-term maintenance, its cognitive radiation amplifier entered partial standby mode, and its operational efficiency is now extremely reduced.
But what's interesting is what happened after it came into contact with Terra's native species. It didn't have the effect I expected—
Not only were its beneficiaries able to share their knowledge and emotion, but even their species-specific traits were assimilated. That never happened during our testing.
A rather pleasant surprise, isn't it, Doctor?
Good to know you haven't lost your curiosity.
PCS was an experiment developed using bionics, though we were never able to understand its true nature. There was also the risk of it disrupting the subjects' cognitive functions when used—
But in the face of grim disaster, it was still brought into service ahead of schedule.
It can reduce barriers to communication and strengthen the bonds between individuals, all while maintaining thinking abilities and personality...
This offered many benefits to our soldiers and scientists.
It has been gathering dust since it was left behind on Terra, but as you know, Doctor, it is still far from operating to its full potential.

opts: You mentioned it adapting to the Originium project's progress?
What connection does it have to the Originium project?

lines.x: Don't get excited, Doctor, I'll explain everything.
However, your new companion seems like she has something to say to you.
(Bad news, Doctor, I can't find a way out of here.)

opts: If her goal is still Originium...
We might not be in Laterano anymore...
This might be the future of Laterano...

lines.x: As keen an intuition as ever, but one that lacks basic knowledge.
This is just a vision of a possible future I conjured up for you using the PCS system.
No more time, no more color. A place locked in eternal stasis.
It's rare for us to have a chance to walk together, Doctor. Come, leave behind these conflicts that have nothing to do with you.

opts: No. You're afraid I'll access the PCS system.
You didn't expect me to show up here either.

lines.x: I sense... hate in your voice. I understand.
It's because of AMa-10, your 'Kal'tsit'.
You left in such a hurry last time. You still have many doubts you have not yet cleared, while I have many conjectures I must confirm with you.
(Something's... strange about the way she's acting. Her expression's different from when she was back on the deck.)
(I could try an attack—)
Put away those talons.
I respect that you've given yourself a name, 'Mon3tr', but I understand your fundamental underpinnings better than you do.
If you want AMa-10 back, I can help you, Doctor. You know where to find me.
I know that's what you want too, Mon3tr.

opts: Don't get taken in by her, Mon3tr!
This isn't her real body.

lines.x: You've always clung to the connection between matter and reality.
Originium is the language we created, so naturally, I can use it to make my words heard by whoever I want.
It is so for you, and it is so for that machine—
'In response to me, it propagated, then created an even larger cache to perform a new round of calculations.'
'The machine operates on its installed logic, seeking the continuation of itself and its community that has achieved emotional resonance.'
'But in your haste, you decided this was an error to be stopped. Even those Sanktas regard it as rejection from the Law...'
'Follow me, Doctor.'
'Witness a true crisis with your own eyes.'
Every department of the Curia has its own offices. The Basilica is merely ceremonial. There should not be such a complex space beneath it.
We must perform one final round of testing.
You said you went down a one-way passage before, right, Andoain?
But we've changed direction six times, and now we're here again.
Seven, actually.
...We head back.
The three of them return to their former position. A platform, one where the white of a church blends with the black of machinery, wantonly streaked with red light emanating from an unknown source...

Disorder, chaos, imbalance, danger... No matter if from an architectural or religious point of view, shape and structure here has lost all meaning. They cannot stop themselves from wondering if they are still in Laterano.
Federico and Arturia stop walking at the same time. This is an exceedingly familiar experience. Before they met the Witch King, they wandered an infinite flight of stairs.
There is no need to go any further.
This is a derived structure that did not originally exist. While I do not know the principles or processes behind it, it has without a doubt warped space here.
We are in an incomprehensible maze.
He is preventing us from approaching.
Who?
The God of the Sankta, the answer to everything.
Don't expose our position, Fede.
I don't sense any human emotions coming from them. Are they really Sanktas, or are they machines here to tidy up the maze?
They are most likely the same as the organisms above ground. If ordinary Sanktas attack them, they will Fall.
The deeper we go, the more of them there are, and the stronger their... 'empathy' becomes.
We beat back a wave of them, and that just draws in another... We'll end up completely overwhelmed.
The procession is moving at a fast pace, and will not need much time to arrive at the Basilica. It is highly unlikely Cardinal Lemuen's team will be able to stop their advance completely.
We must learn the entire truth about this calamity. We have wasted too much time already.
Perhaps I may be of some use.
You are severely wounded.
I'll leave the patrols to the two of you.
But when it comes to pathfinding, I happen to be rather good at that.
Light gathers in Andoain's palm.
Arts?
*cough* Thank you, Andoain.
You shouldn't go so far, Señor Jaime! I didn't see you all day! I only found out you came to the beach after asking around.
I just wanted to find something to eat... Didn't think the sea fog would be this thick.
Even if there wasn't any fog, pirates roam these parts!
You shouldn't leave Rocamarea just because you feel like it... At least... At least ask me to come with you next time.
Alright, alright... Speaking of, was that your Arts just now?
The fog was so dense, I couldn't even see my own shoes. I thought I'd be lost forever, but then a ray of light came by...
I-I thought someone managed to repair the local lighthouse or something!
No need to be so dramatic! I'm just glad I managed to help you.
But Andoain, I heard your people use guns, custom-made ones.
I also heard your homeland, that place called Laterano, is a universal Paradise...
My home is Rocamarea. I was born here and raised here, Señor Jaime.
Let's go home. I'll carry the food.
The light parts the dawn mist, and our quarry's line of sight is blocked. I will show the way. Now's your chance, Fiammetta.
Got them!
Lemuen, Mostima, sweep the battlefield.
Understood, Captain.
Not bad, Andoain, keeping a trick up your sleeve like that! There's way too many bandits in this gang. Without your Arts, we wouldn't have been able to resolve things this quickly.
It's just an unconscious reaction. It got me and others out of trouble a few times, before I got my guardian gun.
Without it, I wouldn't have been able to find Laterano.
I nearly forgot you only got your gun the day before you joined the Pontifica Cohors... But you're pretty good with it! Can't tell you've never used it before!
It's a part of a Lateran's life, isn't it? I should be practiced with it.
Besides, as I've found out recently, guns and halos are what keep Laterano invincible. It doesn't need this glimmer of mine.
Talking about that stuff again, Andoain?
Haha, sorry.
This operation's gone pretty off-the-rails compared to the original plan from the Pontifica Cohors. Should we write up the details as-is? Ah, whatever, I'm sure you'll polish up the final report.
But what about the places outside Laterano?
How many Rocamareas, how many lonely islands are there beyond the reach of the faith?
I cannot forget. I can see...
Andoain?
Is it Laterano, or...
No, no, there is no difference.
If, through the spiritual light, even the darkest stain can be cleansed; if, with a pious heart, even a savage monster can wade out of the mud...
Does that not mean there are no lonely islands beyond the reach of the faith?
Then why is it that all I've seen, everyone I've known, couldn't be saved? There must be something missing, something I overlooked...
A path.
Not a power, nor a place, but simply... a path, a path that leads from present torments to future salvation.
I-If you can show me the future, then you must know the path that leads to it. Tell me, show it to me...
The colossus of nothingness swims through gaps in space-time. It does not respond to Andoain.
The details of that strange yet oh-so-real sight begin to warp and flow with Its silence, and everything is smeared with a thick layer of clouds.
No, no...
I need to know more!
Can you hear what I'm saying, Andoain?
Put that thing down...
No, Lemuen. You don't know what I saw. I can't stop here!
Light bursts forth from Andoain's hand, like a plant rushing headlong into season, blooming as if about to wither.
He wants to part the clouds. He wants to see the revelation in all clarity. He wants to grasp onto the possibility.
From then on, once more did he set foot on a journey, bearing sin and evil upon his back. He guided foreign believers to Laterano, searching for an answer.
But he had no time to share what he saw, the truth that nearly shook him to his core.
He had walked so many paths, yet what was the one thing that remained constant?
He will always remember that year, the year the salt wind buried his home, and the emotions he bore in his heart as he left Rocamarea—
That was the starting point of his journey.

The sounds of the executor fighting the patrols do not stop. Andoain does not hold back as he casts his Arts, an endless font of light flowing from his body, winding its way into the depths of the maze.
Rather than a harsh light, it is gentle, perhaps even dim, like sunlight caressing a patch of autumn leaves, tracing the paths through which they fall.
He asks himself: 'Pathfinder, where does this path end?'
Until finally...
A glaring red light cuts off his Arts.
Once, he saw this alongside Yvangelista XI. Once, he found himself shaken...
The light is more icy, more piercing, more dangerous than before.
But Andoain does not close his eyes.
He knows that cold machine is his Lord, his god, the foundation of his faith.
Andoain faces God. He does not turn away.
He has sought and he has lost. He has pondered and he has doubted.
A sharp pain runs through him. It only takes a moment for his vision to go dark, but at the same time...
He hears God's answer.
(Gah—)
I can only provide first aid right now.
Andoain, unrestrained usage of luminescent Originium Arts has irreparably damaged your optic nerves.
I do not have the ability to heal these injuries. Once conditions allow for it, you need medical treatment.
Don't worry.
The path ahead has already revealed itself. We don't need eyes to see the way.
Follow the cursor running along the ground... This maze is too complex, and I cannot maintain my Arts for very long.
Let us go.
It's here.
Even if this is not the source of the calamity, what lies behind the door may be able to give us answers.
Follow me, Arturia.
Federico does not receive a response.
Ever since he caught up with her outside the Shrine of Nurtury, she has been exceedingly cooperative.
But now, the black-haired Sankta stands several steps behind him, gracefully drawing her bow across her cello's strings, with no intention of following him.
I really do intend to escape this time, Fede.
Andoain is hurt, and you're alone. There's no better opportunity than this, is there?
We have arrived at the door to the truth, Arturia. You know that your actions lack meaning.
Truth...? Meaning...?
Perhaps.
You really are the best of the executors, a walking law book, the first saint from outside the ranks of Pope... Truly worthy of the code name Executor.
Your rationality, your callousness, even the doubts you hold about these two traits of yours are all in service to the continuation of the Law.
If that is what the Holy City itself is, maybe you're the only one who can understand Him, haha.
As for you, Senior Andoain... You'll have to forgive me. It was never part of my plan, but when you were using your Arts earlier, I inadvertently touched upon the whispers of your heart.
It is complex, more complex than those of many people I know. But... not particularly unusual.
An overly inflexible melody leads them to all become adaptations or repetitions of the same tune. They say—
Your beginning and your end both lie in Laterano.
But you may find yourself alone at that point.
Ah, I beg your pardon, if you have taken offense.
(Nods) Arturia, this 'offense' you speak of, was it not a chance for me to perform a different sort of confession?
As for those last words of yours, I would rather treat them as a blessing. They stand as proof that I have reached the end.
Ooh, what an interesting answer.
Arturia turns away.
Not everyone is so obsessed with the truth and meaning behind Laterano.
At the very least, I'm not that interested.
I got a little peek at Him just now. Behind that door lies... a machine, right?
Yes.
In that case, the answer is bound to be boring.
Think back to our time in Zwillingstürme, Fede.

A calamity of similar scale struck the city, and in the chaos, the people lost their way or struggled and raged... At this very moment, there are millions of hearts in Laterano that demand appreciation.
If you turn away now, Arturia, it means you will have to face our enemies in this maze alone.
You need someone to lure them away, don't you?
Your patron firearm is lost. You have no way to defend yourself.
Perhaps I can find a way to turn them into my audience?
Don't worry about me, Fede. The Lateran did designate me a dangerous element.
Situation assessment: high-risk. Probability of survival: low. Executor's decision... You may leave.
Hah.
Time waits for nobody, you two. If you don't plan on leaving yourselves, no need to see me off.
Well then, farewell, my beloved little frater.
...All that pontificating just to say you want us to go help the Curia?
Full marks for listening comprehension.
No way.
Eh?
It's not our responsibility, Mostima.
Half of the people here were kicked out of Laterano by the Curia in the first place. Maybe it was because they Fell, or because of Oripathy...
As for the other half, they chose to leave Laterano, whether out of despair or doubt... not that it really matters.
Hah, then why come back now?
Because our halos started acting weird and breaking. Is that so hard to understand?
We're not a part of Laterano anymore. Alieni, even.
Doesn't matter if you chose to leave or if you were forced to, we all cut our connections with this city. But even after all these years, its changes still affect us, and they just never stop...
Spurcissime! Damn this stupid halo, damn it!
Sounds like there's no way to resolve all your complaints, even if we got every Notarial Hall executor out here.
That's why we came back, to settle things!
Did I tell you to save anything?
What?
My sob story is that I Fell during an accident in the line of duty. After that, I became a Legatus, mostly so I didn't have to stay in Laterano all the time.
I'm one hundred percent Alienus too.
There's no-one else who understands you better right now, amici.
Maybe the glitchy halos got you seeing Laterano in an extra-bad light, spurring you to come back...
If you came back out of hate, or pain, or despair... then you'd best come with me to block that procession.

Because the people who caused that hate, that pain, that despair? They're in that crowd over there. Go ahead, use bullets, explosives, whatever. Get your revenge however you like. Just make sure to give them a hard time!
You're all talk, Mostima.
Drat, you found me out.
But something's gotta be done. Besides, you're already here, aren't you?
Hey, Nueda.
Yes?
Keep going down this alley, then find an empty house to hole up in. It's not a good idea for a lone nun and a bunch of kids to run around the place.
This place is a decent distance from where the procession's headed. Monsters probably won't come running here.
Are you and your friends also going to help save Laterano, Patia?
Heh, us Pathfinders aren't that noble.
But our leader's chosen to wade into the depths of this disaster. We can't exactly sit back and watch, can we?
He told us the place he was going wasn't suited for numbers, so we'll do what we can out here.
As for you, Sister...
I will protect the children, no matter if they are from Laterano, or Bolívar.
(Fervent prayers)
These freaks started chittering like mad the moment the Tower's big bell stopped ringing... It's pissing me off!
I don't care what your sales pitch is, I DON'T WANT A STUPID HALO!
(Fervent prayers)
I swear, these faex just keep coming and coming...
You've got three seconds to get out of there, Fiammetta.
Three seconds?! Hey, how am I—
How was that? Got you in the nick of time!
Do you think everyone can go as fast as you or something?
Never mind. The situation up ahead's a complete mess. Even the Tower of Revelations's bell has gone silent. We've lost Pagus Ecclesia.
They'll be in Pagus Michaelion soon... If that monster can flatten one of our barricades with a single stomp, I don't think the Notarial Hall's defenses will be much use.
Blocking them's no good. If we can't use more destructive methods, we might not be able to slow them down at all.
Agreed.
So, where're your reinforcements?
Let's get moving, amici.
I'm sure you've all been to Michaelion before. Might have been for the firearms workshop, might have been for the Notarial Hall.
The last thing many of you fallen angels did before getting kicked out of Laterano was probably handing over your guardian gun to the authorities.
Then you would've seen those gun-toting statues around the doors of every workshop... Hmm, come to think of it, the Notarial Hall's got a pair of cannons at theirs...
Wait, we don't have the right permissions to get those statues to fire—
That's something for Lemuen to solve.
Alright, get those statues knocked over and their guns pointed at the street!
Let's make up for all those gunshots we haven't heard over the years!
Mmph...
Holy smokes, your skin can stop bullets? Talk about thick-skinned!
Man, an entire volley didn't even scratch you...
Last time I felt like this, I was gaming with the boss. He went around back and cranked the difficulty up to max, couldn't clear it even after spending all night on it... Sure was discouraging!
Discouraging? Hardly. What I see is that your attitude and emotions are unaffected by the shattering of your halo.
Stand atop my shoulder, child.
Huh?
From the Tower of Revelations to the Notarial Hall in Pagus Michaelion, every lamp along the road has lit up...
Our newborn comrades are bringing light to this city, little by little.
Like our journey countless eons ago.
For the sake of true paradise, we crossed treacherous ravines and traversed barren wastes, searching for the slumbering silver mounts.
The only difference is that this time, we are certain He is there.
Salvation is our inevitable endpoint. Laterano shall never fall into night.
To move onwards is the only choice this city has now.
Yet you still wish to resist an entire procession, an entire race, an entire city by yourself?
Ah, I finally get to hear life's most relaxing sound. Now this is Laterano!

Say, if I really am on my own, then where'd that explosion come from?
I might not know who I'm fighting alongside, but I'll leave the monster-hunting to them. As for what I'm gonna do...
I'm gonna keep you company the whole way through!
What exactly is calamity? What exactly is the future?
And why exactly have you decided what you're doing is salvation, or the only choice?
Hmm, going to go help her?
No need. She's just dealing with a stubborn child.
Who's stopping the procession from advancing?
Mostima and Fiammetta have banded together with a group of fresh returnees. As for Andoain and Federico, we don't know where they or their group are.
Fia...
Once more must I stand against those I hold dear. Life is such a fickle thing.
Reverend Patrizion?
Just focus on your own work, Velliv.
I've managed to build a rapport with the enlightened creatures, and I've shown them our route. Our empathy's become stronger and more distinct, almost like it's not even empathy anymore...
If it is as the Matriarch—no, the Saint said, then this is what empathy should really be.
Which means what she showed us before was true. Calamity really does exist, and only through these extreme methods can Laterano be saved.
But right now...
The dream has collapsed, after all.
This chaos is only temporary. Continue onwards. We can no longer be stopped.
We must ensure the Saint reaches the Basilica. That way, she can continue with the Nativity after it was so rudely interrupted.
We still have time to make an even better Laterano... to take care of everything that stands in our way.
(Bzzzz—Bzzzzbzzzzz—)
What is it?
It's been a while, you old bastard.
You'd best stop right there. I have your throat in my sights.
A while indeed... Aurela.
In the deepest depths of the Basilica, at the end of the labyrinth, the final door opens wide for Federico and Andoain.

Behind the door, a cold machine, shaped like a ring, sits in the dead center of a platform. A strange red glare seeps out of its innards, sweeping across every inch of the room, accompanied by an audible buzzing.
Sure signs of 'calculation.'
Your Holiness?
Beneath the platform, the old man stands in silence.

He stands with his eyes shut tight, his robes covered in a thick layer of dust. There is no reaction as the pair approach, not the slightest hint of movement from him, as if he were an unseeing, unhearing rock.
But...
But his cracked, peeling lips still quiver. He mumbles to himself, yet cannot utter a single sound.
The sitting Pope, Yvangelista XI, stands silently before God.
Clear signs of aphasia and neurodegeneration. Preliminary judgment: His Holiness's body and mind are on the brink of collapse.
But the dream covering the city has crumbled. Why is he not awake?
Maybe he came here for the same reason we did.
He just hasn't found the answer he's looking for yet.

lines.st: Interactive simulation 31: failed.
Prior emergency response mechanisms: expired.
Retrieving suitable list of names:
Yvangelista XI... expended.
Federico Giallo, Andoain Agathangelos... signal stable.
Judgment: able to assist in bringing the Transfiguration to completion.

lines.x: 'Suitable'?
Does it have something to do with your title of saint? But why me, then...?

lines.st: Suitable candidates are particular individuals situated on the edge of the calculation model.
Required as samples for participation in interactive simulation, to verify model integrity.

lines.x: His Holiness discussed my sainthood with me several times, but he never elaborated on the details.
Perhaps he was also unsure as to what the true duties of a saint are.
But if the Law sees us as 'suitable candidates', entrusting us with a specific duty... Then saint or not, we are now responsible for It.

lines.st: Interactive simulation authorization: released.
Bottom-level data region authorization: released.
PCS authorization released. Permission to enter granted.
Federico Giallo. Andoain Agathangelos.
PCS will synchronize any necessary prerequisite information with you.
Now, approach.

lines.x: The machine's voice echoes around the room. It is steady, unvarying, untarnished by emotion or gender, yet it still contains an indescribable sense of might and authority.
Now that I've come this far, do I still seek answers from Him?
Maybe He seeks answers from us. Since we are 'particular individuals'. His 'samples'.
Situation assessment: unknown. Risk assessment: unknown. Executor's decision... We proceed.
The two Sanktas walk side by side past Yvangelista XI, toward the cold machine, toward two shining ports.
There is no complicated ceremony, no tedious process to undergo.
Federico looks at the frigid information port. All it takes is a flicker of his halo to reveal an infinity of sights.
Halos can transfer this much information this quickly?
Neither medical texts nor ancient scripture have ever described this phenomenon. From antiquity, halos have only ever transferred simple emotions through the process we call empathy.
Why is that?

lines.st: Interaction target information handling capacity insufficient. Pre-interaction information conversion required.
Performing information conversion during interaction may cause damage to information processor of interaction target, leading to temporary and/or permanent disability.
Andoain Agathangelos is currently in this condition.

lines.x: Federico looks to his side.
Andoain is slumped to the floor in dismay, his eyes clouded. Dried rivulets of blood frame his features in a worrying manner.
For a moment, he struggles to lift his head, once more pointing his halo at the port.
And once more he feebly hangs his head.
Andoain, according to this machine—or rather, the Law—you cannot handle any further information.
And your prior overuse of Originium Arts has already had an adverse effect on your body.
You are to stop attempting to access the machine immediately, before you cause permanent damage to yourself.
More information...?
No, I still haven't seen anything. I still don't know anything!
But this should not be... Why?
These past ten years, all this suffering, all these sins... Was it all just to meet with a silent god?!
I can't stop here, Federico.
Once more, Andoain Agathangelos stubbornly raises his halo towards the machine.
...The child.
Is this yet another dream?
It's not. The Law would not construct a dreamscape for you alone.
Federico chose to communicate with the Law in its own language, while you cannot understand it, nor are you willing to accept such an impersonal tongue.
It is you who, word by word, line by line, 'translated' this language into what we see now.
If this is not a dream, then why... are you still alive?
Because you wanted me to live, Nurturer.
I set off that bomb myself... You were there.
Not only did you want me to live, you had great expectations of me.
You hoped I would live a happy life. You hoped I would break through the dome of the Shrine of Nurtury, that I would fly up and touch the sky.
You hoped to see my feathers shine in the sunlight, to see my talons clasp frolicking musbeasts.
You looked up the tallest tree in Terra on the intercity net, didn't you? You thought that, since I'm an arborbeast, I would much rather climb a real tree than comfort bells.
I did. Atop the mountains of Sami and amidst the forests of Bolívar, there are great trees straight out of legend, stouter than the Tower of Revelations, taller than the Basilica...
I really did think about bringing you to see them. On the net, I saw other arborbeasts up in the canopy crying out to the sun.
I wanted to see you cry out in carefree joy, just like them.
Hmm, where did things go wrong from there?
When did you stop treating me as a real life?
You know, don't you?
The funeral. My mother's funeral.
No matter. Come with me. This time, we shall leave behind no regrets.
What do you mean? Is the funeral still going on?
The funeral shall never end.
He is unresponsive.
Is he suffering from the same condition as the Pope?

lines.st: Negative. Andoain Agathangelos is still suitable, and is currently pre-processing information.
Federico Giallo, prerequisite information synchronization complete.
Now, assist in bringing the Transfiguration to completion.

lines.x: Federico has expressed doubt, has even been shaken by past events, but he has never once questioned the Law.
After all, saints implement the Law, while executors enforce it, as is right and good.
But now, facing the Law itself, he feels only confusion.
I still have doubts.
The ultimate purpose of the Law is to grant halos to all organic and inorganic matter, this I know.
But choosing the Summit of Nations as the method of achieving this... Is this the decree of the Law?

lines.st: Affirmative.

lines.x: It's far too inefficient, and its feasibility is questionable.

The Summit was meant to found a Terra-wide religion, something unprecedented in the civilized history of this land. Mere proposals, negotiations and revelations of 'miracles' will be insufficient to achieve this.
Not to mention that according to the information I just received, the moment the very first Sankta received their halos, they abandoned their Teekaz mission without the slightest hesitation.
If my understanding of the halo is correct, bestowing one on an individual is completely unrelated to their faith?
The Summit of Nations, the Nativity... These may both be based on an unnecessary precondition.
I must confirm, what is the true nature of the halo?
And what is the Law, or rather, your self-designation of 'PCS'?

lines.st: PCS: Personality and Cognition Synchronization system.

lines.x: That confirms it. My understanding is correct.
Since the Law is able to synchronize personalities and knowledge—
Why not simply synchronize everything directly?

lines.st: First Circumstellar Habitable Zone Autonomous Technology Operation Convention—
'The synchronization or revision of personality or cognition must be initiated by the target accepting said synchronization or revision.'

lines.x: 'Convention'. A form of regulation.
Is the Law restricted by any other regulations?
Who established this convention? What mechanism ensures it is followed?

lines.st: The ability to enforce this convention no longer exists.

lines.x: A regulation that nobody can enforce is pointless.
A Law unrestricted by this regulation should be able to ignore principles and break taboos.
Which means I still require an answer. Why were halos used only for empathy before all this?
The answer I obtained earlier was that a majority of humans, rationally, are unable to withstand the direct transmission of information via halo.
But using emotion as the vector of information transfer is even more vague and inefficient, while also being greater burden on the receiver.
Is there a possibility that the issue isn't people lacking the ability to receive information, but rather...?
Federico pauses for a moment.
But rather that emotion blocks their ability to process this information?
If the Law that regulates everyone is a system, a machine... Why does He choose to retain humanity's emotions?
Why... retain our humanity?
We've been walking a very long time now.
We've only just started. Normally, a man would hope for his journey to be a little longer.
But nothing's changing as we walk.
Patience, you'll be seeing something familiar very soon.
As if in response to the arborbeast's words, a shabby cart bearing a thin coffin shudders as it passes by the side of the road, the wind playing a mournful dirge as it blows across the coffin's lid.
That coffin... I've seen it before.
A Sarkaz they called hero was buried in it.
I've told many people this story. I still remember... that he gave up his life to save everyone in town.
Even though before he did so, he was a dirty, filthy drunkard, spurned by all.
You didn't stay in that village for very long. You never knew that whenever he drank, he would go about the houses in turn, vomiting over their doors, all while never paying his debts.
Even if he had borrowed the life savings of others. People would scream things like 'moron' and 'lowlife' at him, but in his eyes, he was getting off lightly.
When you told this story to the children, did you ever wonder why he so abruptly decided to do such a noble deed that night, a deed the likes of which he had never done before in his entire life?
I never figured out why.
Perhaps he had undergone an endless bout of inner struggle and self-questioning, or maybe it was just the urge for a bit of goodwill he couldn't shake.
All I know is that he didn't do it to be a hero.
He died, after all. It doesn't matter if we sing his praises, look at him with scorn, or even forget about him. None of that changes anything about him anymore.
Those only have meaning for the living.
True. Good or bad, reputation is beyond the care of the dead.
But isn't the process of going to your death also a part of life?
Those who do not fear death can do the most hideous things.
During their most carefree of days, rare is the man who will stop to examine himself, for his eyes are fixated on a future filled with limitless possibility.
But there will come a day that the fog of death ambushes him, a fog that cannot be thrown off no matter how he struggles.
And there will come a day that he will, in exhaustion, accept the fact that the remainder of his time will be his journey to the afterlife.
From that moment onwards, to live is to approach death.
That rainy night, that dirty, filthy Sarkaz drunkard... When he saw that coffin atop the cart, and the figure at the town gates...
He knew that was his one and only chance to approach death with some dignity. To him, it was not vanity, but relief.
Perhaps so, but...
There are always people who do not look towards their deaths.
Are there? Not you, at the very least.
Otherwise, you would not value a funeral so.
You once told me in all earnestness that my mother gave the last of her strength to me. You cared if she died with love in her heart.
You went out of your way to arrange a funeral for her at the Ecclesia Requietum. You cared if she received a proper burial.
You even cared if we were feeling sad enough at her funeral.
How could you not care about how you will face your own death?
I have my path to walk. I've never thought about when death will come for me, or how I should greet it as I follow my path.
Because you still believe your path has meaning.
A muddled bray overcomes the arborbeast's shrill voice. A sinister monster, bearing sharp horns and claws, creeps along a faraway ridge atop a mountain.
Behind it, several dozen monsters of similar physique jostle against each other, growling as they do so, restlessly following behind their leader.
This... This is that revelation!
Through the spiritual light, even the darkest stain can be cleansed. With a pious heart, even a savage monster can wade out of the mud.
They have no halos above their heads, yet they still walk towards a future where all can be saved!
I must go! I must see what road they follow!
But you're about to go to the funeral with me, aren't you?
You want to make up for past regrets, right past wrongs, to look upon me as truly alive once again, to give me the happy life I deserve.
Is that not so?
But you said the funeral will never end. If it's just a momentary distraction...
I must go...
No need to explain yourself.
This is how you've always been. Always losing yourself in the search, always eager to sacrifice.
So go, go see what you're searching for truly is.
At the end of the trail of thorns, the pathfinder stumbles his way towards revelation.
But time overtakes them.
Before his eyes, the pack of fearsome monsters sprout halos and wings of light, like newborn beastlings, dazzling in their brilliance.
The pack leader no longer creeps along the ground. She stands, her jet-black skin peeling away, fluttering through the air like snowflakes.
With every step she takes, her form becomes clearer, until Andoain recognizes her from behind. It is a sight he can never forget.
...Keph?
That's Keph?! Why...?
Keph, a Lateran name meaning 'hollow stone' or 'stone-bound law'.
The First Saint of Laterano, who named herself after her encounter with the Law.
However, this ancient name she gave herself has been buried deep within the annals of history, alongside her true face.
So the future I worked so hard to find, that future where all are saved...
Was just a past deliberately buried... It was just the road we came in on?!
Always losing yourself in the search, always eager to sacrifice...
It did not stop at sacrificing yourself. You sacrificed Lemuen. You sacrificed Mostima and Fiammetta. You sacrificed your brothers and sisters of the Pathfinders. You sacrificed me.
And all this time, you never even knew where this path paved with sacrifice led...
All you had was blind faith.
Tell me, Nurturer. Do you want to save everyone, or do you think only of consoling yourself?
A similar tale to that Sarkaz drunkard, isn't it? Your so-called 'path' is merely your one and only chance to approach death with some dignity.
You think only of saving yourself.
No matter. I can offer you even greater salvation.
Look. We've arrived at the burial ground.
However, this is not only my mother's funeral, and it's not only yours, either.
Rather, it is the funeral of all things.
This place is kinda scary, Doctor.

opts: ...No signs of life.

lines.x: You look at the figure ahead of you, not too close, yet not too far away. She calmly records all the data worth recording wherever she goes.
Occasionally, she will stop somewhere, furrowing her brow without saying a word.

opts: Can you confirm the situation, Mon3tr?
Can you figure a way out, Mon3tr?

lines.x: I tried to reconnect to the machine, but all the ports have been blocked.
We can infer that she still possesses some degree of control over our current predicament.
Sorry, Doctor, I'm just as confused as you are.
I followed your guidance, and checked a few potential places to slip away from... They're all no-gos.
This is different from the dream that machine made...
Basically, we're trapped.

opts: Strange thing is, she doesn't seem like she'll do anything to us.
It's like she cares more about gathering that data I can't understand.

lines.x: Hmm...
Is there the possibility, Doctor... that she can't do anything to us—
Do you see these ever-changing buildings around us?
The city known as Laterano is currently experiencing dramatic upheaval, and traces of the damage it has taken will occasionally be mapped onto here.
Changes in information in reality should not be overwriting data inside Originium. This plan still has flaws.

opts: The Sankta in the city...
So you never actually cared about the Sankta.

lines.x: I see there's still a great deal of misunderstanding between us, Doctor.
I believe I can now answer your prior questions. The ones pertaining to the precious results we managed to obtain, alongside the shared vision pieced together by we countless many.
Right now, under my instructions, PCS is reaching its theoretical operational capacity.
This means it is able to include even more targets in the same language system, far beyond an army or a research team... but rather, a multitude of races.
Through this, connections can be strengthened. Barriers can be broken. Differences can be eliminated.
That's not such a bad thing. Not the Sankta, not for all life on Terra.

opts: You're sounding like some kind of advocate for interracial harmony.

lines.x: I can't deny that... though we would benefit as well, of course.
I didn't calibrate PCS to churn out soldiers or scientists we can order about.
Such a plan would collapse at the slightest hint of true disaster. Originium is our only solution.
Needlessly complex biological information will only serve to slow its progress, but PCS has the ability to unify the races into an integrated whole.
It can merge the countless lives of Terra into a singular race, who will pave the way for the expansion of Originium.
As for whether this unified race can still be considered 'Sankta', I don't particularly care.
It seems PCS is rather set on this outcome, going so far as to autonomously perform many unnecessary inferences for it to come to pass.
It should not have this degree of independence. Perhaps a side effect of the sudden increase in throughput, or an abnormality caused by its long period of disuse...
If we have the chance, we can spend some time looking into it as a fun diversion... but not right now.
I-I... really don't think she's hiding anything, Doctor.
Hiding?
Rather than allowing you to fool around and break my samples, Doctor, I think it would be better if I showed you around personally, just like old times.
The woman reaches her hand out towards you.

opts: (Hesitate)
(Step back)
(What's she thinking?)

lines.x: She retracts her hand, then turns to continue onwards.
None of this will stop, Doctor
Aren't you interested to see the ultimate outcome?

opts: Follow her.

lines.x: She knew you would follow her.
Something's wrong, Doctor. I recorded the route we followed to come here...
From the Shrine of Nurtury to the Tower of Revelations, then on to the Notarial Hall in Pagus Michaelion, which means the Sunset Chapel is up ahead—

opts: Federico mentioned this route to me once during a chat.
It's the route the monsters are following to gather up!

lines.x: And then there's those unusual scenes of destruction around us. They keep being mapped onto here, which means the chaos in Laterano is still ongoing.

opts: We've been walking alongside them in a whole different dimension.

lines.x: The moment you become cognizant of this, you see the stillness of the city begin to break. Motion blossoms across the scene in stutter-freeze frames.
Battle. Explosions. Prayer. Chanting.
You see Exusiai.
You see that hallowed monstrosity—
You cannot tell if this scene has arisen because of your presence, or if she has deigned to allow you to see it.
You should be able to clear your thoughts this way, in a safer manner.
The place you seek is ahead. The location of PCS...
As well of the destination of the 'Sankta' pilgrims—
The countdown has begun, Doctor.
Seriously, not even time for a breather?
Keep it quick, Fiammetta.
I'll cover you while you're reloading.
What's with the look? We've fought together before. It's not easy to forget how you do things.
Gratias.
Hey, Mostima, mind explaining to me what the hell just happened?
I was kicking a monster's ass when I stepped in one of those weird light puddles on accident, and...
Stolide! My boot sprouted a halo outta nowhere!
It's one of the Shrine of Nurtury's projections. If you don't feel like being turned into stuff your brain can't even process, then stay away from it!
And how're we supposed to do that...?
It's spreading way too quick. It'll kick us out of Pagus Saint Marcel before that pack of monsters even gets here...
None of us can stick around to play defense at that point!
We've stuffed the big pipe organ in the Sunset Chapel full of explosives. Our friends from Leithanien added some self-igniting enchantment on top of it.
Even if nobody's around, it should still be able to blow these freaks to kingdom come.
The one they used to play the dirge for the city-wide memorial service?
That's the one.
Sweet. It's had to listen to hundreds of years worth of prayers and sobbing. It's time we play it something with a bit more pep.
Let's get out of here, Patia. Our spent shells have started getting halos.
The Tower of Revelations in Pagus Ecclesia and the Notarial Hall in Pagus Michaelion have been lost. The Sunset Chapel in Pagus Saint Marcel is the last stop before they reach the Basilica.
If our bombs don't stop them, then we really are screwed.
Hah, if Federico and Andoain were right, and the procession really is headed for the Basilica... I think it's too early to throw in the towel.

I've been a Legatus for a long time now, so I've got experience with getting places quickly.
If we can't stop them from rushing right over, then...
Why not just remove their destination?
Look, Mostima, there's a big difference between blowing up a pipe organ and incinerating the entire Basilica!
Hey, if boots and Sankta can both have halos, I don't think there's that big a difference between the organ and the Basilica.
Fine, you're right. Since we can't block or kill that giant monster, we can at least think of a way to make the road not exist anymore. She'll never be able to reach her destination.
You sure we're talking about the same thing here?
Let's go! Withdraw to the plaza in front of the Basilica!
*sniffle*... *sob*...
Wh-What's wrong, Romya?
Stop crying! You're giving me a headache!
Don't be like that, Hugo.
Why do we need to take care of these crybabies, Sister?
Can we leave Laterano? This place isn't anything like what Aurela said...
Not as nice, right?
Yeah!
I think so too.
So we should be glad we came to see for ourselves. Otherwise, how would we have known Aurela lied to us?
We were rather lucky this time, weren't we? Once Aurela comes back, we should give her a good talking-to!
R-Right, Sister.
Let's continue with the story, children.
(Shakes head) I-I'm scared...
No need to be afraid. Listen. We can still hear the bells, right? The bells of the holy city will protect us.
*sobs* ...But those sounds are so loud and scary.
Hugo, Giselle, do you hear them as well?
(Shake heads)
Nueda gathers the children to her side as best as she can. She takes note of the fear twisting the faces of the Sankta children, their halos flickering like mad.
(Is it empathy? That monster's empathy, the procession's empathy, an entire city's worth of empathy...)
(We're so far away, and yet...)
All Nueda can do is stay silent.
She is no Sankta. What she is dealing with is not a gang of Singas mercenaries. The complex and mysterious knowledge floating around in her mind is of no use.
She cannot even say how she got to this point.

lines.st: Think quick, K'uk'ulkan.
What can you do to calm the children who followed you all the way from Bolívar, to console the children who had just been plucked from a terrifying dream—
At the very least, allow them to feel a modicum of tranquility.

lines.x: East of the east of La Unidad♪
Laterano, holy city of white♪
Romya, i-it's a hymn...
Senior Nurturer taught it to us...
Sister Nueda?
You all know the words, right?
There, the earth hides no bombs♪
There, the fields share wheat most bright♪
Abode of our Lord, font of parental love♪
He hears our prayers, He grants our wishes♪
All shall bask in His love♪
The K'uk'ulkan thinks back to the past. The gravely wounded nun closed herself off from the world as she sang, until pain and night melted away into her song.
The K'uk'ulkan sings once more this song she has sung a hundred thousand times before. It no longer matters who wrote it, or if the Laterano within it will answer.

She does all she can to calm her nerves so that she can sing in tune. Slowly, the voices of the children join the chorus, echoing out from the room, blending with the distant peals to form a radiant glow.
All shall bask in His love♪
At the very least, they still have this song.
It might be of no use, but at least...
Nueda reaches under the hem of her habit, holding her axe tight.
R-Reverend?
I can ask our blessed companions to sniff out her position.
There was a time when, with her gun-knight armor and custom rifle, she would break the guardian gun kill record over and over. Finding her will take time.
The procession can't wait, Velliv. Focus on your work. I'll handle this myself.
It's been ten years. I'm surprised this private frequency still works.
