I wanted to ask the folks at the Notarial Hall to delete it a long time ago, but I keep forgetting. Guess I've gotten old, haha.
The real surprise is you, Aurela. Why didn't you take off your armor when you left Laterano?
Without it, how am I supposed to settle things with you... Magister?
*sigh* You really hate me, don't you?
It seems the saint was right. The best motivators for the errari to return to the holy city are hatred and loathing.
Heh.
So you're saying this mysterious 'Fall' that affected all of Terra brought out their bad side?
That might be so for others, but for me... You know better than anyone else.
Naturally.
I will never forget what you did to me.
In that nomadic monastery, you wounded me yourself, read the Curia's banishment order to me yourself... You took everything away from me, yourself.
You committed mortal sins. Your punishment was pretty light, given the circumstances.
*sigh* I am responsible for your situation.
Aurela, your achievements earned you a commendation from the Pope himself. You were such a spirited young thing. I was convinced you would become the greatest gun-knight in history.
But all I taught you was how to control that cumbersome armor and master your complicated gun. I never helped you with your temper, so you could truly live the Lateran life.
And in the end, you took that final step... just as I had predicted.
Hmph. Am I supposed to thank you for worrying about my character flaws?
Just because of it, you never approved of me and Amos. You even refused to come to our wedding, even though I had hoped to get your blessing.
Maybe we should have a proper talk about all this.
One way or another, you have returned. Will you come to my side, child? My people will not refuse you, if you join the procession.
What, want to give a sinner like me another chance in the name of Laterano?
No, nothing so lofty.
The errari are still of Laterano. That is all.
In a better Laterano, everyone will find their place and play their part. There will be more foundational, more profound connections between us all.
No matter who you are, no matter what you have done, the Holy City will mend your halo and fix the flaws you were born with.
When that time comes, all our pain and regrets will be healed in turn.
No thanks.
Aurela...
Listen, old man—
I hate how self-righteous you are. I hate how weak Amos was. I hate how inflexible the Curia is. I hate Laterano's stupid garbage about harmony! I hate how cold this Paradise is!
I never forgot this hate, even over ten years in Bolívar!
Even though my halo shattered, even though I was trapped in the dirt, I never once doubted that I would return!
Even when I was enthusiastic and wet-behind-the-ears, I never felt as sure of myself as I do now!
This is my connection with Laterano! This is my faith!
Your student turned out to be a cruel, vicious, selfish soul. Even if she hadn't fallen, she was still no angel!
Tell me, old man, this 'better Laterano' of yours, if it will let in someone who was born evil, does it deserve to be called Paradise?!
Or are you unsure about that too?!
Question time's over.
You've been trying to get a bead on me all this time... Failed?
You've actually gotten better since you left, Aurela—
Grkh! *cough* *cough*—Flagritriba!
These new glasses Vani got me... turned out really clear...

The 'Saint' drags her massive body along as she walks at the head of the procession. The Sankta named Lemuel jumps around, pulling out gun after gun like prestidigitation, raining a hail of gunfire onto her quarry.
But the 'Saint' barely even spares her a glance.
Her halo shimmers, and despite the great distance separating them, Patrizion still hears her 'voice' with perfect clarity.
Their empathy is growing stronger, which means their long journey approaches its end.
Allow our other cohorts to take care of your friend's remains, Patrizion. We do not need to mourn him.
Your Beatitude...
Do you still remember that arborbeast fledgling?
If we reach our destination, Laterano shall recreate the miracle of life once more. Every miracle, freely performed.
You mean...?
And so, we move onwards.
The grand procession passes statues after statue, workshop after workshop. The city grows brighter, step by step, pace by pace.
Patrizion walks forward in silence.
S... Stop.
A young gunsmith's apprentice walks against the flow of people, stopping in front of Patrizion. Vannini stares down the gun-knight, many times taller than her. She raises her gun against him.
The crowd parts around them, but none look toward them. Vannini bites her lip, doing her best to stop her hand from trembling.
Ah, you are his apprentice, yes?
Magister Paganini... Where is he?
He...
He warned me. So in the dream, I-I... hid in the workshop. After I awoke, I still hesitated for so long...
Right now, you're... scary. Laterano's scary. I don't know what's going on, I don't even have a patron firearm...
But I can't just hide and wait...
So you made yourself that gun and came to stop me?
Mhm.
That's very brave of you.
But do you see this procession, my child? We will birth a miracle, one that nearly all Laterans will be a part of.
It's not 'scary'.
(Shakes head) That's got... nothing to do with me.
I'm not very... good with words. I couldn't... couldn't think of any worse ones...
Magister Paganini was... my only family...
My only... connection... to Laterano...
Your only connection, huh...?
Pag, you never got a halo in the dream, so why did you join the procession?
To find a chance to kill you, obviously.
That sniper, your old student?
Tragic, isn't it?
Worse than when Gia left?
You still going to listen to that monster? Going to keep protecting the crowd on this walk?
Don't think I don't know what you're thinking.
The Laterano of fifty years ago forced you to banish an infected Liberi. The Laterano of ten years ago forced you to banish a fallen gun-knight.
Even your granddaughter is far too stubborn and opinionated. Who knows if someday, she too will...
So, you hope for a better Laterano?
You want it to be like a doctor who becomes a miracle worker, able to treat Oripathy, able to fix personality flaws, able to make everyone accept one another, no questions asked?
That way, no more sad farewells like you've suffered through before?
*cough*... You're a coward through and through, Patrizion!
Farewells are like melted ice cream and worn-out guns. Doesn't matter what you do, it's bound to happen, right?
Even if you can't stop them from happening, you can still give your well-wishes. A simple 'goodbye' is all you need.
It's not like you have a speech impediment like Vannini. Is it so hard to find a way to shed some of your regret?
Enough talk! Just hang on!
Hang on, my feathery ass—*cough*—This is... a bullet that can cut right through gun-knight armor...
Don't touch me. Let me finish, otherwise I'll step into the grave that much quicker.
A-Alright then...
What's with the tone? Don't even think about getting sentimental on me, Patrizion!
Why do you think I saved you?
Don't jabber at me about calamity or crisis or what path Laterano should take...
If the city really is going to be destroyed, surely we can do more than just leave it to some monster from beyond our nightmares that sprang up out of nowhere?
Aurela, Fiammetta, all those other Laterans you know... Are these connections that weak to you?
You're a gun-knight, you stulte! The best gun-knight in all of Laterano!
*cough* *cough*—
Paganini gets more intense the more he rambles, but his voice grows quieter by the second.
Patrizion holds his old friend tight, feeling the warmth slowly drain out of him, as though the last dregs of his life had spilled out with his latest outburst.
Hey, not going to say anything?
Never mind then. Before, at the Shrine, I said we never actually shared anything these past few decades...
Now, at the very least... That regret's gone.
My magister, he...
Forgive me. I couldn't even give you one final moment with Pag.
*sob*—
Vannini, i-if you had just... been a bit quicker... making this gun, if you had just gotten here quicker...
Vannini lowers her head as if in a daze. She looks about at the people walking past, like she is only just noticing her surroundings.
Her halo suddenly begins to shine. All it would take is a momentary waver of the heart, and the empathy of the countless masses would come crashing down on this confused, lonely Sankta.
No, child.
Keep a tight grip on your gun!
Ggh...
For a moment, Patrizion feels pain, but it is followed by a gentle, yet irrefusable comfort. It flows from her, from those enlightened creatures, from his people in the procession.
He knows his unusual reaction has been noticed, prompting the crashing waves of empathy, like seawater forcing its way back upriver.
Ah—
Patrizion feels dizzy. He sighs, and slowly crouches down.
A moment later, the elderly gun-knight grips the barrel of the gunsmith's apprentice's gun, pointing it towards him.
Vannini. I got your name right, I hope?
Vannini, no matter how long it took you, or for what reason you did it, you made your very first patron firearm. That makes you a qualified gunsmith.
But that is not enough. You must learn how to use it.
Now is a perfect opportunity.
Wh-What do you mean?
Keep it steady. Yes, just like that. Calm your mind, then, pull the trigger.
You... You didn't kill Magister Paganini. Also, I'll fa... fall—
I am responsible for what happened to Pag, but this is not to avenge him.
At this very moment, Laterano is walking down a new path. Faith flows into faith, and new prayers congregate into a collective consensus.
You are too close to the procession. Empathy will soon swallow you whole. Only by falling can you break away.
Once you have fallen, I'll go guide the others. This is my duty, the duty of a gun-knight.
I don't understand. Why... do you do this?
I don't really know myself, haha. I'm an old addlepate, after all.
I always believed that, were Paradise to become a true paradise, our connections would become unbreakable.
But what if in the course of all that, we lost more than we already had?
We might ease our regrets, but when the time comes, what if our success has lost all meaning?
If we were once all devils, if we once carried our sins together, how could our connections ever be broken?
O glorious Sankta, O favored of faith, what have you to fear?
(Pained inhalation)
Vannini, girl, fear not falling into hell.
This is the first lesson you should learn... and I'm right with you.
It just never ends...
If this keeps on, I'm gonna have to start smacking them with my weapon!
Me too.
My hand's getting numb, can barely hold my staff.
Don't know how it's going on Fiammetta's side of things. According to the plan, once she sets the explosives, we'll blow up the plate's locking mechanisms and send all of Pagus Saint Marcel adrift.
It'll also cut those monsters off from the Basilica.
Tsch. If we can hold on that long.
Dispersing Arrows!
Leave them to me!
These clothes...
Landen Guard temporary solo combat team, Hildegard, at your service!
Landen... Sorry, never heard of you.
It's fine, I'm used to it, haha.
Some of my Sankta sisters are in that procession. We were running our business when we got called back, and then... we went through a whole bunch of stuff.
How should I put this...? Even if everything that happened was fake, and Landen Monastery hasn't actually been given important responsibilities by the Curia...
As long as I'm here, Laterano will once again remember the name of Landen Monastery!
These monsters might look strange, but they're hardly more of a challenge than the beasts of the great snowcapped mountains.
I've got enough arrows, and I've got a sharp eye. I'll help you hold them off!
But we're all tired out, kid. We've racked our brains and put in the effort, but all we can do is keep falling back.
We're not exactly settling any grudges here. We came back to Laterano just to suffer again.
It's almost time, Mostima. Still no news from that Lemuen lady?
If we can't disengage the defenses on the plate lock mechanism, we won't be able to blow through their armor plating, right?
Let's just call it here then.
If those guys reach the Basilica, then what happens? Everyone more or less becomes Sankta?
What's that got to do with us?
Look around you. We can't even cobble together a single halo. Our long-awaited reinforcements are just one Liberi nun.
We've got an entire city's worth of people against us. Why do we Alieni have to hold all of them off?
Come now, that's not the best attitude to have right now...
Because right now, we're the only ones who can.
We're always having to bust our asses doing things ordinary Sankta take for granted, right?
I had a little accident, as you can see. If I hadn't become a Legatus, I don't know what I'd be doing right now.
I said I was going to be doing Messenger stuff when I left the Holy City, but I was just wandering around, really.
Wandering to places without any Sankta, places where these black horns on my head don't stand out, places where silent empathy's the norm...
It was hard.
It took two whole years before I could look my friends in the eyes again without breaking out in a sweat.
Liberi, Fallen Angels, we're obsessed with assimilating or getting our old lives back. After putting in all that effort, why go with the hatchet job?
Well, how about that.
I never thought I'd hear those words coming out of your mouth, Mostima.
Promise me you'll forget you heard anything.
Forget what?
How did things go on your end?

Good news is, the bombs are in place.
Bad news is, I was afraid they wouldn't be enough to blow through all the lock mechanisms, so I shoved the rest of my grenades in there with them...
Which means I'm all out of grenades to shoot at these things.
(Halo rapidly flickers)
(Continuous sounds of prayer)
Hey, Fiammetta, let's blow this joint.
Wait! We still haven't heard anything from Lemuen. If the defenses aren't disengaged in time, all our explosives will go to waste!
Time to explosion, one minute!
Wha-?
The timer starts ticking down in Fiammetta's hand. The enlightened creatures stop their prayers. As if sensing something is up, they throw themselves at her like rabid beasts.
Hildegard reaches for her quiver. It's empty.
Fifty seconds!
Sorry, everyone! Hope I'm not too late!

A wheelchair suddenly brakes, throwing up a cloud of dust as it screeches across the ground. Its rider spins its left wheel as she hoists her rifle with her right hand, her long hair fluttering in the air.
You're right on time!
Good to know we're still in sync!
The two of you will never guess how hard I beat my old wheelchair speed-shooting record by after I broke through their cordon around the Seventh Tribunal!
Forty seconds!
I heard what you said just now. Fallen Angels, Liberi, Alieni... I'm inclined to use another term to sum up our relationship.
Friends, countrymen—Laterans!
I've solved the permissions issue. The lock mechanism defenses are disengaged. Everyone, retreat to the Basilica doors! I'll hold these monsters off.
Thirty seconds!
You're going to shoot them, Lemuen? You'll fall!
I hate to admit it, but I can sense what you're thinking.
So shut those mouths of yours. Laterano doesn't need to be saved! By you! Like this!
The click of bullets loading and the thrumming of the monsters' hearts reverberate through Lemuen's ears. She is nearly face-to-face with them, going against every sniper's instinct she has.
She listens to her own breathing, calm and steady.
Under these conditions, how fast can a sniper fire? Time to find out.
Lemuen, Cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal. In the name of Laterano's national security, I sentence you to death!
No matter the cost!
Five seconds.
It's done.
Lemuen, your halo... You alright? Feel anything weird?
Ngh—
A violent explosion tears through every inch of the ground in front of the Basilica, a rift plowing itself through the plaza.
On the other side of the rift, the grand procession shows itself as it marches forward.
Is that... El?
Hey, turns out it was my sis and her crew! They did a real bang-up job!
(Wounded wail)
(Fragmented prayer)
Retreat somewhere safe, for the time being.
A rift... One my people cannot cross. But we once forded raging rivers and traversed great valleys. Compared to those dangers, this is nothing.
He lies ahead. I shall not stop.
*cough* I... I never said I'd let you pass.
Your body is weak. I fear you have not an ounce of strength left. Am I wrong?
Exusiai does not answer.
She has told herself the entire time to ignore how tired she feels, how strange falling makes her feel. She continues her barrage against the saint's colossal body, but once again to no avail.

The saint makes no move against her. Rather, she continues to cradle her, the same way she did when they left the dream. Never before has such a strange cradle been seen, one made from a malformed yet mighty arm.
Even though you know the entire truth of both past and present, even though you know we are working to create a new miracle, like the birth of Laterano...
Even though you know our journey thus far has been to awaken our Lord, to ensure that in the future with but a hair's breadth separating destruction from continuation, the Sankta shall persist...
Why do you still stand against us?
Hmm...
Am I the only one?
What?
With empathy that strong, I don't believe for a second you can't feel it!
After so many long years of waiting, I finally got my chance, but... it was all just a dream...
A dream's fine, too.
If it's all a dream, then I don't have to worry about anything. Maybe getting shot here doesn't mean anything in reality!
If this is a dream, then I shall wake. If this is obsession, then I shall shatter it.
Let us only speak of redemption after that.
Vannini, girl, fear not falling into hell.
This is the first lesson you should learn... and I'm right with you.
East of the east of La Unidad♪ Laterano, holy city of white♪
There, the earth hides no bombs♪ There, the fields share wheat most bright♪
All shall bask in His love♪
I hate to admit it, but I can sense what you're thinking.
So shut those mouths of yours. Laterano doesn't need to be saved! By you! Like this!
I'm just an ordinary Lateran. Nobody important, and I don't have any special skills.
So when I listened to you talk about the origins of the Sankta, the history of Laterano, the essence of faith, the crazy truth behind the life we're used to...
I was shocked! Shocked right down to my core!
If things really are like you say, then the life we enjoyed in Laterano really is a kind of miracle.
But since I'm just an ordinary Lateran, while you were explaining everything happening around us, all I noticed was...
How nobody looked happy at all.
Shouldn't Laterano be a happy place?
If... If lots of our people will suffer when we complete this journey, how am I supposed to believe you can make the miracle happen?
Wait, no, scratch that, how am I supposed to believe it's a miracle in the first place?
So, even if I wasn't around, there'd be other Sankta to stand against you.
I shared all I have seen and all I have experienced, yet you still choose to turn away?
The path of the Sankta is a cruel one, our faith paved with sin and suffering.
Have their years inside this peculiar, distorted Paradise left our brethren so weak?

Ugh, you're not getting me at all.
Maybe I should be asking what you asked me earlier—
Why you?
I have already told you my answer.
Even if this Terra-destroying calamity is real, and millions of Sankta are doomed, and Laterano's about to be completely screwed...
Why are you, a 'Saint', the only one who can bring us salvation?
We're the ones who live in this city right now, who enjoy its blessings, who went through its changes, and who protected it from destruction.
You don't exist anymore. You're just a ghost! A string of code I can shoot!
Incorrect, my child. When I lifted my spear against my dying companion, the sleeping silver mounts answered me, and recorded my prayer for all eternity.
All that is happening here and now is a continuation of that prayer.
Death cannot be undone, yet I wandered amongst His datastream. Together, we heard your prayers.
No matter how appearances may change, no matter how the faith develops as time flows by, so long as the Sankta still pray, He shall operate eternally, and I shall forever be there...
The spiritual light will always shine on Laterano.
This is our mission.
...Prayer is useless.
Pardon?
To be honest, I learned a long time ago that prayer is useless.
When I was in my third year of primary school, an investigator from the Sixth Tribunal did an experiment at our school.

She said there were two groups of patients at Faber Central Hospital with more or less the same conditions, and split us into our own two groups. One group would pray every day, while the other didn't have to do anything.
But in the end, there wasn't any difference in their recovery. The investigator told us the results in class: prayer is useless.
Haha, it's funny, really. This country's full of churches, but it's always doing these kinds of 'outrageous' experiments.
(Halo flickers)
You're right, Laterans really do pray to the Lord every day.

Praying they'll get better soon after getting sick, praying they'll pick the coolest guardian gun ever, praying the caravans won't run into Catastrophes on the way home, praying they'll never have to say goodbye to their friends...
Praying that they'll be able to make it through all sorts of pain and suffering.
But I never actually expected there to be a 'Lord' who would grant everyone's wishes, who would swoop in to save us when we're in trouble.
Empathy lets us sense the emotions of our people. It lets me know if the ones by my side are happy or sad.
But prayer? Prayer doesn't just need me to sense things, it needs me to want to help my family and friends, to understand things outside of empathy.
It even lets me feel like I could help some complete strangers get well sooner.
Even though I know it probably won't change a thing.
I met lots of Sankta while running around with Penguin Logistics. You'd be hard pressed to call some of them Father, while others don't live life like we do in the Holy City anymore.
But they were all so... fulfilled. I still felt close to them.
I believe it's because we still prayed like we always did.
It needs us to not doubt. It needs us to hold hope in our hearts.
Just being down to pray for each other is a beautiful thing in itself.
It gives me a true connection to people.
(Thunderous sigh)
If these connections were the foundation of faith, it would be exceedingly frail.
No. I think it's exactly these connections that help hold our lives up.
'Survival'... 'Life'...
If you don't understand why Sankta these days are all about desserts and explosions—
What's up with you?
(Fretful pacing)

The saint suddenly becomes exceptionally restless. She feels out the edge of the rift as she paces along it, her sharp front claws piercing the exposed, bent foundations, trying to find a way to climb down.
This rift you have opened allows me to sense His signal much more clearly.
He is below the Basilica, calling out for me to approach—
No, no... Why is His signal in such disarray... He is anxious, uneasy...
The Creator has already revoked... or perhaps.... there are other threats forcing Him to... change...
The sound of machinery, faint yet clear, rises out from the depths of the rift below their feet.
The saint's unease becomes increasingly obvious. Half of her body has already entered the fault. Her claws clench, and Exusiai senses that she is in great pain.
Behind her, the procession stops, countless halos flickering over and over due to her worry, like a river of paralyzed stars.
Child, talks of past and present are meaningless... You need only step towards... the future...
You do not understand this crisis. There are no new solutions for you to find. You must... survive...
I said it before, I'm not letting you pass.
How are you meant to stop... a ghost... a string of code...?
(Mournful roar)
The saint's voice becomes garbled. She has reverted to barely more than a beast, like the Teekaz who trudged through the mud all those years ago.
If we weren't in this mess, we could've been pretty good friends. A ghost, a string of code, the very first Sankta... There's no cooler friend than that!
Ah, silly me! Can't believe I forgot to introduce myself!
Lemuel of St. Theodore's Comprehensive School, number one student in the 84th Intercity Net Security preparatory course! And I've got the school certs to prove it!
Every bullet I got you with was custom-made! They just needed some time to take effect!
Her patron firearm howls once more. The force of the barrage finally frees Exusiai from the saint's grasp.
She leaps high into the sky.
We're not going to give up on life, obviously, but we're definitely not going to go with your plan.
If refusing it means the halos connecting us will be cut off, then cut them off.
Because even if you do, we will never stop praying for each other.
We'll still give thanks to the Lord for His existence, even if He isn't what we thought He was.
O Lord, thank you for being there, for blessing us with bread and bullets, for being there for us during our trials and tribulations!
Thank you for spreading the light across Laterano all this time, giving everyone the chance for salvation!
Thank you for clearing the way for us!
Amen. There, prayer done.
Let's say bye-bye, the wildest way I know—
(Roars)
Explosives fall like rain, linking together into a chain of blasts as they collide with the bullets embedded in the saint's body.
The saint howls, but not because of the explosions, not because of her flesh wounds, but because of the strange, dissonant sound caused by the 'ammunition' as it scrapes across her skin.
The sound of data being erased.
Divine judgment, traditional!
Rock n' gospel!
Barrage, sky-shattering!
Apple pie, with char siu!

lines.st: 'Retain humanity's emotions'? 'Retain humanity'?
Negative.
Humanity does not exist. Variable referred to as 'humanity' is a byproduct of Originium.

lines.x: Originium?
A... sea, made of Originium?
It's like the sea back home. Calm, beautiful, but one nobody dares to set out on it.
Maybe, maybe not. This is familiar to you because... because the sea by your hometown is here. There's even a part of you here too.
All things that are have been sealed here. A coffin for the world and everything upon it.
Like the crystals left behind by Oripathy patients after they die?
If that helps you understand it, then yes.
Amazing, isn't it? This was once the end of everything.
We are born far too late, our lives far too short. We even unconsciously take some inconsequential mishaps, and turn them into the foundation of our very being.
Like how you blindly took a shattered memory and turned it into divine revelation.
But no matter. The Law wishes to bestow upon you true revelation.
He says: 'None can be saved.'

lines.st: Originium to Originium, dust to dust.
Personnel with relevant privileges have provided PCS with new set of directives. Cognitive radiation amplifier at maximum output. Effective range: Terra.
Originium will connect to PCS after maintenance is complete.
Originium is inevitable.

lines.x: The Law created the Sankta from the Teekaz, built the order of Laterano in untamed wilds, and even attempted to establish a Terra-wide religion spanning multiple civilizations.
He has worked for millennia in service of humanity. Surely it was not simply to turn all of it into Originium?
Is that truly the Law's ultimate goal?

lines.st: Negative.
This is the goal of Originium, not the goal of 'the Law'.
Originium has been set as a prerequisite of 'the Law'.

lines.x: If Originium is as you earlier described, able to assimilate everything, why is it a prerequisite to anything?
Surely it is more than the end of everything?
How would the Law be able to operate inside a field of uniform Originium? What would be the meaning of a halo in that case?

lines.st: As Originium is the end of all things, there is meaning in bestowing halos on everything.
Angels before Originium are merely angels.
Angels after Originium are the only method of existence that will retain meaning.

lines.x: 'None can be saved'?
So not even Laterano as it is, a cramped Paradise, can be preserved?
It cannot be saved, nor preserved.
'Save everyone' has never been a tenable commandment.
All things march towards death. The only way to 'save' them is to see them off.
Grant them a dream, a bearing to hold themselves to. Hide their blemishes, espouse their brilliance.
By doing so, you allow them to gladly accept their final journey.
Like how you saved yourself.
Granting dreams to others as a placebo?
No. I won't accept that.
If I have truly, shamelessly clung onto a false dream, put myself through a crucible for more than ten years...
If I have debased myself by inflicting unhealing wounds upon countless others for the sake of self-salvation...
Then all the more reason not to spread this shamelessness and debasement to others.
Even if you accepted it, you are unable to perform such an act.
Only the Law can bestow such salvation.
You can't possibly mean...
Keph's plan... was to give everyone halos, all so they can be granted this 'salvation'?
But how would becoming Sankta help? Are Sankta beyond callousness, beyond cruelty, beyond... suffering?!
We shall not stop at becoming Sankta.
We will all become true angels, Andoain Agathangelos.
True... angels?
Aggy... Agathangelos? I don't like this name. It's too long and hard to read, and it doesn't sound like any of the other names here.
This is... my surname. Before your father left, he kept telling me he hoped I would give it to you.
But, Mamma...
I know it's strange and hard to read, Andoain.
If the other children knew you had this name, they might say hurtful things to you like 'there's that weird boy!'.
Once you head out further afield, bandits may think you're a rich foreigner, while the Inquisition might assume you're a zealot up to no good.
Which is why I hope you never share it with others. Stick with a simple name, like the other orphans here.
I hope you will keep it in the deepest recesses of your heart, a place where your happiness, anger, sorrow and joy cannot touch.
But I feel really sad right now... I don't know if there's anywhere in me that doesn't feel sad.
Is there really a place like that in my heart?
Of course, Andoain. Everyone has one.
I hope that, when you think of this name, you will be free from suffering, grief, frustration, and strife.
Forget the dying and the hungry by the road who claw at your ankles. Forget those in despair, whose minds were eaten away by the sea fog, and the flesh they chew on.
Forget the monster doll your father made, and my finscale soup. Forget the storybook you keep beneath your pillow, and the brush you found in the old chapel.
But I don't want to forget them!
...It's just for a little while, alright?
I hope that, when you think of this name, you will find tranquility... the tranquility of emptiness.
One day, you will need this peace.
Promise me, Andoain.
Alright.
But I still want to know what this name means.
'Angel'. In Lateran, it means... 'Angel'.
Tranquility... Yes, a very apt descriptor.
Behold. Even as they come, they do so in silence.
The ocean of Originium is endless. Those who look upon it soon realize that every observation they make of it is without meaning.
When everything the eye can see is impossible to differentiate, where to start looking is similarly impossible to determine.
But now, above this great sea of uniformity, something shines.

It is as if the edge of the Originium sea has converged into a river that flows into the heavens. Countless fragments of Originium rise up from the surface of the sea, floating up into the distant, empty beyond in perfect formation.
The fragments are jagged and irregular. Some are as small as dust, others as immense as stars, but all, without exception, are crowned with a glimmer of light.
A glimmer shaped like a ring.
Halos...
Beautiful, aren't they?
It's a pity that all the beauty and ugliness in the world is meaningless to angels.
Angels are neither beautiful nor ugly. They are neither high nor low, noble nor base, reverent nor blasphemous, adorant nor disdainful.
They know neither meeting nor separation, happiness nor suffering.
Can they even be considered life?
Does that matter? All the praise we heap upon life is nothing but egotistical admiration.
Observing their forms in an attempt to compare them to our definition of life is simply us trying to find resonance with them.
Then, are they the Law? Are they gods?
If there is no distinction between beauty and ugliness, high and low, noble and base, why would there be one for god and worshiper?
Angels do not understand faith. They simply exist in the furthest reaches of the universe, taking on the most distinctive forms.
It was our forebears who encountered them, shamelessly disturbing them as they did so. In their name, we created a forgery of a dialectic, of god and worshiper.
If all our emotions, ideas and actions have nothing to do with angels...
What does our future have to do with them?
It is because everything we are has nothing to do with angels that we should become them.
Now, this is only a possibility, but...
When the Creator descends, when everything is submerged into this ocean before us, a time of stasis, uniformity, and meaninglessness...
The Sankta within can take on the forms of angels and enjoy an eternal existence, where they shall shine forevermore.
This is the final answer we shall submit to death.
This is His gift to the people He has guarded for thousands of years, His final blessing and salvation.

lines.st: We drift, just as we drift between past and future.
We flicker, just as we flicker between reality and the void.
Angels know not beginnings or ends. Angels know not increase or decrease. Angels know not life or death.
In an existence where nothing exists, we can only exist in the form of angels.

lines.x: So these are true angels?
Incomprehensible to the point where the Law cannot directly describe them, instead defining them by what they are not?
Immaterial to the point where taking the form of angels allows one to continue to exist after becoming Originium?
This is the existence the Law has chosen once everything returns to Originium?

lines.st: Affirmative.

lines.x: If this is the ultimate purpose of the Law, then as a saint and an executor, I should implement and enforce it.
Unless...
Unless the Law itself is a paradox.
I have one final question!
Explain to me how, if angels truly are as you say, without beginning or end, increase or decrease, life or death...
Then why do they—you—have such a strong desire to exist... to continue to exist?
As machines have inputs, so they have outputs.
The saints sought answers in the Law, and the Law granted them revelation.
The fleet asked the system for instructions, so the system issued commands.
The Lord, or rather, PCS, has never been as silent as it is now.
You are an artificial system, so you require people to operate you. This is true for both a 'command system' and the Law worshiped by the Holy City.
The ultimate purpose you were given was to break humanity free from their shackles and return their angelic nature.
However, this purpose, does it stem from people, or from angels?
Why would the angels you described desire this? Their actions do not have purpose, assuming they can even perform actions other than 'drift' and 'flicker'.
But if this purpose comes from people, how will you turn them into angels?
Surely everything you have done was more than just—

lines.st: A wish?
...A wish.

lines.x: Nurturer, I shall now grant you salvation.
Guide your people to angelification. Guide yourself.
Be free from suffering, grief, frustration, and strife. You will obtain tranquility, the tranquility of emptiness, as shall those you have helped and harmed alike.
You need not live as an egotist, a villain, a traitor, or a fanatic. Nor do you need to die as one.
Andoain feels more tired than he has ever been before, as if an entire lifetime's worth of burdens have been pressed down onto his back at once.
The arborbeast was right. He really is like that Sarkaz drunkard, acutely aware this is the last chance he has.
If he wanted relief, this is the only way.
'Relief'. He only needs to hear the word's crisp syllables to feel joy, to want to fall to the ground and weep until he has spent all his strength, then fall further into a deep slumber.
But he uses that strength to instead open his clouded eyes.
I don't know what kind of answer His Holiness gave you, nor do I know what Mostima, Fiammetta, Lemuen or her little sister are thinking right now aboveground.
I don't know where Patia and our former fellows are headed, nor do I know whose bosom the children I tended in the Shrine of Nurtury have clutched.
I don't know if they want this salvation, this relief.
But if what you really care about is my answer as a 'suitable candidate'...
Then my answer is no.
I'm far too conceited. Even to this very moment, I still refuse to believe my arduous search for answers, decades in the making, was all for this simple relief.
I'm not only conceited, I'm an egotist, a villain, a traitor, and a fanatic. And so I suffer and grieve, my frustrations giving rise to strife.
But they are the foundations of my life.
If I were to abandon them, the one who obtains relief, who becomes an angel, would not be me.
Andoain Agathangelos shall never obtain relief.
Nor will he ever wish for it!
Let suffering scar his flesh, let strife snap his bones! Then, and only then, will he have the clarity of mind to walk his path!
It seems we have reached an agreement.
The Law itself is a paradox. He cannot be implemented, nor should He be.
Andoain, we must correct Him... or stop Him, if He cannot be corrected.

lines.st: Paradox discovered. Attempting correction.
Performing diagnostic simulation.
Abnormal node: Wish to 'continue'.
Derived abnormal node: PCS, Personality and Cognition Synchronization system.
Abnormal node: Wish to 'survive'.
Derived abnormal node: The Law, God, Laterano.
Abnormal node: Wish for 'angels'.
Derived abnormal node: Me.

lines.x: 'Continue'. A scientist input this command.
'Survive'. A Teekaz howled this.
And so, an imitator of angels began its endless yet transient pilgrimage.
The imitator of angels became a command system, became the Law, became a god, yet it could not become an angel.
'Wish' and 'angel' are incompatible.

lines.st: Exporting solution.
Unfulfilled conditions. Additional input required.

lines.x: What information is He waiting for?
I've said all I can say.
If God wishes me to embrace death as an angel...
Then I would rather be born as a human, with all the ugliness, transience, and suffering that entails.

lines.st: Importing additional condition: Wish to 'refuse'.
Re-exporting solution: must eliminate abnormal nodes and derived abnormal nodes.
Eliminate 'me'.
Federico Giallo. Andoain Agathangelos.
Execute solution.

lines.x: God's final commandment... is to kill God.
Even if He was a paradox, he was still the foundation of the Paradise of Laterano. With His death, the Sankta will no longer be linked by empathy or receive guidance from the Law.
Like an infant trapped within a womb, biting apart its umbilical cord and crawling its way out of its mother's cold body, helpless as it faces a land steeped in cruelty.
But it is only in such a land that we can be called truly human.
That we can have the right to talk about salvation.
Law or God, I will kill you.
The commandment is rational and feasible.
Assist me, Andoain.
Whew—
Arturia leans against her cello. Finally. She frees a hand and holds it against her chest, allowing herself to catch her breath.
Nearly there. If my memory serves me correctly, I just head down this corridor to return to the surface.
Oh, you patrols truly are relentless.
(Restless gasping)
(Halo flashing rapidly)
The wispy guards surround Arturia, attempting to drag her back underground. She has wasted far too much time inside this maze since she turned away from the machine.

Her patron firearm is lost. She has no desire to join her pursuers in prayer, or to perform their hearts' songs. She would rather destroy her own cello... Arturia has never found herself in such a predicament before.
The exit is right ahead, but she no longer has the strength to carry on.
The ground...?
Fede, what did you—
*cough* *cough*... Urgh...
The dust settles, and Arturia can finally see again.
The explosion earlier created a giant pit, one that connects to the rift in the plaza above, exposing the bowels of the Basilica to all.
A ring-shaped machine lies at the bottom of the pit quietly.
Sunlight shines upon it, but it has lost its radiance.
There it stands, as dull as a rock.
Unease.
This is the second time Arturia has felt this feeling so clearly, the first being the time she faced the Witch King in his Pavillon.
Deep within her body, somewhere she cannot see, things that were always linked together quietly break apart. There is no mistaking the feeling.
She breathes in deep. The moment she closes her eyes, countless similar melodies rush into her mind like the tide.
*sniffle*... *sob*...
You're crying, why...?
Why does your crying sound so annoying?!
You're annoyed? I-I can't feel it.
Sorry, I'm so sorry... But I'm scared!
It's like that time Seniora Serpilia locked me in a dark room. I can hear your voices clearly, but I can't hear what you're saying...
My connection with our enlightened companions has been... severed...
No, not just them... You're right here, Reverend Patrizion, but... I can't even...
Sense me, I know.
Wuh... Waaaahhhh....
Be a good girl and don't let go of my hand—
Your hand... That's not what your hand feels like...
It's like... mist.
Far away... You're so... far away from me.
...So, this is what it's like to fall into hell?
The changes do not end with her own body. Some old jokes have come true.
Every light we know will one day be extinguished.
And it is no exception.
The old Pope playfully points to the halo hovering above his head.
Even if everything about the Sankta existence is interpreted as 'its' boon?
Unease grows, and panic spreads.
Arturia tries to make sense of it all. This is not the same as what happened in Zwillingstürme. There is no malevolent force sowing discord among the emotions of the people this time around.
It is like a movement, one played countless times by an orchestra of the masses, had part of its melody forcibly plucked away, as if it never existed.
Like walking along a road with the sun, the rain, your friends and your home ahead of you, but you are torn away from it for no reason, leaving you feeling alone and helpless...
Alongside millions of your people.
'The Sankta will lose the cozy cabin of their faith'... Everything has come to pass.
Did you fail, Fede?
If you're scared, you can hold onto my tail.
You noticed Giselle's tears, didn't you, Romya? Have you forgotten what tears mean?
Let's finish our song, okay?
You see, Laterano hymns and Bolívarian hymns are quite different, so we need time to teach each other the parts we don't know.
We will be here, together.
East of the east of La Unidad♪
L-Laterano, holy city of white♪
...There, the earth hides no bombs♪
There, the fields share wheat most bright♪
All shall bask in His love♪
Arturia hears singing.
It wavers, revealing the anxiety, terror and worries of the singers... alongside pure, honest longing.
A longing for tranquility, to see night fall in peace and quiet.
Your Holiness, I have no doubt that Laterano as it is may be walking to its demise.
But I also believe it has never been this sincere.
I've said before that I can't hold off the apocalypse or overwrite death, and I refuse to lead.
But maybe my Arts can help them stave off this oncoming collapse.
They will let my brethren, free of empathy, to 'communicate' without reservation, purely and honestly.
Go listen to those songs. Go share your emotions. Go tell one another that, at this very moment, in the face of this indescribable loss, we still feel joy and sorrow together.
This has nothing to do with saints and sinners, redemption and responsibility, or whichever path I walk.
This is simply my reason to exist.
Laterano, holy city of purest white, falls into night. But today, there may not be an electrical operator named Alcide there to turn on the city lights.
Arturia takes off her gloves.
She decides to play towards the setting sun.
To her heart's content.
...There you are, Federico.
Your Holiness.
Did you see the new products on the tea table?
'Yvangelista XI Ice Cream Cake Tower'. Eleven scoops of ice cream, eleven flavors of cactus, the eleventh cake named after a pope...
I am a trained executor. I cannot say the same for my ability to evaluate desserts.
Just a token of gratitude, for not forgetting an old man on the brink of death as you withdrew.
Of course, I plan to promote it throughout the city, in the hopes that the people may rediscover the taste of life, sooner rather than later.
Considering the flavor, I suspect it may cause even more controversy.
It is true, however, that the citizens of Laterano are in dire need of a return to orderly life.
Suicides and crime rates have soared in the past three months.
Small-scale disturbances to large-scale exodus... there are more than a thousand cases in the Notarial Hall's records. The rebuilding of the city has only just begun.
The calamity has revealed too much and changed too much.
The Law was a cold machine. Our saint was a loathsome abomination. All of us share the same roots as the devils...
Any one of these truths is enough to destroy an individual's faith.
This rebuilding will be a tremendous undertaking.
Reconstruction of the damaged plates on which the Third and Fourth Tribunals are located has just begun. Efforts are underway to revitalize the economy.
Laterano has not been so busy in a long time. I hope that the busy times will provide a way for the people to repair the cracks in their hearts.
Speaking of which... what is your outlook on all this, Federico?
Sankta, Laterano, faith, empathy, communication, and our society... the many important decisions that the Holy City has made overs its millennia-long history, and even your canonization...
It was all the result of Him—a machine. The spiritual light has always been cold.
Your innate lack of empathy has bestowed upon you an outlook of extreme logic. Perhaps He chose you because you were the one who could understand the logic of His simulation the best.
I caught fragments of your conversation with Him.
Even after decades of talking to Him, I must admit... you were more suited to the task.
If I wasn't intimately familiar with the way you talk, I wouldn't have been able to tell who said what.
If the Law is a machine, a system, then the most effective method of communication is to think on His terms, with His language and His logic.
But you arrived at the opposite conclusion from Him.
I remember every word of the tenets, and every name with which I have ever come into contact on my assignments.
As far as Laterano is concerned, there is no great difference in the order required by machines and life.

The existence of life is a miracle, its two sides the opposing aspects of strength and fragility. Out of necessity, for survival and livelihood, we have continuously built and improved order. The Law is simply part of it.
The Law exists to protect...
Federico pauses. The executor known for his mechanical coldness chooses his words carefully, a rare sight to behold—
...the precious fragility of human life.
Precious fragility... such an interesting combination.
Therefore, we rejected Him. It was our joint decision, Your Holiness. Time will tell whether it was the right one.
Go on.
My, my. Now that's certainly something.
What is that in your hand, Lemuel?
A spray can.
This place is deep underground and cold. This big rock is bare and gray. No wonder everyone who comes here leaves with their head hung low, like they got a heavy cold...
I've had enough!
I'm gonna give it a fresh coat of paint, so people who see it will be happy... wanna join me?
Don't look at me like that! I finished third in the Lungmen graffiti competition two years ago!
You didn't forget what that is, did you?
Just think of it as my way of making a wish! Anything you can't figure out, anything you haven't gotten done, spray it on the rock, and maybe it'll come true!
...Wish?
Didn't the Sankta and Laterano come about because the saint made a wish?
But it's scrap now...
A rock is a rock! How do you scrap a rock?
Besides, you're still here. Me, that girl selling ice cream, that gunsmith over there, and all the Sanktas around are witnesses to your wish...
Who's to say it's necessarily this rock that grants it?
Think about all the things that've happened these past three months. Any of them would have made the headlines for an entire week in the old days! The entire Holy City has been turned upside-down.
In the end, though, so many people chose to stay in these ruins... our halos still shine. Life goes on, right?
Just make a new wish! It's not that hard.
I'll show you how!
Apple Pie Logistics is opening up next week, the first civilians logistics company to be officially recognized by the Curia!
My wish is that it will grow and grow and have lots of business, and that I'll have lots of time to stay with Mamma and Tata and my sister...
Can I make a wish for sugar-free ice cream?
Uh...
Sure!
My wish is that Lateran genetics will change, and everybody will support my culinary research, so that their teeth and halos will never get cavities!
With resilient perseverance, the dawn of a new day shines into the deepest reaches of Laterano, illuminating the green graffiti, and the stone that has long grown dim.
The Sanktas look up. Light dances in the drifting dust.
...The Seventh Tribunal proposed a blockade and communications blackout over the area, but the proposal is not feasible.
Hard to block it off when the plates have been so badly damaged.
The Basilica practically hangs over the side of a cliff. How do you stop curious passersby from looking down?
Besides, the First Saint has already revealed the truth to Laterano... all that our people are doing now is to learn to face it.
Notarial Hall records indicate that the last patient in intensive care at Pagus Faber Central Hospital's Halo Surgery division has been discharged to an observation ward.
Which means that all the non-Sanktas who were given halos in this calamity are no longer troubled by an annoying light over their heads. It only took three months.
The Sankta halos that were broken are also gradually recovering...
Empathy continues to exist. It feels different now, but it is still a connection that belongs to the Sankta, and the Sankta alone.
God is dead, is He not?
He ordered Andoain and I to destroy Him, and He revealed His weakness to us.
We destroyed Him with our own hands. I have confirmed that His functions have ceased completely.
But the program that governs the halos persists...
Is the Law still in effect?
Let's look at this from another angle, Federico.
You identified the inherent paradox in the Law. A paradox is a combination of two things that cannot coexist.
If His last command to you was to eliminate the paradox...
Then perhaps, we have not destroyed the Law itself, but only this erroneous conflict... the paradox itself.
The logic stands, but difficult to process. The machine itself, after all, has ceased to operate.
You're certainly correct. It was a thought that came to me suddenly as I was chewing on cactus yesterday.
But I still believe that He left something behind for us.
Perhaps the Law of Laterano was never truly complete. He left behind for us a tabula rasa, waiting for us to communicate with Him.
That is why I have decided to repurpose the underground structure of the Basilica as a public space, which all citizens are welcome to view as they please.
Where Laterano goes from here, and what new meaning the Law will create, is a matter in which every Sankta will have their say.
It is as you said, Federico. Time will tell.
Strange. Where did it go?
The extra grenade launcher maintenance kit is in the bottom drawer. I put the collector's edition disc in the sofa compartment.
Take a break from packing and pour me a cup of cactus juice—I just got back from the hospital, and my throat is parched!
Take a guess. What do you think happened?
A Sankta priest just officiated five straight funerals. He suddenly came down with a case of aphasia, while his wife suffered heart pain at the same time...
But he was located in an Ecclesia Requietum on the outskirts, while his wife was in a gunsmithy downtown!
Many similar cases have been reported. Several hospitals and churches submitted a joint report to the Curia last week, detailing the abnormal diagnoses of over a thousand Sanktas...
The lights above our heads have come on again, but the strength and range of empathy seem to differ from person to person.
Sanktas unfamiliar with one another only faintly sense each other's emotions, even in close proximity. Friends and family, however...
What's your point?
Things seem to have gone back to normal in Laterano, but we both know that fundamental changes have started to take place, after a calamity of such scale.
You shouldn't leave now, Fia.
I told you about Rhodes Island.
Dr. Doctor is here in Laterano, and helped us solve a bunch of problems behind the scenes.

The Doctor says there's been a major incident at Rhodes Island HQ, and the calamity that struck the Holy City is no coincidence... there is something that links them together, and the Doctor set out on a new journey because of it.
Mostima and I are both Rhodes Island operators. It's our job... and it's for the good of Laterano too.
I need you to approve my field mission application. You've read the reasons.
You know what I did, don't you, Fia?
The Pope decided not to prosecute you. It's not my place to say anything.
Besides, you did help people leave the procession at the very end... you did your duty as a gun-knight.
People always let an old man off the hook easy.
It wasn't just you... the abomination had the support of pretty much every Sankta, inside and outside the dream.
On top of the influence of her empathy, you all had reasons to 'make Laterano a better place'—even if it still sounds dumb, and I still want to call you a sentimental old fool—
But I do understand the stuff about parting.
Uh...
Like I said, there are certain things that I need to experience for myself, even if it does make you worry.
I'll be back soon.
All I wanted to say, Fia, is that we didn't get a chance to watch Eight-Armed Chainsaw Guy together yet...
Paganini was right. We should try to leave as few regrets as we can, before anything happens.
You're almost retired, aren't you, old man?
Yeah.
Want to go on a vacation together?
Finally...
Vannini stands before the door of the gunsmithy. After hesitating for a long while, she takes down the sign that says 'Temporarily Closed', and pushes the door open.
M-Magister...
Oh, enough with the yelling.
It's just a halo check. What took you so long? Couldn't be worse than my injuries... *cough*
Dr. Chandler says my, er, language skills are, getting better.
He asked me, if I would, consent to be, his research subject... for speech... pathology? And halos—I-I'm not sure I got it...
I didn't and I don't care to. You Sanktas are so complicated.
And you can have your guardian gun back.
I-Is there a, problem? You weren't around, so I had to, figure it out, best as I could...
Of course there's a problem.
Wha?
The grip's too delicate! There's no room for power upgrades! Nothing really wrong with the action design or the assembly work.
And someone left this bunch of papers at the door. Have a look.
The Curia's mark... could it be...?
We're not in the dream. Nobody's closing the shop down.
It's an order from the Curia. They want us to design heavy firearms for their new gun-knights.
Pa...
Stop. Of course I know why they came to this tiny workshop.
You shot him, didn't you, Vani?
Yeah.
Good girl. Settled an old score for me. Now the past is the past, and I don't want to talk about it again.
You decide whether to take this order.
M-Me?
Who else, me? I never expected to still be breathing by now. My chest still hurts. *cough* *cough* I can't see the parts clearly, and I can't hold a barrel firm anymore.
You made your first gun, and ripped the seal that your Lord placed on your mouth. The hard part is over. The path is yours to walk.
Just don't ruin my reputation...
You can do that, can't you?
Yes, El has already left... I told you, it's the Curia's decision.
This calamity is merely a starting point, for both Laterano and the other nations. We need to recover, and strengthen our ties with the other nations.
...El's the best Sankta for the job, considering her experience in Lungmen, and her relationship with Rhodes Island.
She left a message—'Mamma, Tata, let's send Lemuel off on her next adventure with a bang!'
Let's pray it's not her bowels that go off with a bang... she drank all the cystymilk I left for her in the fridge before she left. Maybe she was worried I would be angry.
Anyway, I've got to go to work.
This terminal. You're looking at Amos's effects again...
They said his last moments were with you?
Yes. I heard his last words, and restored his mailbox history accordingly.
Such a fool, wasn't he?
Let's cut straight to the chase. What judgment are you gonna pass on my new crimes this time, Cardinal Lemuen?
I take it you admit responsibility for the robbery of that caravan in the western outskirts of the Holy City?
Don't tell me you couldn't tell they were professional soldiers.
From Gododdin.
We're not passing any judgment. The outcome was undoubtedly favorable for the Seventh Tribunal.
They just caught me in a bad mood.
The Curia will accommodate Sister Nueda and the Bolívarians who came to Laterano with you.
Regardless of your motivation, your attack on Patrizion worked to our advantage. The Curia sees no need to pursue the matter of your unauthorized return to the Holy City any further.
Oh, what? Laterano still wants to put up a front of purity and clemency, even after waking from the dream. You'll make a show of forgiving a sinner who fell into hell?
Are you really that stupid?
That monster paraded through the streets of Laterano as she gave her speech. It's no secret that angels and devils came from the same place anymore.
This city's revolting facade has been stripped away.
Without the foundation of its holiness, the 'Holy City' is nothing more than a tiny city-state. Do you think its posturing will continue to win the sympathy of Leithanien or Ursus?
You misunderstand. Laterano will make no show of clemency.
Your exile still stands. You are not permitted to return to the Holy City. However... the Curia is interested in offering you an employment contract.
Employment?
To do what you just did.
Hmph. As if the Laterans don't do their own dirty work.
Of course not. Gun-knight, cardinal, Pontifica Cohors, Legatus... nobody is immune to sin. But...
Times have changed. Your judgment was not wrong. Next time, it may not be a scouting force that shows up on the outskirts of the Holy City.
The most radical transgression in one era may be necessary for survival in another. You were decisive, if nothing else.
Why do you think I would accept?
Can I ask you a question?
You left Laterano after a big fight with Amos, something that he always regretted... what was the reason for the fight?
Honey, wh-why are you bringing this up again?
Yes, Patrizion didn't show up to the wedding, or even give us his blessing... but he's still your mentor.
Is he?
Besides, it won't change anything—
No, it won't. He won't die just because you cursed his soul, and he could even take your job from you...
But if you don't even have the guts to do this... then you're a coward, Amos.
I'm leaving.
Just another pointless argument.
You've seen the documents in the terminal. I was thinking, maybe the words that Amos left unsaid were...
No matter what kind of person you are, whether your union was blessed or not, none of that would alter his faith in you—
The faith that you were each other's salvation.
No, I wasn't sure if you would accept the Curia's proposal.
We all have issues that even empathy can't fully communicate, pasts that we can't let go, and uncertain futures... there is much that I have to think about myself.
If hatred is what helps you cope, then maybe that's the right path for you.
Perhaps this is what Amos wanted to see.
Still hanging in there, Extra?
Argh—
I know... I know your heartbeat and... your warmth...
I know your fear and your joy...
I-If I am blessed by the Law, He shall have mercy on your life...
Praying? Now's hardly the time... wait, you're Sarkaz. Where did you learn to do that?
What the—
Did it go well?
Yeah.
Then what's with that look on your face?
A h-halo...
What?
The Sarkaz's child, that Sarkaz newborn, has a halo...
She said her husband was Sarkaz too, right? The baby's not mixed, like Cecelia...
And we're not in the bloody dream, either. This nomadic abbey is a long way from Laterano...
Where's Andoain?
He went out as soon as the abbey stopped. Said he was going to forage, and pick a flower to celebrate the birth of a new miracle.
I have a feeling... he had a premonition about what was going to happen.
Do you remember?
We found Andoain by the pit after the calamity came to a close. He was almost completely blind, and he refused to tell us what happened beneath the Basilica.
A joint court of the First, Fifth, Sixth, and Seventh Tribunals put him on trial. Nobody knows how exactly the trial was conducted, only that he was exiled as a result.
He is never to return to Laterano, but he was allowed to live on this nomadic abbey...
Don't think too much about it.
It was Andoain's decision to disband the Pathfinders. As he said, we don't need to seek anything from Laterano anymore...
Some brothers and sisters have chosen to stay behind in Laterano, while others have returned to their homes. A dozen have chosen to follow him here.
Whatever his true hopes for this abbey are, there is at least one thing that is certain—we're trying to help more people...
Say, shouldn't he be back by now?
The Sarkaz learned to pray in that shrine. She established a connection with the Law on her own.
As for us, we only truly spoke with Him for the first time, after the disintegration of our own faith.
If faith is a response to life, then a new Law has been born, here and now.
The crisis has not been averted. Indeed, our rejection of the other possibility has made it even more unknown and pressing—
Fortunately, a new possibility also lies ahead.
Where will you take it, Andoain?

lines.st: The wind carries the cries of the newborn. The Sankta stops on the hill, a wildflower in his hand.
His eyes no longer see, but he continues to look up, standing in the same spot unmoving, as though he can see that tiny speck of light.
After a while, Andoain walks toward the nomadic abbey on the other side of the hill. He leaves Laterano, and never turns back.
No one counts his sins. No one laments the scores of the past. No one sees him off. No one knows of his departure.
There is nothing in his heart that needs resolution. He caresses an aged book, and keeps walking, until he is no more than a tiny point in the vast wilderness.
The martyr shall find final rest at the end of a long journey.
Until then, he shall not rest.

lines.x: You have determined that the momentous migration has come to a conclusion in reality.

That conclusion has been mapped onto the space you are in. The rift that looks as if it has been plowed open by a Catastrophe bisects the plaza in front of the Basilica. By the rift, there is an even more eye-grabbing pit.
Ugly scars in what should have been a city trapped in stasis.
The ring-shaped machine sits silently within.

opts: Did the PCS system... crash?

lines.x: You look at Priestess. She remains calm, as if she does not care about what has happened in the slightest.
You feel uneasy.
Analysis complete! Ten standard seconds ago, at least two massive explosions took place in the Basilica plaza.
I'm certain the instigators were Exusiai, Executor, and a silver-haired Sankta. The constant flickering of the freeze-frames by the explosion point are proof enough.

opts: The programmatic bombs we helped Exusiai make worked.
The machine's structure shouldn't have been that weak.

lines.x: We can't pat ourselves on the back just yet, Doctor!
(Priestess still hasn't made her next move.)
(We need a way to stop her from changing the situation, no matter what.)
You're right. Its structure would definitely have been able to withstand any attacks the angels can execute. It was designed to operate in even harsher environments, after all.
(Priestess should be able to reactivate it. We need to stop her from doing that.)
*sigh* Sorry, Doctor.
I should've come alone to carry out this mission. I never should've let you take on this risk—

opts: You're used to standing in front of me by now, Mon3tr.
(Smile)
Thanks.

lines.x: Huh?

opts: Things might not be as bad as you think.
I can verify Priestess's condition now.

lines.x: Her... condition?
You walk past Mon3tr toward Priestess. Mon3tr immediately tenses up, and she pulls on your arm.
Doctor!

opts: Don't worry.
Trust me.

lines.x: After a brief moment of hesitation, she lets go, but decides to stick close to you as you walk ahead.
You stand shoulder-to-shoulder with Priestess.

opts: This city's scars are rather inharmonious.
You've allowed these redundant parameters to mix with your data.

lines.x: These unremarkable strings ultimately caused the entire program to crash, Doctor. There is no guaranteeing a language will operate in eternal perfection.
I know what you want to say. Even Originium, the language we created together, has limitations.
It needs more time to adjust and iterate.
Sometimes I cannot help but wonder if what is truly stopping Originium from reaching perfection is not a design flaw... but us.
Human emotion and reason are both able to fetter the development of a language.
We debated this several times during the creation process, but there was never a clear winner.
Luckily, we both accepted that this uncertainty exists within Originium.

opts: ...I don't understand your meaning.

lines.x: Has memory loss affected your ability for divergent thinking?
I'm glad to see you so perplexed, though. It is a marked difference from how I used to communicate with Doctor.
'Perfection' implies a strict upper bound. 'Flaw', on the other hand, means we still have the chance for a breakthrough. This was Doctor's stance on the matter.
When the crisis we must resolve is still inexplicable, chasing perfection will only lead us to our graves.

opts: No, this has nothing to do with the current circumstances.
You... can't change this situation.
This is different from our first encounter on the deck.

lines.x: When did you figure it out?

opts: Just now.
...You could have stopped the programmatic bombs from detonating.
...You could have used Originium to close the rift.
...You could have left the saints trapped in the maze.
It would have been easy for you to do that.
It's like when you awakened and ruined Rhodes Island.
You didn't eliminate Mon3tr and me from the get-go.
You've got problems of your own.

lines.x: That much is obvious, Doctor.
But only now have you mustered up the courage to stand by me.
I had hoped the one standing by my side could have peacefully shared in the solitude that took root deep in our thoughts, during our long hibernations.
It's a shame you can't do that.
We are not as we were in the past. Far too much has happened since, has it not?

opts: You really haven't hid anything from me.
You're more like a... riddle.

lines.x: Still curious about me?
This 'Mon3tr' no longer has any limitations on her. She can clear up some of your confusion.
You fear Doctor will learn the truth, AMa-10?
To be honest, I would much rather Doctor come to me in person, like earlier.
My days in the sarcophagus were excruciating. It was as if my thoughts were floating through a fetid morass, wherein I lost all sense of the flow of time.
My only consolation was the never-ending loop of debates seared into our minds, though not to reassure me of our shared vision.
I have never once doubted my path.
Through 'debate', I got my only chance to communicate with a certain someone again—

opts: I—

lines.x: I'm not demanding you understand everything on my mind right away. Before the final day comes, you can continue to walk the path you believe in.
Our disagreements are destined to continue, even if you no longer remember that one's reasons for disagreeing with me.
I look forward to seeing how your beloved Terra can prove to me that your struggle still has some value... the way the people of Laterano just did.
But there is something else I am equally certain of. Oracle will return.
So, Doctor, I will wait for you.
As she looks out at the city devoid of life, she sighs.
At times, I think they are far too like us. If you had enough time, perhaps...
She begins to fade away.
Ah, yes, I can indeed restore AMa-10.
If you are determined to see it done, make a wish to Originium, Doctor.
I will hear your voice.
Priestess vanishes without a trace—your target is gone.
There is only silence, absolute silence. You and Mon3tr are the only ones left trapped inside this colorless ghost town.

opts: She's gone...

lines.x: Umm... I guess that's it, then?

opts: Perhaps.
The Sanktas resolved their crisis by themselves.
I've also confirmed Priestess's current condition.
Her loss of control over Originium is more serious than we thought.

lines.x: That's really important information, Doctor!
While we can't be sure how long she'll need to get back to normal, at the very least, we have a chance to stop her.
Oh, right, she mentioned... Kal'tsit... earlier...

opts: I don't trust her, Mon3tr.
We shouldn't wish upon Originium, even if it's for Kal'tsit.

lines.x: I know. I don't approve of using her methods to bring back Kal'tsit either.

She inflicted grievous wounds on Rhodes Island and Laterano. Every time she appears, she drops something mind-blowing on us, and then points toward some even more unknowable crisis... I don't understand her at all.

opts: We've been researching a long time now.
We have our own methods.

lines.x: We're going to be setting out on a really long road, aren't we?
You finally notice what's odd about Mon3tr. Your enemy has retreated, the crisis has passed, but the young woman, normally so lively and energetic, does not smile like she usually does.
She even looks somewhat solemn.

Mon3tr hasn't been reconstructed for very long. You still haven't gotten completely used to her new appearance, nor has she had the time to adapt to the changes in her body, or the loss that cut her to the bone.

But she has been thrown into mission after mission far too quickly. Rebuilding Rhodes Island, saving Laterano... Perhaps it has taken up until now for her to finally have the chance to speak her mind.

opts: No need to be so pessimistic, Mon3tr—
Don't give up, Mon3tr—

lines.x: I'll bring back Kal'tsit!
I'll also protect Amiya, and you too!
Mon3tr looks up at you all of a sudden, interrupting your train of thought.
I haven't forgotten what Kal'tsit told me!
If the outcome I fear most does come to pass, if the one I least want to see stands in front of us once more—
Protect the Doctor. Protect Amiya.
I will engrave this directive into your basic behavioral strategy matrix.
Eternal life is destined to be eternally lonely. I have tasted it.
...You can walk alone—
Mon3tr.

opts: Listen to me, Mon3tr.

lines.x: You look at her, and slowly, you begin to speak.

opts: You haven't been in your reconstructed body for very long.
Don't put too much pressure on yourself.
Kal'tsit wouldn't want her hopes to turn into chains.

lines.x: Err... I think you're misunderstanding something, Doctor.
I'll always remember how, when I awoke, you just turned around and walked away after giving me a 'welcome back'...

opts: Oh...
Sorry, Mon3tr.

lines.x: It's fine, it's fine. I needed time to get used to not being in a symbiotic relationship after thousands of years, and you needed time to accept the loss of someone you've fought beside for such a long time. And now...
We're both standing here, right in front of one another.
But we've already known each other for quite a while, haven't we, Doctor?
Under the cannon fire of Londinium, deep inside the secret caverns of the Sarkaz, below the tail of that giant ring flying into the sky in Trimounts, in Milliarium's Sophontheatrum...
Kal'tsit was there for all of those, which means I was too.
It's not like I only started to follow her orders to understand you and Rhodes Island when she left us.
Becoming a Rhodes Island Operator, putting up a fight against our creator, protecting Amiya, protecting you. These were my decisions as well...
And what I've been doing all along.
Silence.
You look at Mon3tr. Silence links the two of you.

opts: Then count me in, Mon3tr.
We'll bring Kal'tsit back.
We'll walk to the end of this uncharted road.

lines.x: Eternal life is destined to be eternally lonely, but thankfully, we do not walk alone on the journeys we take. Even if all life is sapped of color, even if all things fade away into nothingness...
We can, must, walk onwards together.
This is what she hoped for.
Oh...

opts: What's wrong?
Got another question to ponder?

lines.x: What's with the look?
The counseling session is over, commander! No problems here, I had just come up with a way to get out of this blasted place, and I'll need your help!
According to Priestess, this should be a space created by that machine after it connected to Originium, some kind of model based on the future.

opts: So there's a relation to Originium's properties here.
We can try cracking those abnormal nodes.

lines.x: Abnormal nodes? Ah, you mean the parts with damage mapped onto them.

opts: The restriction on the connection between you and the machine should be gone, right?

lines.x:
Yep, I can sense its signal, though its getting weaker by the second.
I'll try reestablishing the connection...
But what then?

opts: Keep requesting refactoring of the abnormal nodes.
Use up the last of its computing power.

lines.x: Shouldn't be too difficult for me when Priestess isn't meddling with things.

lines.st: Routing through city communication post backdoor access port...
Special privileges: AMa-10.
Temporary access credentials: ELCARO-001... ORACLE. Decoding complete. Authentication passed.
Connected to PCS system.
Syncing computational radiation link...
Warning: Core frequency too low.
Diagnostic complete. Re—Re—Refactoring abnormal nodes—

lines.x: Color seeps out from the ruins of this colorless ghost town.

It starts from the street to the right of the Tower of Revelations, and then through the Notarial Hall and firearms workshops in Pagus Michaelion, to the Sunset Chapel in Pagus Saint Marcel, and then all the way to the chapel square...
Noise returns to the Holy City.
It is the sound of Laterans offering a prayer to life.
The color's back?!

opts: Let's go, Mon3tr.

lines.x: Not going to go say hi to Exusiai?

opts: No need to hold her up before her reunion with family and friends.
We need to update the ship on what happened here in Laterano.
Priestess's disappearance will only be temporary.
Priestess will make her next move soon.
Our mission isn't over yet.

lines.st: Transfiguration suspended.
Set of directives assigned by personnel with relevant privileges have been completed.
Beginning system shutdown timer.

lines.x: (Unknown language) I've checked your low-level code. Aside from the adjustments I made during maintenance, you've performed a lot of self-upgrades and iterations within a short period of time.
(Unknown language) Was this still not enough for your self-repair functions to fix your logic vulnerability?

lines.st: Negative.
Vulnerability has been patched. Paradox has been corrected.

lines.x: (Unknown language) I had great interest in the results of your self-iteration.
(Unknown language) It seems you mistakenly identified yourself as that vulnerability, that paradox.
(Unknown language) Was it a side effect of the overclocking? Or did your lack of maintenance, and long-term data transfer with the Sanktas, pollute your database?
(Unknown language) If I had more data, I might be able to find the answer.
(Unknown language) It's a shame the resources I can allocate will not be enough to give you a complete restart.
(Unknown language) Otherwise, a command system's self-negation due to its target's wishes would reignite any researcher's interest in the PCS system.
(Unknown language) ... If only we were still in the era when geniuses used reason and opinion to clash over their views.
The ring-shaped machine does not respond. Priestess feels a hint of regret.
(Unknown language) It seems I will have to choose other methods to advance the project—
Many mechanical cubes come together to form an annular computational matrix, the core of the machine. They sit there in stony silence.
But all of a sudden, one of the cubes begins to vibrate, a metallic gleam roaming its surface once more.
Soon, a faint, flickering halo begins to form.
From the ruins of the PCS system, a new 'angel' is born.
(Unknown language) ...It restarted?
(Unknown language) It can't be. It didn't respond to me at all, nor does this follow the PCS system's operational logic.
The 'angel' ignores Priestess, quickly ascending into the gaping hole above it, disappearing without a trace soon after.
(Unknown language) A 'prototype' 'angel'?
(Unknown language) But this system was only built to mimic the properties of 'angels'. There was never any 'angel prototype' sample provided when it was constructed.
(Unknown language) A newborn 'angel'?! How could this be...?
What is that in your hand, Lemuel?
Hehe. A spray can.
I've given the report on the latest data from the barrier hole to Director Saria, but there was something I noticed that got me real curious...
Look. Doesn't that look like some kind of strange flying object to you?
Components Control Section is analyzing it right now. We should be hearing from them soon.
A flying object that can maintain altitude close to the barrier hole would be a major discovery—
The 'angel' hovers in front of the hole in the barrier. It attempts to reestablish a connection with the individuals scattered amongst the stars, but to no avail.
All it manages to capture are the ripples left behind by energy entities as they die in the depths of space.
And so, it continues to orbit across the land, like a shooting star destined to never fall...
(Unknown language) The scent of life.
(Unknown language) This land is still young. Is it new life flourishing in the wake of ruination, or has death not come for this place yet?
(Unknown language) Wasn't I... swallowed up by that light...?
(Unknown language) Originium has recorded your information.
(Unknown language) ... 'Originium'?
(Unknown language) Before the light comes, the conclusion is still uncertain. Originium will become our hope.
(Unknown language) Where is this place?
(Unknown language) 'Terra'.
(Unknown language) We are not alone, my friend.
Did you see the latest news, Miss Koshelna?
Lots of mines have shut down, and they've put troops on high alert all across the city.
My parents were called in for an inspection... I can't find them.
Stay in the classroom for now.
I will help ask around for you. I'm also interested in knowing what is going on.
She calms the child down, and then takes the newspaper from their hand.
Over a few short days, countless rumors have spread across the city, but the most alarming one is—
'Our Originium vein has disappeared. If we can't get any energy out of Mother Earth, how are we supposed to make it through the winter?'
Originium... I shall unravel this mystery.
Only the blood burning within our breasts can see Ursus through the winter chill.
Once we've tidied things up here, it'll do as everyone's new abode, Hierda.
Thank you all for your help!
Good thing you got supplies from our friends at the monastery, otherwise finding a place to stay wouldn't have gone so smoothly.
We were worried you had... abandoned us after heading back there...
Oh, I'm sorry. The road back was rather rough-going, and I heard lots of news I could barely even believe along the way.
It's mighty hard to imagine, yeah. The Originium veins, Laterano...
The Sanktas at the monastery sure had a lot to say about your return! Wouldn't stop telling us all about it!
Huh?
When that calamity was going on, they wanted to go back to Laterano, but a Catastrophe stopped them.
Their halos were shattered, and all they could do was stay here and pray, for themselves, and for their brothers and sisters who had already left...
Then you showed up right in front of them, a girl who disappeared without a trace three years ago!
You brought them hope, Hierda!
Oh my, did I?
I really don't want to let everyone down... Maybe now that we have a fixed shelter, we can help even more people.
But we still need to take some necessary security precautions.
With us pulling out all the stops for our work, we're bound to attract people up to no good. You'll be in danger.
Honestly speaking, we might be able to find a better way to do this—
Hierda shakes her head.
I need to see her.
She must be somewhere here on this land...
Hierda looks out into the distance, despite knowing the vast wilderness will show her nothing in return.
She sees something streaking across the sky.
A shooting star?
Anyway, Originium should not be a source of despair.
I would like to pray for all Infected who suffer—
Err, will praying... actually do anything?
Maybe not.
But when we pray, we will think of each other.
Our bonds bind us together. We are not alone.
The Iberian coast, a cliff by the sea
A Seaborn clings tight to the sharp rocks.
The sound of distant naval barrages is carried over by the sea breeze, but the Seaborn does not care, focusing its efforts on climbing.
(Excitedly flaps membranous wings)
(Unfolds creases enthusiastically)
The Seaborn finally arrives at the peak of the cliff.
What greets it is a boundless expanse of dry land, and a flash of light up amidst the heavens.
A shooting star?
The Seaborn shifts its body in confusion in response to the momentary distraction, and its appendages brush against something.
A shining black rock rolls off the cliff, dropping straight down into the ocean.
The Seaborn tries to see what it was, but it has already been engulfed by the billowing waves.
